recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Green Peas (Matar) Steamed, 1 Cup Rice, 200 Grams Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) Rinsed And Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera) Powder (Haldi), 2 Cloves (Laung), 2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta) Torn, 2 Tablespoons Ghee Or Sesame Ol Saseme Oil Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the methi matar pulao recipe, we will be cooking the paulao in a single pot. Keep the steamed green peas aside, we will add it towards the end when the entrere paulao is cooked.begin by heating oil in a heavy bottomed pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds, grated ginger and sauté for a more seconds. nature in the fenugreek leaves, turmeric powder and saute until until the leaves have softened. Add in the whole spices, salt, green chilies and the rice, add in 1 cup of coconut milk and 1 cup of water. Check the salt and adjust to suit your taste.bring the methi rice to a quick boil. Once the rice come to a boil, turn the heat to low, cover the pan and allow the rice to cook along with the spices and methi leaves until all the waters is completely absorbed.Once you notice all the win The heat and allow the methi matar paulao to rest for about 5 minutes. After 5 minutes open the pan, add the steamed peas and a tablespoon of ghee. Stir in to combin well into the rice and the methi matar paulao is now ready to be served warm.the methi matar paulao makes a great lunch box meal filled with nutrition of methi and RASTO RICE RICE RCE RCE TATO RICE RES .

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Sooji (Semolina/ Rava), 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 1 Green Chilli Finly Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Must Bes (Rai/ Kadugu) Water, 1 Teaspoon Lemon Juice To Grind, 6 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Chopped Green Chilli Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the kothamalli rava upma recipe, first grind the corridor leaves for the upma.into the mixer Add corrider leaves, green chillies, ginger and grind into a somoth passter. Add a little water to grind to a smooth paste. Set Aside. This is the kothamalli paste. In a pan heat 3 cups of water in a saucepan along with salt and sugar. Bring It to a Boil and Stir Until the Sugar Dissolves. Turn the heat to low and keep the water simmering.heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add the mustard seeds, urad dal and allow it to roast until the urad dal is browned. Add in the curry leaves, onions, green chillies and cook till the onions become soft and translucent.Once the onions have softtened, add the sooji and roast for 3 to 4 minutes UNTIL You Get A Lightly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly Roastly ROSTLY ROMA. Stir in kothamalli paste. Gradually add the hot water and keep stirring the kothamalli upma until all the water is absorbed. Stir in the lemon juice, cover and cook for a few minutes and turn off the heat.Once don, check the taste and adjust the salt accordingly.serv Wholesome Breakfast.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Cauliflower (Gobi) Florets, 1/2 Cup Carrots (Gajjar) Diced, 2 To, 3 Tablespoons Green Peas (Matar) Fresh Or Frozen, 1 Onion, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 2 Tablespoons Garlyspoons Green Chills S., 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 2 Teaspoons Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Sprigs C. recipe_description: To begin making the Kerala Style Cauliflower Kurma Recipe, First HEAT SFFICINENT WATER In A Saucepan and Add Chopped Vegetables and Boil Them.Drain Water and Collect the Vegetables in a Mixing Bowl. (Do not over cook the veggies) heat some oil in a heavy bottomed pan and add cinnamon stick and curry leaves.Now add finly chopped onion and saute till they Turn Translucent.add Ginger Green Chillac Paste and Fryn Pured Tomato Along with Red Chilli Powder, Turmeric Powder, Cumin Powder and Coriander Powder and Fry Till the Oil Starts Leaving the sides.pour in the thin coonut millk, coconut mill, coconut mill, coconut Milk, Coconut Milk, Coconut Milk. Cook for 5 to 6 Minutes on low flame.once the curry is don, add thick coconut milk, some chopped cashews and turn off the gas.Garnish with fresh chopped corridor and some grated coconut. Vadam for a comfort meal. Cauliflower kurma also goes well with whole wheat lachha parathas.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal) Washed And Soaked For, 15 Minutes, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Finly Chopped, 6 Clocles Garlic Finly Chopped, 2 Greeto Chillies Slito, 1 Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Salt To Taste, 1 Taste, 1 Taste, 1 Tasteespoon Juice, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 4 Cloves Garlic Thinly Sliced ��1/4 Teaspoon Read Chilli Powder
recipe_description: To begin making the punjabi toor dal tadka recipe, press the menu option of the electricity pressure cooker and keep pressing the plus button till you Reach p-15 which is the sauthe Allow the pan to preheat for about 4 minutes. Once the pan is heated, add the ghee, onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies and saute until the onions are soft and slightly golden in color. Once done add the tomatoes, turmeric power, Red Chilli Powder, Coriander Powder, Garam Masala Powder and Salt to Taste. Saute until the tomatoes are soft and mushy.after the tomatoes are soft add the toor dal along with two coups of water. Stir Well to Combine and Cover the Pressure Cooker. Change the nozzle to the pressure mode. Now change the menu option to dal mode. To do this press the keep warm option and then the menu. Press Plus button Till You Reach P-4 The Dal Mode.ONCE Allow the pressure to release naturally. Wait until the red nozzle goes down and then open the pressure cooker. Stir in Coriander leaves and squeeze juice from one lemon. Keep Aside.
recipe_name: Punjabi Toor Dal Tadka Using Preethi Electric Pressure Cooker

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Cooked Rice, 10 Pearl Onions (Sambar Onions) Finly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Finly Chopped, 4 Cloves Garlic Finly Chopped, 3 Tomatoes Finly Chopped, 2 Carrots Grated (Optional) Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Sprig Curry Leaves Finly Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Sambar Power, 1 Teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi), 1/2 Teaspoon, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Taste, 1 Tablespoon Ghee
recipe_description: To begin making the Thakkali Sadam Recipe - Spicy Tomato Rice, Get All the ingredients Ready. Make sure you have cooked rice ready.heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan; Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds; Allow it to crackle. Then add onions, ginger and garlic and sauté until the onens are tender and golden.add chopped tomatoes and grated carrots; Sauté until the tomatoes and carrots are tender and the moisture has evaporated.Continue to cook until the mixture looks like a semi-thick paste. Stir in the chopped curry leaves at this point.next add in the sambar powder, turmeric power, cardamom power, salt and mix well.add mint leaves and sauté for a feew more minters. Gradually stir in the cooed rice to the tomato mixture and fold the thakkali sadam gently so that all ingredients are combined.finally add a tablespoon of ghee or sesame oil and stir it into it into the Thakkali Sadam and SADATOVE SADATIVE SADATIVE SADATOVE SADATOVE Rice along with Boondi Raita and Kachumber Salad for a Weekday Lunch or Dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Whole Wheat Flour, 1 Cup Sugar, 1 Cup Ghee +, 1 Tablespoon, 3 Cups Water, 2 Teaspoons Cardamom Powder (Elaichi) Freshly Ground, 1/4 Cup Cashew Nuts Halveded
recipe_description: To begin making the gehun ka halwa | Atte ka halwa | Kada Prasad Recipe, Heat the Water and Sugar in a SauCepan, Stirring Continiously until until the Sugar Dissolves. Turn the heat to low and continue to heat the water until you get flour mixture ready by following the steps that follow.heat a heavy bottomed pan on medium heat; Melt the one cup of ghee and stir in the wheat flour and roast the mixture on medium heat. Continue to roast until the mixture begins to release a Roasted Aroma and Changes color to a roasted brown; The process will take about 5 to 8 minutes, ensure you roast the wheat flour in medium heat to avoid the flour from burning.Keep stirring Continuously while it is roasting. When the mixture will begin to resent like coarse crumbles, turn the heat to low and stir the cardamom power to the wheat flour mixture.slowly start string in the prepared hot sudor waters. The mixture will start splitting and curdling. Continue stirring until the atte ka halwa / kada prasad thickens, yet feels coarse and crumble and not like a blob of dough.stir continuously for 10-15 Minutes on Meedum Heat AFTE AFTE AFTE ATTE AFTEE AFTEE AFTEE AFTEE ATEE MINUTES You will notes the atte ka halwa come away from the sides of the pan.turn the heat off and let the gehun ka halwa/ atte ka halwa rest covered for about 5 minutes; Heat the 1 Tablespoon of Ghee on Medium Heat; Add the cashew nuts and roast them until the cashew nuts turn golden brown. Turn off heat and stir this into the gehun ka halwa/ atte ka halwa. Serve the gehun ka halwa/ atte ka halwa/ kada prasad during festivals, Special Occasions, after a delicious weekend lunch or at a party as a aS as as as asesrt with friends and family.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Foxtail Millet Little, 2 Green Chillies Adjustable, 1 Dry Red Chilli, 8 Curry Leaves, 3 Cloves Garlic, 6 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Chopped (Leave Bit For Garnishing) Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split), 1/2 Teaspoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 2 Tablespoons Raw Peanuts (Moongphali) Asafoetida (Hing) A Pinach, 1 Haldi) Tamarind Extracted (1/2 A Lemon Size) Oil As Needed Salt To Taste
recipe_description: In a vessel add 1/2 cup millets with one cup of water and brings this to a boil, turn the heat to simmer and cook the milllets covered with a steam vent cracked open for 8-10 minutes for 8-10 Minutes ONTILI THETIL THE. Let it sit for 2 minutes and transfer this to another bowl and let it cool completesly.in a mixie jar add coriander, green chillies, cumin power, garlic, tamarind recovery and salt, grind to a smooth prostation. Set aside And saute until the raw smell disappears approximately 4 minutes. Add a tiny splash of water if the mixture builds very dry.finally add the cooked millets and gently mix so that the mixture is uniformly applied. Adjust the salt if required.top with Chopped Coriander and Serve.Serve Coriander Tamarind Millet Rice Along with Tadka Raita, Tomato On Cucumber Raita Oor On Other Raita of your choice.

Saute for Few Minutes Till the Onens Todays become translucent and soft and turn off the heat. Stir Well to Combine and Check The Salt and Adjust According.Whisk The muttai paniyaram batter well until the egg and onion mixture is well Combined into the dosa batter.heat the dosa batter.heat the Paniyaram Pan on A ACHISITE, AACOSOTE OHHEASOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHIASOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHESOTE, AAHESOTE, AADION Spoon in the muttai paniyaram batter into of the economy.cover the paniyaram pan and let the following allow the muttai paniyaram to steam cook for about 3 to 4. recipe_ingredients: 3/4 Cup Whole Wheat Flour, 2 Tablespoons Basmati Rice Boiled Soaked For, 2 Hours, 1/4 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated Salt To Taste, 1 Taste, 1 Tablespoon Sugar
recipe_description: To begin making whole wheat flour putu recipe, dry roast atta on low flame until the flour releases a pleasant aroma. Take off the heat before the flour starts browning.drain all the water from steamed rice and grind it in a mixer grinder into a crumby mixture. Then add Roasted Whole Wheat Flour and Pulse for Less Than Half a Minute. Do not over blend it. The end result of this grinding should be a crumbly well-mixed mixture of soaked rice and all wheat flour.put the crumbly flour to a wide mixing bowl. Add Little salt, sugar, and a tablespoon of grated coconut to the flour mix.boil enough water in a pressure cooker (without the weight). In a putu maker, add a thin layer of freshly grated coconut, top it with wheat-rain flour mix. If you are using the long cylindrical putu maker, add 2 to 3 layers of grated coconut and flour mix. Cover the Puttu Maker with Lid.place the Puttu Maker on Top of the cooker (Sits on the hole where you put the weight). Steam for About 5 to 10 Minutes Until Cooked. Once cooked, carefully slide whole wheat flour putu from the putu maker and servE hot.serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) Half Inch Slice, 2 Inch Ginger, 3 Cloves Garlic, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Teaspoon Read Chilli Powder, 3 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeeei Cumin Cumin Powder (Jeei), 3/4 Curd Smooth Salt To Taste Oil For Cooking
recipe_description: We begin making the dahi ma bheeda recipe (dahi bhindi - parsi style okras cooked in yogurt recipe) Non-Stick Pan, Add the Cut Okras and Shaallow Fry Them Till it is crispy. Once done remove them.in the same pan add the ginger, garlic and sauté for few minutes. Then add the spices and fry till the raw smell of the spice goes away.lower the heat and gradually add the beaten come to the pan, mix well. Later add the crispy okras and simmer it for 2 minutes.serv .

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Semiya (Vermicelli) Roasted, 1 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) For Tempening, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split) Pinch Asafoetida (Hing), 1 Sprig Curry Leaves, 2 Teaspoon Oil Raw Peanuts (Moongphali) Garnishing
recipe_description: To begin making the thyir semiya recipe, firstly boil the vermicelli in 2 cups water. Drain it and spread on a congress cold water through it. This ensures that the vermicelli does not become sticky. Keep it aside and let it cool down.heat a kadai/wok and add oil. Add the peanuts and roast till it turns brownish. Remove and keep it aside.in the same kadai, add more oil IF needed and add the Mustard seeds and after it splutters add the other ingredients meant for tempering.add the boiled vermicelli and mix everything. Add salt to taste and mix.finally just before serving add curd and mix well. Serve thyir semiya on its own for a light and healthy lunch or dinner

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Horse Gram Dal (Kllu/ Kulith) Soaked For, 3 To, 4 Hours, 2 Cups Drumstick Leaves (Moringa/ Murungai Keirai) Roughly Chopped, 1 Cups Tamarind Water, 10 Pearl Onegans (Sambarl Ones) Chopled, 2 Tom, 1 Teaspoon Sambar Powder, 1 Teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns Coarsely Pounded Salt To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Jaggery Ingredients For Seasoning, 1 Teaspoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves Roughly Torn
recipe_description: To begin making the murungai keerai kolu kuzhambu recipe, first get all the ingredients prepaped and ready. Make the tamarind water according to the recipe - how to make tamarind water.soak the horse gram for a couple of hours. Soaking Helps it to cook faster.Pressure cook the drumstick leaves, Along with Salt and 2 Tablespoons of Water for Just 1 Whistle. Release the pressure immediatily, to prevent overcooking the greens and also to retain its fresh green color yolour.Once the ingredients are prepaped, we will cook them all in the pressure all in the presses. Into the pressure cooker, add a teaspoon of oil - saute the onions until lightly tender. Once the onens are tender, add the Horse Gram, Tomatoes, Tamarind Water, Salt, Jaggery, Sambar Powder, Pepper and Salt. Stir Well to Combine and Cook for 6 to 8 Whistles. After 8 Whistles, Turn of the Heat and Allow The Pressure to Release Naturally. Once released, stir in the cooked murungai keirai into the kolu kuzhambu.give the murungai kerai kolu kolu kuzhambu a brisk boil and turn of the heat.the kuzhambu will be a lattle Runny, IF you can be a like Low heat until it thickens.check the salt and spices and adjust to suit your taste.The final step is the seasoning. Heat a little oil in a small tadka pan, add the must bes, fenugreek seeds and allow it to crackle. Once it crackles add the curry leaves and stir. Turn off the heat and add the seasoning to the murungai kerai kolu kuzhambu and serve.the murungai kerai kelu kulu kuzhmbu recipe is a perfect dish to go along with some ROT RCABBBAG PORIYAL and Appalam. Serve it for lunch or dinner and you will surely enjoy this spicy curry.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Sooji (Semolina/Rava), 1/2 Cup Ghee, 3/4 Cup Sugar, 1 Cup Milk, 2 Cups Water, 1 Teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi), 6 Safron Strands Strands, 1 Tablespoon Gehee, 10 Cashew Nuts Halsthans Halsthans.
recipe_description: To begin making the Rava Kesari BHATH (Sooji Halwa) Recipe, We will first begin by boiling the water, milk, sugar, saffron and cardamom in a sauce pan and stirring until the SUGAR MELTS. Keep the liquid mixture aside on simmer in another stove.in wide heavy bottomed pan heat ghee on medium heat; Add the semolina (rava/sooji) and roast until the semolina gives out a roasted aroma and also turns light golden color. Take care not to let it over brown.gradually begin to stir in the hot liquid mixture you prepared earlier. Take care to keep the heat in medium as the mixture will splutter Around as you pour the hot liquid to the semolina.keep stirring the mixture and the semolina will continue to thicken. Once it has thought it will begin to leave the sides of the pan. Cover the PAN and Simmer the Rava Kesari BHATH for a Couple of Minutes Stirring Occsionally.you will not notice the semolina is cooked and when you stir the whole rava kesari bhath comei bhatha comes of the sides of the pans. At this stage, turns off the heat, cover the pan with a lid and let the Rava Kesari bhath to rest for a less minutes.in another small pan; Heat the ghee on medium heat; Add the cashew nuts and raisins and roast them until the Cashew Nuts Turn Golden Brown.Turn off Heat and Stir this to the Rava Kesari BHATHSERVE THE REVA KESARI BHATH ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONG ALONL .

recipe_ingredients: 1 Kg Chicken, 2 Tomatoes Crushed In Pestle & Mortar, 5 Clooves Garlic Crushed, 2 Inch Ginger Grated, 7/8 Green Chillies Crushed, 1/2 Cup Bamboo Shoots Sliced, 1/2 Cup Cabbage (Patta Gobi/Muttaked Sing) Onion (Bulb & Greens) Few Chopped To Garnish Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To prepare naga style chicken with bamboo shoot recipe, wash chicken pieces thoroughly and drain all the excess water.heat a walk on Medium Flame, Add the Ginger, Garlic, Green Chillies and Saut for 30 decades. To this add the cabbage, bamboo shoots and sprinkle some salt. Cover and Cook for About 2-3 Minutes. Once the cabbage has wilted down add the chicken and the tomatoes.mix it well, sprinkle some water and cook covered, on a Medium Flame Until Chicken is tender and well cook This will take about 20-25 minutes. Garnish with spring onions and some bamboo shoots Slices, optionally.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Green Peas (Matar) Steamed, 1/2 Cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Cut Into Small Cubes, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera), 1 Pinch Asafoetida (Hing), 1 Pinch Asafoetida, 2 Cups Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Tighly Packed, 2 Green Chillies, 5 Clooves Garlic, 1 Inch Ginger, 1 Tablespoon Lemon Juice
recipe_description: To begin making the hariyali matar recipe, peal the green peas, wash and steam it using a steamers and keep aside.make a fin paste of the ingredients listed in the green masala, coriyander leaves, garlys, garlics, garlic, ginger and leame Using a mixer grinder adding very little water and keep aside.heat oil in a wide pan, add the asafoetida, cumin seeds and let the cumin seeds splutter.add the ground green masala and sautes for 2 Minutes UNTILI THE MANUTEs Togeether.add the steamed green peas, season with salt, add 1 cup water, give it a stir and let it simmer for 2 to 3 minutes the gravy has been thicked, add the paneer cubes and mix the paneer cubes Another 2 minutes and switch off the flame. Remove into a serving bowl.serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Baby Potatoes, 1-1/2 Tablespoons Seeds (Til Seeds) Oil As Needed Sal To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Power, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Power (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Tiloric Poet Powder (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finally Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making Chatpate Aloo Recipe With Sesame Seeds, take all the baby potatoes in a saucepan with water and boil well till done.You can also choose to pressure cook them for just one whistle in sufficient water and run under cold water for immediate release of Pressure.Once the potatoes are cooked al -dent, peel them all and put aside.Now, in another small bowl add all the spices -Salt, Red Chili Powder, Red Chili Powder, Turmeric Powder, Jeera Powder, Amchur Powder, Amchur Powder and MALDEDER POWDER POWDER POWDER POWDER POWDER With a spoon, and keep aside.in a kadai, lightly roast sesame seeds and keep them aside.in the same kadai, heat 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil into it for frying the boils.on Add potatoes gently and fry them well. You need to cook them till they get a nice light brown color. A bowl against and put the whole masala into the bowl and mix everything thoroughly toge Cooking the potatoes help the prepared masala to coat well which enhances the taste .Garnish it with few freshly chopped coriander leaves and Serve it like that.Serve Chatpate Aloo Recipe With Sesame Seeds along with Punjabi Dal Tadka, Butter Naan Recipe and end your meal with A Refreshing Mint Lassi Recipe.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Tablespoons Sundakkai (Turkey Berries), 2 Tablespoons Sesame (Gingelly) Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu) (Sambar Onions) Peled And Quartred, 6 Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant) Quartred And Soaked In Salt Water, 50 Grams Tamarind Soaked In Water, 2 Tablespoons Sambar Powder Homemade, 1 Cumemede, 1 Cumemede, 1 Cumemade
recipe_description: To begin making kathirikai sundakkai vathal kuzhambu, prep all the ingredients and keep ready.extract the tamarind water, until you get 2 cups of tamarind water from the soaked tamarind. Keep this aside.heat oil in a pan over medium heat; Add the sundakkai and roast until it is dark brown in color and crisp. Once roasted, keep the sundakkai to the side.preheat oil in a pan over medium heat, add the Mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds. Allow it to crackle.add the curry leaves, asafoetida, onion and brinjal (drain the water it was soaked in). Saute the onion and brinjal until the onion is roasted and brinjal is cooked and roasted. Cover the pan so the brinjal cooks faster.Once don, add the roasted sundakkai, tamarind water, water, homemade sambar power, jaggery and salt. Simmer the kathirikai sundakkai vathal kuzhambu for 15 to 20 minutes until the raw taste from the tamarind goes away and the vathal kuzhambu has thought. Give the kathirikai sundakkai vathal kuzhambu a stir occasionally so that the gravy does not stick to the bottom. Once done, check the taste and adjust the salt and spices according. Turn off the heat and transfer the Kathirikai Sundakkai Vathal Kuzhambu to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve this delicious Kathirikai Sundakkai Vathal Kuzhambu along with Hot Steamed Rice, Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe and Elai Vadam for a delicious Tamilnadu Style lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 4 Potato (Aloo) Skins Removed And Sliced ��Into Rounds, 2 Onens Cut Really Small, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 2 Tablespoons Oil For The Pathare Prabhu Sambar Masala, 1 Tablespoon Wheat Germ, 1 Tablespoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 1/2 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns, 5 Tablespoons Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 5 Dry Red Chillies, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/, 2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Pinch Asafoetida (Hing)
recipe_description: To begin making the Maharashtrian Aloo Bhujne Recipe (Potato Stir Fry), Peel The potatoes and Cut them Into Roundals and Pressure Cook with Enough Water For One Whistle and Leave it to rested. Proceed to make the masala, dry roast the coriander seeds, wheat, chana dal, cumin seeds, Mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, dry read chillies, hing and Turmeric Powder in the Order. Roast all of them togeether for 5 minutes. Cool it down and add in the mixer grinder to make a smooth power. Set it aside.heat a kadai with oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and allow it splutter. Add the Chopped Onions, Ginger Garlic Paste and Saute for 10 Minutes Until ITCOME Translucent. Add green chillies, turmeric power and the ground masala. Add little water so that it does not dry up. Add your cooked potatoes/aloo and salt. Saute Well for 2 Minutes and Add Chopped Coriander Leaves and Mix Well and Serve. Serve the Maharashtrian Aloo Bhujne Along with Dal Tadka, Steamed Rice and Phulkas for an everyday lunch or dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 4 Potatoes (Aloo) Peled And Cut Into Medium Sized, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu) Salt To Taste To Grind, 5 Dry Red Chillies, 18 Grams Tamarind
recipe_description: To begin making the konkani style batata song, Roast the dry red chillies in a pan till it gets crip and changes its color a bit. Add it into a mixer along with tamarind, a little water and grind it into a smooth paste.heat a pan with oil, add must bes seeds and crackle it for 10 seconds. Add in the ground red chilli paste and fry for at least 10 minutes .in the meaning the meaning of the potatoes with little water and salt to one whistle till it is just cooked. Allow the pressure to release by its water.peel the potatoes and let it cool down. Cut the potatoes in cubes and keep them aside.add the potatoes into the pan in which the gravy is getting sauthed sautéed and mix well. Once the gravy is well coated with potatoes, switch off the stove. This will take about 2-3 minutes.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Amchr Tadka, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Green Chillies Slit, 1 Sprig Curry Leaves
recipe_description: To begin making the sindhi dal pakwan recipe, wash and soak chana dal in water overnight or for at least 4 hours.Once soaked, drain the water and cook the Chana Dal in 2 Cups of Water In the PRESURE CHISURESURESURESURESTES With Salt, Sugar, Turmeric Powder, Garam Masala and Amchur Powder.After A 4 to 5 Whistles, Turn the Heat to Low and Simmer for another 5 minutes and turn off the heat. recipe_ingredients: 1 Tablespoon Mustard Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 1/2 Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/4 Kalonji (Onion Nigella Seeds), 1/4 Methii Seeds ( Inch Ginger  Finely Chopped, 6 Cloves Garlic  Finely Chopped, 2 Green Chillies  Finely Chopped, 1 Onion  Finely Chopped, 4 Tomatoes  Finely Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 2 Teaspoons Jaggery, 1 Tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Leaves, 1 Tablespoon Mint Leaves (Pudina)
recipe_description: To begin making the khatta meetha tamatar sabzi, heat the mustard oil in a kadai on high heat.ONCE it reaches smoking point, reduce the flame, now add the Mustard seeds, fanges, fennel seeds, fanges, oned Seeds begin to sizzle, quickly add the ginger, garlic and green chilli and give it a good stir.When the garlic turns brown in color, add the onions and saute for a couple of Minutes.ONCEs AROMATES , Cover the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes or until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. You can use a potato masher or the back of the ladle to mash the tomatoes on them, they turn soft.open the lid and add all the dry masalas to the tamatar sabzi, amchur power, cumin power, cumin power, Garam Masala Powder and Red Chilli power. Mix well to combine. Finally add the jaggery and sprinkle some water, add in the corridor and mint leaves and give it a good mix.serv Simple weeknight dinner.

recipe_description: To begin making the dhaba style dal palak, add the chopped spinach along with 2 tablespoon of water and pressure cook for one whistle and turn off the heat. Release the pressure immediatily to keep the fresh green color of spinach. Keep the spinach aside.heat oil in the pressure cooker; Add the cumin seeds and allow it to crackle. Add the onion, ginger and garlic and saute until the onions turns Soft.Once the onions have Softened, Add the Tomatoes, Turmeric Powder, Turmic Powder, Green Chillies, Garam Masala Powder and Garam Masala Powder and Saute Until The Tomats J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J J Washed toor dal, salt to taste, 2-1/2 cups of water and cover the pressure cooker. Pressure Cook for About 4 to 5 Whistles and Turn off the Heat.allow the pressure to release naturally. Once done, open the cooker and add the cooked spinach/ palak to the dal. Give it a brisk boil and turn off the heat. Check the salt and adjust to taste accordingly. Transfer the dhaba style dal palak to a serving bowl.for the tadka, heat ghee in a tadka pan, add cumin seeds, red chillies and garlic. Saute until they crackle and the garlic turns golden. Add this tadka to the dhaba style dal palak and given it a stir.finally squeeze in the lemon juice and serving.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Tablespoon Gingelly Oil, 1/4 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/4 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds) Asafoetida (Hing) A Pinch, 3 Dry Red Chillies, 1/2 Curllies, 1/2 Curllies Curry Leaves, 3 Urad Dal Papad Broken In To Quarters, 1 Cup Tamarind Water, 1 Teaspoon Sambar Powder, 1 Tablespoon Jaggery, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 3 Cups Water Salt As Needed
recipe_description: To begin making the Appalam vatha kuzhambu recipe, prepare all the ingredients and keep them ready.heat oil in a preheated pan over medium heat. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, curry leaves and drilled red chilies. Allow it to crackle.add the onions and saute until the onions have softened and lightly golden. Once the onions have softened, add the tamarind water, turmeric powder, sambar power, jaggery and appalam.add 3 cups of water, salt to taste and simmer the Appalam Vatha KuzHhambu, in the CUSHAMBU, and the APPalam As well. The Appalam vatha kuzhambu will have a gravy like consistency with the starch from the Appalam giving it this texture. Adjust the consistency of the kuzhambu by adding water and also check the Salt and Spices and Adjust to Taste Accordinglyserv

recipe_ingredients: 200 Grams Green Beans (French Beans) Finelt Chopped, 1 Tablespoon Gingelly Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split), 2 Sprrige Currig Currig Currig Curriges, 1/2 Teasolethesoothesoothesoothesoothesoh, 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 4 Dry Red Chillies Salt To Taste, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi)
recipe_description: To begin making the green beans paruppu usili recipe, soak the toor dal in water for about 1 hour.Onece the Dal is soaked until soft; Drain the Excess Water.into a Food Processor/Mixer, Add in the Toor Dal, Turmic Powder and Driad Red Chillies and BLEND to Make a Coarse Mickle. You want the paruppu usili to have a coarse texture when steames Heat. Once steamed, turn off the heat and keep aside and allow it to cool. Once the cooled, transfer to a large mixing bowl and crumble the steamed paruppu - this mixture is called the paruppu usili (crumbled dal). Keep this aside.The next step is to cook the green beans. In a pressure cooker, add the chopped green beans along with 2 tablespoons of water. Sprinkle in a little salt. Pressure cook the green beans for 2 whistles on high heat, turn off the heat and run the pressure cooker under water to release pressure immediatily. The beans will be cooked perfectly with loss of color and nutrition. Place a wok or kadai on medium heat and add a teaspoon of oil. On the oil is hot, add mustard seeds and urad dal and allow it to crackle and the dal to turn golden brown.after the seeds have crackled, Add Asafoetida, The Curry Leaves and The Steamed Parupu Usili and Mix Thoroghe. Saute the Dal in the Oil for a Good 3 to 4 Minutes Until It Gets a Perfect Crumbly Texture. Once you have a good texture, stir in the green beans and turn off the heat. Check the salt and adjust to taste accordingly.Serv

recipe_description: To begin making the jowar atta roti/ phulka recipe, keep all the ingredients ready. Into a large mixing bowl, add JOWAR Flour, Whole Wheat Flour and Salt to Taste. Drizzle 1 Teaspoon of Oil Towards the end and knead until the dough comes away from the sides of the pan and is smooth.divide the douto lemon size portions. Take a portion of the dout and dust it in wheat flour. Roll the dout a 5 to 6 inch diameter circle.place the rolled out dout dough on a preheated skillet and cook for a less seconds until small bubbles Flame. Cook Till JOWAR ROTI POFFS up. Keep Flipping and Cooking the JOWAR ATTA ROUTI TILL BROWN Spots appear on both sides and the roti is cooked through. Once done transfer the JOWAR Atta Roti a serving plate. Drizzle ghee Around the roti and serve immmediatedly. Serve the JOWAR ATTA ROTI ROTI Recipe along with Panchmel Dal, Aloo Baingan Ki Sabzi and Satvik Carrot Sprout Salad to make it a Complete Meal.

recipe_description: To begin making thekera tenga recipe, heat a wide pan with oil and shallow fry the potato pieces and set them aside.heat oil and tempera with ingredients giving 'for tempering' for tempering '. recipe_description: To begin making the vaghareli roti, in a large bowl, combine the chopped rotis.heat oil in a pan on medium high; Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and allow them to crackle. recipe_description: To begin making the Railway Mutton Curry, firstly marinate the Mutton with ginger garlic paste, yogurt, turmeric powder, mustard oil, red chilli powder, salt for minimum 3 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.Dry roast fennel seeds, black peppercorn, coriander and Cumin seeds and grind them to a fin powder.peel the potatoes and cut them into hal. Once done, open the cooker and mash the Dal Well. Heat ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat; Add the mustard seeds and allow it to crackle. recipe_description: We begin making the khatti methi lauki dal recipe, prepare all the ingredients and keep them ready.preheat oil in a pressure cooker over Medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and allow it to crackle.add the cinnamon stick, bay leaf, ginger, tomatoes and curry leaves. Saute for About Minute Until The Tomatoes are Slightly Mushy.Add the Turmic Powder, Red Chilli Powder, Salt, Washed Toor Dal, Chopped Bottle Gourd, Salt to Taste, Jaggery and 2-1/2 Cups Cook the Khatti Meethi Lauki Dal For 3 Whistles. After 3 Whistles, Turn the Heat to Low and Simmer for 3 Minutes and Turn off the Heat.allow the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure releases, check the taste, add salt if required and adjust the chilli and jaggery as well decordingly.when you are ready to serve, give it a brisk boil just and Squeeze in the Juice for Schoper Leaves.Transfer The Khatti Meethi Lauki Dal to a Serving Bowl. recipe_description: To begin making the bhaat ka thepla, in a large mixing bowl combine all the ingredients -mashed rice, gram flour, asafoetida power, asafoetida powder, Turmeric Powder, Green Chillies, Cabbage, Onion, Onion, Onion, on Combine the dough until it all comes togeether. Divide the mixture into equal portions.preheat the skillet on medium heat. Keep water in a separet bowl to wet your fingers and a wet the muslin cloth dipped in water squeezing out any excess water.spread the cloth on a smooth counters. Take a portion of the mixture and pat it on the wet cloth to about 4 inches in diameter. Keep thepla to medium thickness as you don't want it to be breaking while flipping it on the tawa. Carefully lift the cloth along with the patted mixture and flip the patted rice thepla over on the skillet. Drizzle a Teaspoon of Oil Around the Patted Rice and Cook on Medium Heat.afer about 3 minutes, you will be able to flip the bhaat ka thepla over to the other side. Allow each side to cook on Medium Heat for About 3-4 Minutes, Until theplas are slight Crisp and Browned. Remove from Heat and place on a serving plater.Continue the same procedure with the other portions.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Yellow Moong Dal (Split), 1/2 Cup White Urad Dal (Whole), 1 Inch Ginger Finly Chopped, 2 Green Chillies Finly Chopped Salt To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Cumin Seeds Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finely Chopped Ingredients For The Filling, 1 Cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Grated, 1/2 Cup Onion Finly Chopped, 1/2 Cup Tomatoes Finly Chopped, 1 Green Chilli Finly Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Chapoon Pos Finely Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the moong dal cheela recipe, soak the moong dal and urad dal togeether for at least 6 hours/overnight.grind moong dal along with green chilies and ginger into a smoth batter adding JUSTER DOTER DEAN BATER DIANDING JUN BATER DIA The consistency of the batter should be that of a pancake (do not add too much water) .combine all the ingredients for the paneer filling mixture. Fill about scoop of paneer filling along the center diameter of cheela and fold it lengthwise tightly. Cut the roll diagonally into 2 or three pieces and sprinkle more chaat masala on the top.serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Mutton, 1/2 Cup Shallots, 1/3 Cup Tomatoes, 2 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1 Green Chilli, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Tablespoon Red Shidershes Frecos Taste, 1 Tablespoon Coconut Oil For The Masala, 1 Tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 8 Dry Red Chillies, 8 Cloves Garlic, 2 Inch Ginger, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 1-1/2 Teaspoon Whole Black Chesponcorns Laung), 2 Cardamom Powder (Elaichi), 1 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1/2 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 1/2 Cup Shallots, 1 Teaspoon Coconut Oil
recipe_description: To prepare kerala varutharacha mutton curry recipe, Mash Mutton Pieces Well. Add the Mutton Pieces Into Pressure Coukeer Along With Cumin Seeds and Little Shallot Slices, Turmeric Powder and Salt. Add ½ cup water and pressure cook for 1 whistle.Now in a wok add 1 teaspoon coconut oil. Add shallots, ginger and garlic pieces and sauté for 2 minutes. Remove it from the pan and keep aside. Add Driad Red Chilies, Coriander Seeds, Cumin seeds, Fennel Seeds, Cinnamon, Cloves, Cardamom seeds, and roast.afer add grated coconut and sauté till spices are roasted cells and coconut Turns Golden Brown. Make sure not to burn the spices. Remove from the pan.transfer shallots, ginger garlic mix, roasted spice-coachut mix into a blender. Add little water and grind into smooth paste.in a walk add coconut oil. Saute SLICED Shallots Until Translucent. Add the coconut Slices.add the cooked mutton pieces and roast for 5 minutes.next add tomato Slices and continue to cook.after some time add add add add red chili power, salt to taste and mix.add the GRONDD MAINDD MAINDD DEDDDDD The sides of the pan.add the water in which mutton was cooked into the walk and brings it to boil.Sprinkle curry leaves. Check for Seasoning.Onece Fully Cooked Turn Off Flame and Serve Kerala Varutharacha Mutton Curry Recipe Hot with Steamed Rice.SERVE Kerala Varutharacha Mutton Curry Ailong with Appam, Idiyapam or rice

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Rice Cooked Sesame (Gingelly) Oil Or Gingely Oil As Required Ingredients For Pulikachal (Puliyodharai / Puliyogare Spice Mix), 2 Teaspoons Tamarind Paste, 1/2 Teaspoon Jaggery, 1/4 Cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali) Roasted Salt As Needed Water As Needed Ingredients To Roast And Grind For Pulikachal, 2 Tablespoons Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 4 Dry Red Chilli, 3 Teaspoons Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Tablespoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 3 Dry Red Chilli, 1 Tablespoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 1/2 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1-1/2 Teaspoon Turmoon Turmon Turmon Turmon Turmon Powderic Oil
recipe_description: Method for Making Pulikachal-Soak The Tamarind in Water for at Least One Hour and Extract the Juice. Keep the Juice Asidetake all the ingredients listed under roast and grind and dry roast them until it turns golden brown. Cool Them Completely and Grind to a Fine Powder in a Mixer Grinder.Heat Oil in a Heavy Bottomed PAN, Add the Mustard Seeds, Read Chillies and Allow It to Cracked Add the tamarind extract, turmeric power, salt, curry leaves and asafoetida. This is the Pulikachal mixture to make the tamarind rice.you can store this mixture in the refrigerator for about a week and use it as and when it requires.

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Prawns Devened And Chopped Small, 1 Onion Chopped, 1 Tomato Chopped For The Recheado Masala, 10 Dry Red Chillies, 3 Cloves Garlic, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 1 Teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns, 1 Cup Whole Wheat Flour, 1/2 Cup Vivatta Maida, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Salt, 1 Tablespoon Sugar, 1/2 Cup Butter (Unsalted) Cut Into Small Pea Saze Piies
recipe_description: To begin making the goan style Empada de Camarao Recipe, We will make the Pie Crust First. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and sugar. Add the butter into the Flour mixture.work the butter into the flour with your fingers until the mixture results a coarse meal. When you hold the flour togeether, it should hold tight and when you let it loose, it will fall apart.Sprinkle ice water NTO the Flour and butter Mixture. For this heat milk in a heavy bottomed saucepan and brings the milk to a boil.Ol.Ol.Ol.Ol.Once the milk come to a boil, turn the heat to low and simmer the Milk with almost Continuous Stirring and Reduce the MILKE The MILK with the MILK with more. It is important to note that during the initial stages of milk condensation frequent stirring is important, but as it begins to start condensing and reaches less than half the quantity it is absolutely important that you stir continuously.This is because condensed milk tends to get stuck to the bottom and sides of the pan and burn; Leading to a very unpleasant taste. Burning of Milk is also toxic so you want to be extra careful.Once it is reduced to about more than half of its Quantity stir in the Sugar Untilwes Completely. Pour the kesar pista kulfi mixture in the mounds until 3/4th full, seal them airtight and freeze them in the refrigerator. The Kesar pista kulfi takes a good fifteen hours or more to freeze. If you get wooden ice cream sticks in the market, you can insirt them in and freeze the kulfis.ONCE FROZEN You Can Run the Kesar Pista Kulfi Moulds Under Water to Enable Easy Reluase on Holi or diwali party or when you have an elabrate Indian dinner menu planned for your parties. TOSS It Well and Coat It Even. Check for Seasoning as Well.Pressure Cook the Yam for 4 Whistles, Let the Pressure Get Released Naturally. Pour it into a serving dish.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Sooji (Semolina/ Rava) Roasted, 1/2 Cup Instant Oats (Oatmeal), 1/2 Cup Curd (Dahi/ Yogurt), 1 Teaspoons Enos Fruit Salt Salt To Taste Red Chilli Power For Sprinkling For, 1 Tablespoons Oil Plus Extra Oil For Greasing, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1 Teaspoon Sesame Seeds (Til Seeds), 1/4 Teaspoon Ssp Aspafotida (Hing), 2 Green Clites Slites Slites, Fresh Coconut Grated Coriander (Dhania) Leaves A Few Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the instant sooji oats dhokla recipe, add the roasted sooji, instant oats, yogurt, salt to taste in a bow. Add Little water at a time and stir to make a thick batter.allow the batter to rest for 5 minutes and add enos salt. Stir Well to Combine.Prepare The Steamer By Pouring Water and Preheating it on Medium Heat. Grease the Dhokla Pans with a Little Oil.Pour The Dhokla Batter Into The Greased Dhokla Plates. Sprinkle some chilli power over the dhokla and place in Dhokla Steamer.cover the Steamer and Let it Steam for About 10-15 Minutes Until A Tester, like a butter KNIFE, When Inseted Clean. Once done turns off the heat. Remove the stand from the steamer and allow it to rest for a couple of minutes.cut the sooji oats dhokla into squares or diamonds and arrange them on a serving platter and keep aside.into a preheated pan. Once the oil is hot add the musteds, sesame seeds and asafoetida. Allow the seeds to crackle.Once by add the green chillies, curry leaves. Stir for a more seconds and turn off the heat. Spread the seasoning over the sooji oats dhokla. Garnish with a less chopped coriander leaves and grated coconut and servE. Serve This Instant Sooji Oats Dhokla Recipe - Steamed Semolina & Oats Dhokla with Green Chutney and Masala Chai for Breakfast or a tea time snack.

recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Cup Jowar Flour (Sorghum), 1/2 Cup Rice Flour, 1 Cup Water, 1 Teaspoon Salt, 1 Teaspoon Oil
recipe_description: To begin making jolada roti, heat water in a pan and add salt and oil to the water. Mix it well. When it starts boiling, add the rice flour and mix continuously to avoid any lumps Till all the water is absorbed.Once the water is absorbed, turns off the heat and allow it coole for 5 minters.After 5 Minutes, Add the The cooked rice flour and knead well. Keep Adding Little Hot Water at a Time Until The Dough batcomes soft and non-picture .KNEAD properly, as we will make the roti's mmedietedly with giving on resting on resting on the dough Dry. The next step is to cook the rotis. Keep a iron tawa/griddle on medium flame.Dust your rolling surface with little whole wheat flour.Now we will start making the rotis using our hands. Divide the dough into 6 - 8 equal portions and roll out a portion of the dough into a round and thick roti using your hands. You can shape the roti by pressing out a circle with Side for a less more seconds. Lift the roti with tongs and roast over the direct flame till brown spots appear on both the sides.Similarly, prepare the remaining Jolada Rotis.Serve Jolada Roti Recipe (Jowar Bhakri) along with Karnataka Style Badanekai Yennegai Gojju Recipe (Stuffed Brinjal Recipe), A bowl of yogurt (curd) and uchellu chutney for north karnataka inspired meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 1 Cup Tamarind Water, 200 Grams Drumstick Leaves (Moringa/ Murungai Keirai) To Taste For Seasoning, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Ssp Asafoetida (Hing)
recipe_description: To begin making the murungai keerai sambar recipe - Drumstick Leaves Sambar, First Prep All the ingredients and Keep Them Ready.Into a pressure cooker, Add the tamarind water, onion, mooli, muoli, muoli, murungai keera, murungai keera, murungai keera, murungai keera, murungai keera, murungai keera, murungai keerai, salt and soult Add 1 cup of water and pressure cook for 2 whistles.turn off the heat and release the pressure immediatily, to prevent the murungai keirai from Getting over. Into a pressure cooker, add the toor dal along with 2-1/2 cups of water, turmeric power and pressure cook for 2 whistles. After 2 Whistles, Turn the Heat to Low and Simmer for 4 to 5 Minutes and Turn off the Heat.allow the pressure to release naturally. Once released, open the pressure cooker and mash the dal well using a potato masher.add the cooked dal into the murungai keirai sambar masala and stirll. Check the salt and adjust according. Heat Ghee in a Tadka Pan Over Medium Heat; Add the mustard seeds and allow it to cracke. Add the asafoetida and curry leaves and turns off the heat.pour this seasoning over the Murungai Keirai Sambar and Give it a brishk boil for a couple of minutes and turns off the heat.Transfer the Murungai Kerai SARVINGI SARVING BOWING Serve the Murungai Keirai Sambar Recipe, Along with Steamed Rice, Cabbage Poriyal and A Carrot Cucumber Tomato Salad with Lemon and Corrider Recipe for a Classic South Indian Lunch or Dinner. You can also pack the murungai keirai sambar into a lunch box by mixing it Along with Rice.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves), 1/2 Cup Sweet Corn, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 2 Tomatoes Finly Chopped, 15 Cashew Nuts Soaked In Warm Waters For, 15 Minutes, 1/2 Cup Fresh Cream, 1/4 Teramon Turaric, 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder), 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar, 2 Teaspoons Butter (Salted), 2 Cloves Cardamom (Elachi) Pods/Seeds, 1 Teaspoon Cimin Ciminck (Dalchini), 2 Cloves (Laung), 3 Whole Black Peppercorns, 3 Whole Black Peppercorns
recipe_description: To begin making corn methi malai recipe, the first step is to wash the fenugreek leaves/methi leaves thoroughly and roughly chop them.soak the cashew nuts in warm water for a minimum of 15 minimums. In a pressure cook add the sweeet corn along with 2 tablespoons of water and pressure cook for 1 whistle. Turn off the flame and allow the pressure to release naturally. Drain the water and keep the corn aside. In a pan, heat oil on medium flame. Once the oil is hot, add chopped onions and saute until until translucent.next add chopped tomatoes and cook until the tomatoes are soft and mushy. Turn off the flame and allow the mixture to cool. Add the cooked tomatoes and onions to a mixer along with the soaked cashews and grind everything togeether to form a smooth paste. Keep aside.in a heavy bottomed pan, heat butter on low flame. Once the butter becomes frothy, add the spices - cardamom, cumin seeds, cinnamon, clooves and black peppercorns.saute in the butter until the Spices Turn Aromatic. Next add the ground onion Tomato mixture, required Amount of Salt, Turmic Powder, Chilli Powder and Dry Mango Powder to it.mix and Cook for a Minute Until Raw Smell of the Spices Go AWOREST MOKEHLYS . Stir and Cook Simmered on a Medium Flame Until oil separates.finally Add Sugar and Cream to the Corn Methi mixture, Stir Well and Cook on a Completely Low Flame for two Minutes. Turn off the flame. The delicious corn methi malai gravy is ready. Serve Corn Methi Malai Gravy Recipe along with Hot Phulkas or Naan Along with a Kala Chana Salad and Lauki Raita to make it a complete Sunday Night Dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Tamarind Water, 1/4 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 2 Teaspoons Rasam Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Tomato Diced, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jea Teaspon Powdera), 4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1 Tablespoon Fresh Thyme Leaves, 5 Curry Leaves Chopped Finely Salt To Taste Water As Needed, 1 Tablespoon Lemon Juice For Tempering, 2 Teaspoons Ghee, 1/2 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Curry Leaves
recipe_description: To prepare lemon thyme rasam recipe, keep the tamarind water ready. Into a Sauce Pan, Add the Tamarind Water, Tomatoes, Rasam Powder, Cumin Powder, PEPPER POWDER, Hing, A Few Curry Leaves and Salt into a PAN. Add the thyme leaves, stir.bring to a Boil on Medium-High Heat and then Simmer Till the Tomatoes are cooked. Clean and wash dal and pressure cook dal with enough water with 1 cup of water and turmeric power for 3-4 whistles. Turn off the flame. Once the pressure from the cooker is released naturally. , Whisk the Dal and Keep It Aside.Add the Dal to the Tamarind Tomato Base and Bring The Thyme Rasam to a Boil and then Reduce the Stove to low flame. Let the rasam simmer for 5 minutes or more, so that the raw taste of the chilli in the rasam powder if incorports well into the rasam.adjust salt and simmer for 3 more minutes and turn off the heat. Tadka Pan, Heat a Teaspoon of Ghee and Splutter Mustard and Cumin seeds. Add curry leaves, let it crackle and take it off heat. Pour the tadka to the lemon thyme rasam.squeeze lemon to the lemon thyme rasam recipe just before serving and pair it up with rice and beetroot poriyal recipe for lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Ragi Flour (Finger Millet/Nagli), 1/2 Cup Bajra Flour (Pearl Millet), 1 Cup Rice Flour, 1 Cup Drumstick Leaves (Moringa/Muurungai Keerai) Salt As Required, 2 Tape Coconut Grated
recipe_description: To begin making the murungai keirai putu recipe, heat ghee in a heavy bottomed pan.add the drumstick leaves to it and saute for a minute until until it shrinks. Keep Aside.Add Ragi Flour, Bajra Flour and Rice Flour in a Mixing Bowl. Add salt to it and mix well. Sprinkle water little by little and rub into flour to make it crumbly.it has to be in puttu consistency. That is, if small amount of flour is taken and presided in palm it has to hold shape and crumble if posted further tightly.add waters to the base vessel of puttu maker UNTIL 3/4th and Place It is Now place the disc with holes at bottom of top cylindrical vessel.Add 3 teaspoons of coconut to the cylindrical vessel, followed by 2 teaspoons of sauteed drumstick leaves and half of the prepared puttu flour.Layer it again with coconut, drumstick leaves and remaining puttu Flour until it reaches top. Close with lid and place the cylindrical vessel on top of base vessel on stove.steam the murungai keirai vessel for 10 minutes on medium flame or until the top starts releasing steam fut flicce Vssel using the stick that decompanied the putu maker.the healthy drumstick leaves putus putu is ready to serve. Serve Murungai Keirai Puttu Along with Kerala Kadala Curry for a Healthy Breakfast Meal or You Can ALSO RILISH IT For LUNCH.

recipe_ingredients: 3 Cups Brinjal (Baingan/Eggplant) Cubed, 1 Cup Potatoes (Alo) Peled And Cubed, 1/2 Teaspoon Kalonji (Onion Nigella Seeds), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera) Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar, 2 Tablespoons Coriander (Dhania) Leaves  Chopped, 2 Teaspoons Oil Salt  To Taste, 2 Teaspoons Oil Salt  To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the ringna batata nu shaak | Aloo Baingan Ki Sabzi Recipe, We will first heat oil in a pressure cooker on medium heat. Add cumin seeds, nigella seeds and allow them to crackle. Add the Tomato Puree, Turmeric Powder, Red Chilli Powder, Coriander Powder, Garam Masala Powder and Sauté until it com as to a single boil. Stir in the potatoes and brinjal adding 3/4 cup of water and sugar, salt to taste. Close the pressure cooker and pressure cook for 4 whistles. Turn off the flame. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure has released open the lid the whole dish will look a little mushy and have a little a little gravy that is formed from the tomatoes, the softened brinjal and the starch from the potatoes. Baingan Ki Sabzi | Ringna Batata Nu Shaak Along with Methi Thepla or Phulka's and Boondi Raita Recipe Spiced with Black salt for a simple lunch.

Marinete the chops with 1 teaspoon red chili power and salt for about 2 to 4 hours.to the marinated mutton add 1 teaspoon cumin seeds and 1 finly chopped onions and 1 Cup Water and 1 Cup Water and Pressure Cup Water and Pressure 3 to 4 to 4 to 4 White FORSTES Them along with Green Chili. recipe_description: To begin making the khandeshi dal recipe, we will first begin with the pakka masala for the Dal.heat the oil in a wide pan. Add grated coconut and SLICED Onses, Sauté for 3 to 4 Minutes.Add Dry Red Chillies, Coriander Cumin Powder, Cloves, Cardamom, Cinnamon and Black Peppercorn. Saute for another 2 to 3 minutes on low-medium flame.add garlic and sauté on medium flame for few seconds. Turn off the stove. Let it cool down completely.once cooled, blend in a mixer or in a blender to a smooth power/paste and keep it aside.the next step is to prepare the Dal. Clean, wash and soak the mix dals and moong in a water for atlest 30 to 40 minutes.pressure cook dal and moong in a cooker with 2 cups of water, salt and turmeric power. recipe_description: To begin making the malvani fish curry recipe, we will first wash the king fish well with water, drain the water completely. Add in Red Chilli Powder, Turmeric, Cumin Powder, Coriander Powder, Salt to Taste and Lemon Juice. Give it a mix and marinate the fish for at least 30 minutes.

recipe_description: To begin making carrot cauliflower peas sabzi recipe first prep all the required ingredients.heat coconut oil in a pan and on the oil is hot add in all the whole the whole add in all the way nice aroma.add in the carrots, cauliflower florets and green peas. Saute for a minute.Season with salt and sprinkle some water in the pan and stir.cover and cook till the gajar and gobi are almost cured and until all the flavors are incorporated into the carrot cauliflower paasi recipe. Finally Once Done, Stir in the Black Pepper Powder and Toss Well to Combine. Once done, check the taste of the Gajar Gobi Matar Sabzi and adjust salt accordingly.Serve this delicately flavoured Carrot Cauliflower Peas Sabzi Recipe with Lahsuni Palak Dal,  Ragi Wheat Phulka and Methi Raita Flavoured With Garlic for a nice comforting diabetic meal.

Dough and keep aside.pre heat a non stick tawa or roti pan.Now wet a muslin cloth with water or green a plantain leaf. Grease your palm and place a portion of the dough at the center of the cloth. Using your fingers pat the dough to make circular discs of medium thickness. Make Three Small Holes in the Akki Roti for even cooking of the akki roti.preheat a skillet over medium heat and green the skillet. Carefully invert the akki roti onto Flip the roti and drizzle a teaspoon of oil around the roti and cook it on bot sides until brown spots appear.Onece don, rem Or an evening tea time snack along with a hot cup of filter coffee or masala chai.

Heat a heavy bottomed kadai or pan with oil and tempera with ingredients giving under 'to tempered'. The mixture will thicken and turn glossy. Switch off.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) Cut Into, 1 Inch Pieces, 3 Potatoes (Aloo) Peled And Cut Into Wedges, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1/2 Teaspoon Safoetida (Hing), 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania) Salt To Taste, 2 Tablespoons Oil
recipe_description: To begin making the aloo bhindi sabzi recipe, heat a tablespoon of oil in a heavy bottom pan over medium heat.add a teaspoon of cumin seeds, allows to crackle. Add half the curry leaves, cut potatoes along salt, 1/2 Teaspoon of Turmic Powder. Stir to combine.cover Minutes and turns off the heat. Keep covered aside, while you cook the bhindi. Cover the PAN (with Lid Slightly Open) and Allow the Bhindi to Simmer, Stirring Occasionally.Bhindi releases moisture when covered covered and will get sticky. But after sometimes it will turn fine.Once the bhindi is slightly tender, add the turmeric power, 1/2 teaspoon of coriander powder and read chilli power.keep the Pan Slightly OPEN and COK TILGHTLY OPEN and The Bhindi will change its color to brown.Once don, gently stir in the sauted aloo into the bhindi and combine well. Cover the pan with the lid and simmer for another couple of minutes.Turn off the heat and allow the vegetable to rest in the pan, covered for another 3 to 4 minutes. This helps the vegetable to gain a little moisture and gather its itgeator.Serv

recipe_description: To begin with vendakkai podi curry, prepare all the ingredients and keep them ready.heat oil into a preheated PAN; Add the mustard seeds and allow it to crackle. Add curry leaves and asafoetida and saute for a more seconds.add the cut vendakkai/ ladys finger, Sprinkle some salt and give it a stir. Cover the pan and with the Lid Little ajar cook the vendakkai until done. Keep stirring it intermittently in between so that the vendakkai poriyal gets cooked even.once 3/4 th done, stir in the sambar power, turmeric power and idli paruppu Podi. Give it a stir and cover and cook the vendakkai podi curry for another couple of minutes.finally stir in the fresh coconut and check if more Salt is needed and adjust to taste to according. Turn off the heat and transfer the vendakkai podi curry to a served bowl and serving hot.Serv

recipe_description: To begin making fusion style paneer malai makhani recipe, prep all the ingredients negreded for this recipe.in a kadai heat coconut oil, when it is hot enough, add in the cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds and learned Wait for about 10 seconds or till sizzling, and add in the curry leaves. Sauté Till the raw smell goes and appears even add in the coriander power, black pepper power and red chilli power, continue frying for 2 minutes for another till the spices ARESTES AREVESDES , Till the tomatoes are softtened. recipe_ingredients: 10 Dry Red Chillies Adjust According To Your Taste, 2 Tablespoons Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 1/2 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 300 Grams Sorrel Leaves (Gongura), 2 Tablespoons Sesame (Gingelly) Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Teaspoon Black Urad Dal (Split), 2 To, 3 Dry Red Chillies, 3 To, 4 Cloves Garlic Crushed (Optional)
recipe_description: To begin making the Andhra Gongura Pachadi, first pick the sorrel/ gongura leaves from the stems, wash them thoroughly, dry it with a kitchen towel. Once dry, chop the gongura leaves and keep aside.next, heat a medium heat and roast the dry red chillies, coriander seeds and fenugreek seeds untilke seeds untilil they are light brown, aromatic and focus. Take care not to burn themtransfer them into a plate and set them aside to cool. Use as many or as less red chillies as you need, depending on the quality of the chillies and your desired Spice Levels. Once cooled, add them to a mixer grinder and make a smooth power.in the same pan; Heat oil until warm; Add the mustard seeds, urad dal and red chillies and allow it to crackle and the dal to turn golden brown and crisp.ster in the crushed garlic and saute for a more a more a few seconds until you can smell the aroma coming through. Once once, add the Chopped Gongura Leaves, Salt to Taste and the Ground Powder.Keep Stirring Until The Gongura Leaves Become Soft; It will take less than a minute. Check the taste and adjust the salt and chilli accordingly.you will also not also notice You can store the gongura pachadi in the refrigerator for a week and serve it Along with Dosa, Idli, Cheela, Adai, Pesarattu or Simply Along with Hot Steamed Rice and Ghee.

recipe_ingredients: 250 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese), 1 Tablespoon Kasuri Methi (Driad Fenugreek Leaves), 3 Tablespoons Curd (Dahi/Yogurt), 1/2 Cup Fresh Cram, 1/2 Teaspoon Teaspoon Garam Masala, 1/4 Techasoomoosoomohasoomoha To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Ghee For Cooking To Be Ground To Paste, 8 Cashew Nuts, 4 Cloves Garlic, 2 Green Chillies Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the lehsuni methi paneer recipe, soak the cashews in a bowl with 1/4 cup water for about an hour.after an hour, grind cashew along with ginger, garlic and green chillies and the suaked cashew waters Keep aside.in a big bowl, Add Cashew Ginger Garlic and Chilli Paste, Fresh Cream, Curd, Garam Masala, Cardamom Powder Salt and Whisk Well to Form a smooth mixture. Heat Ghee in a Pan Over Medium Heat; Add the cashew cream mixture, the chopped paneer cubes and simmer for about 5 minutes.add water to adjust the consistency of the mixture while it's simmering. Check the salt and spices and adjust to taste accordingly. Finally stir in the kasuri methi, cover the pan and turn off the heat.allow the flavors of methi to get infused into the lehsuni paneer methi sabzi. Transfer the Lehsuni Methi Paneer Into A Serving Bowl and Serve Hot.Serve Lehsuni Methi Paneer Recipe Along with Smoked Makhani Dhaba Style Dal, Jera Rice, Tomato onion Tadka Race and Garry A Hus.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Rice Soaked For, 3 Days (Wash & Change Water Everyday), 1 Cup Jaggery Powdered, 1/2 Cup Milk, 1/2 Ripe Bananas (Optional) Mashed, 3 Tablespoons Poppy Seds Oil A Pinechch
recipe_description: Drain the water from the bowl. Spread the drained rice in a cotton cloth and allow them to fry for 4-5 hours. Ensure that the rice should not lose its moisture completely. It has to retain the minimal amount of moisture.grind them without adding water an coarse power. This is called pithi. You could also use about 1-1/2 cups of rice flour directly to make this recipe as well.Now, in another bowl, Add Ground Rice, Powedred Jaggery and Combine Them Togetle BIELK LITTLE and MILK LITTELE BILTLE and MIILK LITTLE and MII It should be in the dough consistency.the addition of Banana ensures the SUGAR is Completely Dissolved. You can optionally add half of banana mashed, at this time and mix the dough till it comes toge together. Gently flattened the balls with your fingers so that one side of it is coated with poppy seeds. The flattened disc should retain their shape.in a kadai, Heat Over Medium Heat.Onece the oil is hot, place the flattened disc with poppy seeds on the upper side and fry them in batches. Pour hot oil over the disc so that it is even cooked and puff up.place the deep fried pasties on the tissue paper for the excess oil to drain.deep fry the remapp of a festive meal.
