Saute for 10 more secondsadd the soaked and washed rice, curry powder salt and stock pressure Cook for 4 Whistle Till the Rice is Done.Remove and Mix Well Add the chickpeas as well and check for Add chopped coriander leaves and serve.serv

recipe_ingredients: 8 Baby Brinjals Washed And Cut Into Wedges, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste, 2 Tablespoons Coconut Oil To Shallow Fry For The Curry Masala, 1 Tablespoon Coconut Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves Torn, 2 Green Chillies Slit, 1 Cup Pearl Oness (Sabarl Ooners) Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 2 Tomatoes Finely Chopped, 1/2 Cup Coconut Milk Salt To Taste, 6 Sprig Corridar (Dhania) Leaves Finly Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the kerala style brinjal curry recipe, we will first shallow fry the baby brinjals. Cut the brinjals into wedges, and place them in water as you cut them to prevent them getting discolored. In oil a pan heat over medium heat; Add the cut brinjal wedges, salt, turmeric powder and saute for a few minutes until they soften and get cooked. Cover the pan and cook the brinjal to fasten the cooking process. Once done, turn off the heat and keep aside.to make the curry, heat coconut oil in a heavy bottom pan over medium heat; Add the mustard seeds, fennel seeds and curry leaves. Allow them to crackle. Next add the slit green chillies and onion and saute till the onions soften and turn transparent. Stir in the Tomatoes, Salt, Turmeric Powder and Red Chilli Powder. Cover the Pan and Cook Until the Tomatoes Turn Mushy. This will take about two minutes. Once the whole onion- Tomato gravy has come togetra, Reduce the heat and add in the coconut milk and the saten brinjal. Give the Kerala Style Brinjal Curry a Brisk Boil for 3 to 4 Minutes So The Flavorses SeeP Well to Make a Delicious Tasting Curry. Check the salt and adjust to suit your taste. Transfer the Kerala Style Brinjal Curry to a Serving Bowl, Stir in the Chopped Coriander Leaves and Serve Hot. Serve Kerala Style Eggplant curry with Spiced Ghee Rice Recipe | Neychoru, Kerala Parotta and Cabbage Thoran for a Simple Weekday Lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Tapioca Root Or Kappa Cleane And Diced Into, 1 �Pieces, 1 Carrot (Gajjar) Cut Diagonally, 1 Onion Sliced ��1 Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 1 Cardamom (Elaichi), 1 Cardamom (Elaichi Pods/Seeds, 3 Cloas Peppercorns Crushed, 200 Ml Coconut Milk, 1/2 Inch Ginger Sliced ��Thin, 2 Green Chillies Deseed And Slit In Two, 2-3 Sprig Curry Leaves Rough Torn, 1, 1/2 Tablespoon Ia, 1/2 Tablespoon Oil, 1/2, 1/2 Tabonsosososososospo Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the kerala style kappa Ishtoo recipe (tapioca stew recipe), Start by adding the tapioca pies in a cooker along with green chillies, ginger and salt. Add in about 2 cups of water, so that the tapioca is full immersed and pressure-cock it on high until you hear 3 whistles. When done, switch of the gas and set the cooker down.heat a kadai with coconut oil, crackle the Mustard seeds and curry leaves and then add the Cinnamon Sticks, Cloves and Cardamom. Saute Till the Aromas Are Relapsed and then Add in the Sliced ​​Onions and Carrot and Sauate for 2 Minutes Until the Onions Turn Soft.Add the Coconut Milk Along with the Tapioca, Give It A Stir and KEEP the STIR and KEEPEPERED FOR Turn off the heat and let the tapioca stew rest for a more minutes to allow the flavors to blend toge. Check for Seasoning and Serve.Serve Kerala Style Kappa Ishtoo Recipe (Tapioca Stew Recipe) Along with Steamed Rice Recipe and Accompanied by Sides of Kerala Vendakka Thuran (Okra Stir Fry) Okabage Thoran.

recipe_description: To begin making moolangi tovve recipe - Radish curry, wash the moong dal couple of time with water and add it to the pressure cooker along with radish, Turmeric Powder, Turmeric Powder, Green Chillies and 1.5 Cups of Water. After three whistles turns off the heat and let the pressure release naturally. Once done, Open the Lid and Keep Aside. Heat Oil in a Pan, Add Asafoetida, Mustard and Wait for the Mustard to Pop and then Add Red Chillies and Ginger and Ginger and Ler the Red Chillies Crisp up.Now Add the Cook Mix Dal Mixture in the PAN with Salt to Taste and 1.5 CUPS of Water . Brisk Boil Till the Dal Gets Slightly Thick.ONCE it is done, add the chopped coriander and grated coconut in the moolangi tovve and give it a stir. Tun off the heat and transfer the moolangi tovve to a serving bowl and serving. Serve Moolangi Tovve Recipe - Radish curry with steamed rice recipe and elai vadam for a comforting lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 750 Grams Mutton On The Bone Salt, 1 Teaspoon Lemon Juice, 1 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) To Be Ground Into A Paste, 3 Onions Roughly Chopped, 10 Cloves Garlic, 2 Inch Ginger For The Curry, 2 Tablespoons Ghee, 2 Cloves (Laung), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 1 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Sadanf), 3 Green Chillies Sallies Sallies Sallies Sallies Sallies, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Powder, 2 Tablespoons Meat Masala, 1 Teaspoon Garamon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dridden Fenugreek Leaves)
recipe_description: To begin making spicy pressure cooker mutton curry recipe first marigated the mutton. To marinate the mutton, in a large mixing bowl, combine the washed and cleanened mutton, Along with Curd, Salt, Lemon Juice and Turmeric Powder and Mix Well. Cover and set aside for at least an hour. Make a paste of the onion, ginger and garlic and keep aside. To make the curry, heat ghee in a pressure cooker over medium heat; Add the whole spices, bay leaf, cloove, cardamom, saunf, and cinnamon stick. When the whole spices begin to sizzle, add the green chillies. Saute for 30 seconds or until the raw smell goes away. Next, add in the onion ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute. Stir in the Red Chilli Powder, Jeera Powder, Coriander Powder and Meat Masala and Continue to Saute for Another Minute. Once this mutton curry masala mixture has come togeether, Add the Tomato Puree, Cover and Cook for 2 Minutes. When you feel the raw tomato small has gone, add in the mutton along with the marinade. Stir Well, Check for Salt and Spices and Adjust Seasoning According to Your Taste. Close the pressure cooker and pressure cook the mutton curry for about 5-6 whistles and turn off the heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Then open and stir in the kasuri methi. Garnish the mutton curry with coriander leaves and servE hot. Serve Spicy Pressure Cooker Mutton Curry Recipe along with Whole Wheat Lachha Paratha and Tomato Onion Cucumber Raita for a Weekend Dinner.

recipe_description: To begin making the Punjabi Mango Pickle Recipe (Aam Ka Achaar Recipe), wash the mangoes and dry them completely making sure there is no moisture or dust.make sure your know and the platform you use for the mangoies. Cut the mangoes in half, deseed and further cut them into one inch cubes. You can leave the skin on provided they are tender and smooth. In a large step, combine the salt, turmeric powder and cut mangoes. Cover, the bowl and allow it to marinate for 1 hour.heat a small pan on medium heat, and roast the corridor seeds until you start smelling the aroma. Turn off heat and allow it to cool. Once Cooled, Using a Pestle and Mortar to Coarsely Crush the Coriander Seeds to Half.Blend the Mustard Seeds and Fenugreek seeds into a coarse power. There is no need to roast the seeds.to the marinated mangoes, add the powerard and fenugreek seeds, red chilli power, fennel seeds, kalonji seeds, and coriyander seeds. Stir Well to Combine.Add 1 cup of Mustard oil and Stir Well Again. You don't need to heat the mustard oil. Check the salt at this stage. It should feel extra salt. If it is not extra salty, add a less tablespoon of salt until you get that extra salty taste. Salt and oil are the two most important preservatives in pickling. Cover the Steel Bowl with a Muslim Cloth and Tie it tightly Around the rim. This will absorb any residual moisture. Place a steel lid over the covered bowl and set aside.afer 24 hours, open and give it a stir. Cover Again with Cloth Wrapped Tightly and A Lid and Set it to rest for another day. The third day you will notes the mangoes have suoftened. At this stage add the remaining 2 cups of mustard oil. Give it a stir. Once Again Cover tightly with Muslin Cloth and a Steel Lid. Keep it aside for 2 more days. Give it a stir.by the end of 5th day the pickle will be ready to eat. The mangoes would have softened but might still have a firm texture. The Mangoes will keep marinating and softening when it is stored in bottles and will age with time. Moisture is the biggest killer for pickles. Clean and sterilize the bottles and the lids that you will be using for storage. I Typically like to store them in multiple 500 gram glass bottles, so I only open the bottle when I am ready to use it.Transfer the Punjabi Mango Pickle Into The Clean and Dry Bottles. Add a tablespoon of mustard oil on the top once filled and seal the bottle tight and enjoy.use a clean dry spoon so that there is no moisture when you take the pickle. Mix all the ingredients well and then add the curd.mix well togeether and make a soft dough with his mixture. Make lemon sized balls with the dharan dough and press flat to make it into a cutlet shape.in a deep fry pan, add cooking oil and allow to heat up. Once the oil is hot, add the prepared dharan/besan tikkis in it and fry until they are golden brown. Drain the Dharan/ Tikki in a Kitchen Napkin to Absorb The Excess Oil and Keep It Aside. In a heavy bottomed pan, add 2 teaspoons oil. Once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds, cloves and let the spices sputter. Next add the onion pure and saute until the pure changes color. Next add the ginger garlic paste and stir until the raw smell goes away. This will take about 2 minutes.next, add the tomato pure and chopped chilies. And saute until, the gravy combines well. Finally add in the coriander power and mix well until the kadhi gravy comes togeether.simmer and add the besan tikkis into the gravy. Add 2 cups of water and cook the dharan ji kadhi a low flame with the Lid on. Let the dharan ji kadhi simmer until the tikkis turn soft. Turn off the flameadd finly chopped coriander leaves and serve your dharan ji kadhi.serv

recipe_ingredients: 2 Mango (Raw) Peled And Diced, 2 Tablespoons Jaggery, 1/2 Cup Water Salt For The Coconut Curry Paste, 3/4 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 1 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 30 Grams Tamarind, 1/2 Teaspoon Whole Black Pepper Corns, 3 Dry Red Chillies Salt For The Seasting, 2 Teaspoons Coconut Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split), 1/4 Teaspoon Safoetida (Hing)
recipe_description: To begin making the amlechi uddamethi recipe, wash peel and cut the mangoes into wedges and keep it ready. Heat a saucepan with jaggery, 1/2 cup of water, salt and the raw mango wedges. Boil on Medium Heat Until The Mangoes Soften. This will take about 5 minutes. Turn off the flame and set aside. To make the coconut curry paste, in a mixer jar, combine the grated coconut, dry red chillies, black peppercorns, turmeric power, salt and the tamarind. Add about 1/2 to ¾ cup of warm water and grind this mixture to a fin paste. Set Aside. Into a sauce pan, combine the cooked raw mangoes and the coconut curry paste and give it a brisk boil. Adjust the consistency of the curry by adding water if required. The goan amaechi uddamethi typically has a thick gravy. Check the salt and adjust according to taste. The final step is to make the seasoning. Heat coconut oil in a tadka pan over medium heat, add the urad dal and methi seeds, when they begin to change color add in the Mustard seeds, and hing. Add this tadka to the amlechi uddamethi and give it a stir. Serve hotserve the amlechi uddamethi (goan raw mango curry) along with steamed rice as a comfort food for lunch or a weeknight dinner. The Goan Mango curry also makes a great dish for parties.

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Elephant Yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu), 1/4 Cup Tamarind Water, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste Other Ingredients, 1/2 Cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali), 1 Tablespoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split)  Torn, 2 Green Chillies Finely Chopped Salt To Taste, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Read Chilli Powder
recipe_description: To begin making the chenai kizhangu masiyal, wash and clean the yam, peel the skin and cut them into 1 inch cubes. Transfer these yam cubes into the pressure cooker along with tamarind water, turmeric powder and salt to taste.close the Lid and Pressure Cook for About 4 Whistles. Turn off the heat and allows the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure has released naturally open the cooker and mash the yam using a mashr, leave a more yam chunks to add to the texture of the textai masiyal. Next cook the raw peanuts. Add the raw peanuts into a pressure cooker along with 1/2 cup of water and salt to taste.close the lid and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles. Allow the pressure to release naturally and keep aside. Once the oil is hot, add the mustard seeds and urad dal. Allow the Mustard seeds to crackle and the urad dal to turn golden brown.Once by add the hing, curry leaves, green chillies and saute for a more a few seconds. Now add the cooked and mashed yam, turmeric powder, red chillie power, salt to taste and cooked peanuts. Saute for a more minutes until well combined. Turn off the heat and transfer the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal into a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve the Chenai Kizhangu Masiyal along with Steamed Rice, Chettinad Style Poondu Rasam Recipe and Sprouted Green Gram Kosambari Recipe- With Pomegranates  for a complete meal.

recipe_description: To begin making the kongunad chicken fry recipe, wash and clean chicken breast pieces with turmeric. Drain the Excess Water and Keep It Aside. In a non-stickdrain the excess water and keep it aside.in a non-stick kadai, add the cinnamon stick, clooves, pepper powder, coriander seeds, fennel seeds and poppy seeds. Saute until the Aroma come out of it. Once fried, add red chilli and coconut. Saute for a minute. Ensure not to over fry as it may burn the red chilli.allow it to cool down and grind into a fin paste using water. In another kadai, add oil and heat it over medium flame.once the oil is hot, add chopped onlines and fry until they become translucent. Now, add turmeric, chicken, and salt.cook until the chicken turns in color, it becomes after after fear. Simmer it for more few minutes until the gravy banks dry. Sprinkle curry leaves and coriander leaves over the chicken. Give it a stir and cook for less seconds.remove from the flame. Prepare this simple yet delicious kongunad chicken fry and serve with rice and hot jera rasam for lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Basmati Rice, 3/4 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 3/4 Cup Jaggery Grated, 1/2 Teaspoon Saffron Strands, 9-10 Clooves (Laung), 4 Tablespoons Ghee, 2 Cups Water, 1/4 Cups Mixed Nuts Copped Nuts.  Almonds Chironji And Any Other Nuts Of Your Choice)
recipe_description: To begin making the maharashtrian narali bhaat recipe, first wash the rice thoroughly, drain the water and keep it aside for 45 minutes for 45 minutes.in a small pan, warm 1 tablespoon and sautes the dry fruits and nuts for 2-3 Until they turns golden and are roasted. Remove the roasted nuts from hot ghee and set aside until laater use.in a nonstick pan or heavy-bottomed pan melt 3 tablespoons of ghee on a low heat. Add the clooves and saute for 3-4 minutes.when they puff up and get aromatic, add the washed rice and toss it well until the grains are well coated with ghee.next, pour in the water, the water, the safrin strings and the brings the rice to a brisk boil. Once the rice come to a brisk boil, turn the heat to low and cover the pan. Cook the rice until all the water is absorbed. Meanwhile, add the jaggery and coconut to a small saucepan and place it on low to medium heat. Allow the Jaggery to Melt. Once the jaggery melts, pour the Jaggery and coconut mixture to the cooked rice and mix thoroughly, taking care not to break the grains. You can use a fork and gently mix at this stage, to fluff up the grains.cover the pan and cook once again on a low heat for 4-5 minutes more. Transfer the Narali bhaat to a serving bowl and garnish it with the roasted nuts.the maharashtrian narali bhaat is now ready to be served as a festive dish.

This step will basically remove the typical Sea Food Odor from the prawns.in a wok dry roast - chillies, pepper, cumin, mustard, cloves and cinnamon.grind the roasted Mix of Spices ALONGES ALOGSOTED AINTEGER, GINEG SHINE .In another wok/kadhai heat some oil, add the chopped onions and cook till golden brown.add the chopped tomatoes and curry leaves, Cook Till All the Water All the Water Evaporates and the Mixture Turns Turns All MUILNS OILL MUSHY SILEVING OILVINS and cook till the raw smell disappears.Add the prawns along with the sugar, check for spice levels and adjust accordingly.Cook till the water left by the prawns evaporates and the mixture turns into a thick gravy like consistency.Serve hot Prawn Balchao with any Roti or simple rice of your choice with a nice Tall glass of chilled beer.

recipe_description: To begin making the chilke wali mung dal curry recipe, wash and drain the split mung bean dhal. You can cook the dhal in a saucepan with lots of water till soft and tender.let the pressure drop on its oven. Open the pressure pan and mash the dal. You may choose to make it into a fine paste or leave it a little coarse.in a deep kadai or saucepan, heat oil/ghee. Once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds and cloves; Sauté for a more seconds.add the finly chopped onions along with garlic and green chillies; Fry for a more a few minutes till the onions are slightly soft and translucent. You will start to see the oil separating from the mixture. Give it a good mix and taste for seasoning. Adjust accordingly.bring to a boil on high and Once it comes to a Boil, Reduce to Medium Low and Simmer For About 3 to 4 Minutes Till the Flavors have been blended well and you get the consigncy you will get. Chopped Coriander Leaves and Serve hot.chilke wali mung dal goes well with steamed rice, any pulao or birani and any Indian flat bread like Roti, Paratha, Naan, Puri Etc.

recipe_description: To begin making the aloochi bhaji recipe, get all the ingredients ready and keep them aside.soak the channel in water for about 1 hour. recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Barnyard Millet Washed And Soaked For, 30 Minutes, 4 Cup Buttermilk Salt To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera) Pinch Asafoetida (Hing), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi)
recipe_description: To begin making maheri, heat ghee or oil in a pressure cooker. Once the ghee is hot add hing, cumin seeds and allow the seeds to crackle. Once the water starts boiling add buttermilk and brings it to boil against add the barnyard millet and mix well. Cover the pressure cooker and turn the heat to low. Pressure Cook for 2 Whistles. recipe_description: To begin making the gujarati batata pulao recipe, wash and soak rice and lentil in 3 cups of water for half an hour.using the pestle and mortar, pounder the cinnamon, cinnamon, cloves and cardamom to a fine poder.heat oyl in a fine powder.heat oylon. ; Add the Sliced ​​Onions and Sauté Until Tender and Caramelzed and Keep Aside.Make a Paste of the Ginger, Garlic and Green Chillies in a Mixer Grinder and Keep Aside.heat Ghee in a Large Sport or Handi On Mendiim Heet; Add the cumin seeds, ginger garlic paste, and sauté until the raw smell from the ginger garlic paste turns to a roasted aroma and changes color. Sauté for a more seconds. Finally stir in the soaked rice and lentils along with the water.allow the mixture to come to a boil and then turns the heat to low.cover the pan and simmer for approximately 30 minutes 30 minutes UNTIL THE RCE HAS ABSORIG Desred Texture.ONCE Cooked, Turn off the Heat and Allow The Batata Pulao Recipe to Rest for 15 Minutes. Stir in the Caramelzed Onions, Peas, Chopped Mint and Serve Warm. Serve the Gujarati Batata Pulao as it is or Along with a Palak Raita for a Quick and Easy Weeknight Dinner. recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Bottle Gourd (Lauki) Peled And Cut Into, 1/2 Inch Cubes, 1 Carrot (Gajjar) Chopped Into, 1/2 Inch Dices, 2 Snowet Potato Chopped Into, 1/2 Inch Dices, 2 Drums Green Chilli Slit, 1 Cup Tamarind Water, 2 Tablespoons Jaggery, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 2 Teaspoons Rice Flour Forf, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/4 Teaspoon Yellow Fenugreek Seeds, 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves
recipe_description: To begin making dappalam/ Andhra style vegetable stew recipe prep all the ingredient and keep them aside.add all the vegetables and green chillies into a pressure cup of waters, 33 the heat.Release the pressure immediately by lifting it with a fork or running cold water over the pressure cooker to release the pressure.In a mixing bowl combine the tamarind water along with rice flour, turmeric powder and red chilli powder and make a smooth mixture .Heat oil in a wok or a large saucepan over medium heat; Once the oil has heated add the musteds, fenugreek seeds and allow it to crackle. Stir in the hnig and curry leaves along with the tamarind mixture.add jaggery, cooked vehicles and give the dappalam a brishk bowl for about 5 to 6 minutes until the Flavours are well incorporated in stew. Check the salt and spices and adjust the taste of the dappalam accordingly.turn. Serve Dappalam Along With Steamed Rice, Andhra Style Pappu Charu and Zucchini Thogayal for a Delicious Andhra Meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Whole Wheat Flour, 200 Grams Mint Leaves (Pudina), 2 Green Chillies (Add More If You Like It Spicy), 1/2 Teaspoon Salt Oil For Kneading Ghee Or Oil For Cooking
recipe_description: To begin making the pudina tawa paratha recipe, we will first liveless the pudina leaves along with the green chillies in a blender.in a number. At a time to make firm and smooth dough. Add one tablespoon of oil to coat the dough and knead again. Cover and set aside for 15 minutes.knead once and divide the dough into 8 portions. Preheat the Iron Skillet on Medium Heat.roll the paratha dough portions into balls and flatn them with the palm of your hand. Toss is on flour and roll them out into circles of approximately 3 inches in diameter. Put 1/4 Teaspoon Oil and Spread it on the rolled dough.we need to get a triangle shape, to do so do so, first fold into a semi-circle, this fold the semi-circle into half lead. You get a mini triangle shape. Tossing the Triangle on a Little Flour, Roll it out Gently Into A Larger triangle. Continue the same process of rolling with the remains. After a more seconds you will not notice an air pockets popping out. At this point flip the plain paratha and smear about 1/2 a teaspoon of ghee. Using a flat spatula do a light pressure and turning motion to the pudhina paratha.flip to the other side and press and turn in a similar way. You will notes brown spots Around the pudhina parathas and it will be Slightly Crisp. Remove from heat and place on a platter or serve hot.Continue the similar process with the remaining rolled portions and stack the cooked pudhina paratha one above the other and serve hot.Serve the Pudina Tawa Paratha recipe for dinner along with Batata Nu Shaak or Baingan Bharta or any other dry vegetable or vegetables in gravy combination.

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Lamb Lamb Chops, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder Salt To Taste Oil As Required, 2 Tablespoons Ghee, 3 Clooves (Laung), 2 Cardamom (Elachiychi) (Dalchini), 1 Inch Ginger, 12 Cloves Garlic, 2 Green Chillies, 4 Dry Red Chillies
recipe_description: To begin making the mamsaam chops recipe, first wash and dry the mutton chops and half cook them using a pressure cooker along with 1/2 cup water, Salt, Turmeric and Chilli power. Stirring so that they do't burn. Add the par boiled lamb chops along with water and cook for about 2-3 minutes. Let it simmer covered Till the full is full cooked and the gravy turns thick.serv

recipe_description: To start preparation spiced turns with spinach recipe, get all the ingredients handy. In a pan, heat oil. recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups All Purpose Flour (Maida), 2 Teaspoons Active Dry Yeast, 1/4 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt), 2 Teaspoon Sugar, 2 Teaspoons Oil Salt To Taste For Spica Aloo Stuffing, 3 Potatoes (Aloo) Boiled And Mashed, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 2 Green Chillies Finly Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Powder (Dhania) Leaves Fee Amchur (Dry Mango Powder) Salt To Taste For Garnish, 2 Cloves Garlic Finely Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the spicy aloo naan recipe, in a large bowl add the cups of flour all the ingredients for the dout and adding litle water at a time knead to make a firm and smooth dough.grease the do a little And allows it to rest covered in a warm place until it doubles in volume.ONCE For the naan.in a large mixing bowl, add the mashed potatoes and the remaining ingredients and stir well to combine. Check the salt and adjust to suit your taste.take a portion of the naan dough, dust it in flour and roll it into a 2 inch diemeter circle. Place a spoonful of the spicy Aloo Masala and Cover the filling by pulling the dough over it. Dust it with flour and gently roll to make a bigger oval. Brush the stuffed aloo naan with oil and sprinkle garlic on the top. Place it on a green baking sheet. Processed the same way with the remaiing portions of the dough and potato filling.preheat the oven to 200 C for 15 minutes. Bake the spicy Aloo naan in the preheated oven for about 10 minutes until puffed up and golden in color. Serve the spicy aloo naan as an appetizer for your parties or Along with paneer makhani for a weeknight dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) Whisked Salt To Taste For The Coconut Masala, 1/4 Cup Fresh Coconut, 1 Green Chilli, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera), 1./2 Teaspoon Whole Black Pepper Corns, 1 Cup Spinach Leaves (Palak) Finely Chopped For The Tadka, 1 Teaspoon Coconut Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1 Dry Red Chilli Broken
recipe_description: To begin making palak tambuli recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep readyto cook the spinach; Heat oil in a pan over medium heat; Add the spinach along with a little salt and cook until it has wilted down. Once wilted, turn off the heat and allow the spinach to cool.to grind the coconut masala, in a mixer jar, combine the fresh coconut, cooked spinach, Along with Green Chilli, Cumin Sheds and WHOLE POPRCORNS. Add some water to make a smooth coconut masala, set aside. To make the palak tambuli, in a large bowl, combine the whisked curd and the freshly ground coconut-paalak masala and season with salt. For the tadka, heat oil in a tadka pan over medium heat; Add the mustard and cumin seeds, and allow it to crackle. Next add in the red chilli and curry leaves, mix and turn off the flame. Pour this tadka on the palak tambuli, and serve. Serve Palak Tambuli Recipe along with steamed rice and uudupi style bendekayi palya recipe fpor a simple comforting weekday lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Teaspoons Oil, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Cloves Garlic  Grated, 2 Onions  Finely Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 120 Ml Coconut Milk, 1 Cup Vegetable Stock, 2 Tablespoons Dijon Mustard, 2 Carrots (Gajjar ) Cut Into Rounds Or Lengthwise Of, 1 Inch Thickness, 5 To, 6 Green Beans (French Beans) Cut Into, 1 Inch Pies Steamed, 1/4 Cup Green Peas (Matar) Steamed, 2 Potatows (Alo) Boiled And Diced Smalal Salt  To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the Mustard Vegetable Curry Recipe, We will first have the vegetables and keep them ready you can use a steamer or a pressure cooks to cook the vegetables.potatoes. From the beans, carrots and peas.to begin making the curry base; Heat a Teaspoon of Oil in a Pan on Medium Heat. Add in the cumin seeds, garlic and onion and saute on medium heat until the onion has turned light pink and translucent.Once the onions are ready; Add in the turmeric power, chilli power and stir for a more seconds. Finally add in the coconut milk, the vegetable stock and the dijon mustard.add in the salt and check the spice and salt. Adjust to suit your taste.finally stir in the steamed vegetables; Turn the heat to low, cover the pan and simmer the curry 3 to 4 minutes. Turn off the heat and the mustard vegetable curry is ready to be served.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Tindora (Dondakaya/ Kovakkai), 1 Onion  Chopped, 4 Dry Red Chilli, 1 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns, 1 Tamarind, 2 Cups Fresh Coconut  Grated, 3 Cloves Garlic, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste Oil For Cooking
recipe_description: To begin making the mangalorean manoli curry recipe; We will first steam cook the manoli. You can use a pressure cooker or a steamer to cook it. Precooking the manoli (Indian gherkins) makes the cooking process faster and more nutritious too.i like to use a pressure cooker for steaming the Vegetables. To do this, place the cut manoli (Indian gherkins) along with a pin of salt and 2 tablespoons of water into the pressure cooker.cover the pan, place the weight on and allowed Whistles. After a whistle, turn off the heat and you can place the pressure cooker under running water to release the pressure immediatily. Releasing the pressure immediatily, helps retain the fresh green color of the gherkins and also gives it a perfectly cooked texture.click here to watch the video of how to search In a small pan; Roast the dry red chillies, fenugreek, coriander, cumin and black pepper until you can smell the roasted aromas. Add these to a small blender jar, along with the coconut, tamarind and turmeric powder.grind the spices adding very little water to make a smooth curry paste. The manoli curry paste is now ready. Keep aside.in the next step; Heat a teaspoon of oil in a pan; Add the chopped onions, and saute for a couple of minutes until the onions are soft and tender. Add the curry paste and saute for a couple of more minutes.ster in the steamed gherkins, salt and add about 1-1/2 to 2 cups of water. You essentially want a thick gravy for the manoli curry; hence add water accordingly. Give all the ingredients a good stir. Check the salt levels and adjust to suit your taste. Give the mixture a quick boil only when you are ready to serve. Serve the mangalorean manoli curry recipe along with whole wheat lachha paratha and tomato onion cucumber raita for an everything for an everyday meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Raw Banana Boiled And Mashed, 1 Cup Cabbage (Patta Gobi/ Muttaikose) Chopped, 1/2 Cup Gram Flour (Besan), 1 Green Chilli Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Tablesopoons Corps, 2 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Pinch Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Salt To Taste, 1 Cup Oil For Deeep Oil For Deeep Oil For Deeep Oil, 3 Tomatoes Large Tomatoes Pured, 1 Tablespoon Ginger Made Into Paste, 1 Green Chilli Slit, 1/2 Cup Gram Flour (Besan), 3 To, 4 Tablespoons Fresh Cram, 2 Tablespoons Curd (Dahii/Yoghi/Yogiti) (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 To, 2 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 1/2 Teaspoon Cardamom (Ela Teaspoon) Crushed Salt To Taste Oil As Required
recipe_description: To begin making the pata gobi aur kele kofta curry recipe, chop cabbage and press in batches to remove excess water. Collect the dry grated cabbage in a large mixing bowl.cut the raw banana into half and pressure cook with water and salt till it is soft. Allow the pressure to release naturally and peel off the skin. Mash It Completely and Add it to the bowl with the cabbage.into the cabbage and banana mash add besan, green chili, corridar, cumin power, turmeric, garam masala, red chili power and salt. Stir well to make a dry mix.heat oil in a kuzhi paniyaram pan and meanwhile make medium lemon sized kofta balls out of the mix and keep aside in a plate.Ol.Ol.Ol. Till golden brown in color on all sides. Transfer the fried kofta balls on an oil absorbent paper to absorb excess oil.in a separete kadhai Release. Once done add besan and roast it for less seconds and immediatily add the tomato pure in it and stir nicly for about a minute.After a minute add add coriander power, red chilli power, Turmeric POWDERIC POWDERAN Till Oil Starts Leaving from Siddes.Now Add 2 Tablespoons of Curd and Saute for Another minute or so. Once the masala starts smelling good, add fresh cream and mix nicaly.cook for another 2-3 mins and then add water to make the curry. The curry should be a little thick hence before adding water.adjust salt and let the curry come to a boil. Add freshly crushed cardamom, mix well to combine and cover the pan. Simmer the curry for another 5 mins on low heat.after five minutes drop in the fried kofta balls and turn off the heat.transfer the curry in a serving bowl and add a swirl of fresh cream to garnish along .Serve the Pata Gobi Aur Kele Kofta Curry Recipe along with Phulka or Naan with Kachumber Salad Recipe with Cucumber, Onion & Tomatoes by the side.
recipe_name: Pata Gobi Aur Kele Kofta Curry

recipe_ingredients: 2-1/2 Red Yellow Or Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum), 1 Green, 1 Red And, 1/2 Yellow Cubed, 2 Onion Finely Chopped, 2 Tomats Finely Chopped, 2 Green Chillies Chopped, 5 Garlic Chopped, 1 Bay Leaf ( Tej Patta), 1 Teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds Coarsely Pounded, 5 Whole Black Peppercorns, 1-1/2 Tablespoon Cashew Nuts Chopped, 1-1/2 Tablespoon Peanut Butespoon Peanut Buttere Chopped, 1/2 Posoone Tablespoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves)
recipe_description: To begin making capsicum curry, first add 2 tablespoon oil in a pan and saute the cubed capsicum for a lesses. Do not over-fry the capsicum and let it be a bit crispy. Remove them from the pan and keep aside into a separete plate.in the same pan, roast the cashew-unuts and peanuts for about 2 minutes. Switch off the gas and let the nuts cool down. After cooling, powder the roasted nuts by adding them to a grinder. Remove and keep it aside in a separete bowl.heat a kadhai and add 1 tablespoon oil. After the oil gets heated, add bay leaf and coriander seeds and fry them for 1 minute.add chopped onens and fry them till onions turns pinkish. Now add Chopped Garlic and Fry Till the Raw Smell of Garlic Goes Away.Add Chopped Green Chilies and Tomatoes and Fry Them Till the Tomats TOMATOES TURN MUSHY. Switch off the gas.after cooling, add the onion-tomato mixture to the grinder and make a thick gravy by adding 1/4 cup water.Now Again, Heat the Kadhai and Add 1 Teaspoon Oil. Then pour the gravy and saute it for about 5 minutes. Once it is done, add the garam masala and red chilli power.add the fried capsicum and stir well. If the gravy is very thick, add 1/2 cup water and simmer it for about 10-15 minutes.Add salt Burani Raita.

Release the pressure naturally and drain the water out.heat a skillet with oil. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves and let the musted seeds crackle.Once don, add the cooked banana stem and mix everything properly.after a minute, add in the green chili, salt to taste and Give it a Mix. Let it cook for about 2 minutes. After 2 minutes, add in the grated coconut and give it stir fry for about 5 minutes. After 5 minutes, switch off the stove and it is ready to be served.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Arhar Dal, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 10 Curry Leaves Coriander (Dhania) Leaves As Required For Garnishing
recipe_description: To start the preparation of garlic dal, pressure cook the dal well and let it cool.dry roast the green chillies.blend the caked dal, roasted green chillies, garlic, tamarind paste, tamarind paste, salt in a mixer till in dal turn in a pan. Add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Let the curry leaves crackle. Add the blended Dal and Immedited Switch off the Flame. Do not heat the Dal. If the dal cooks then it will lose the raw garlic flavor. Garnish with corridar leaves.serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Rice, 2 Tablespoons Ghee, 2 Bay Leaves (Tej Patta), 2 Cloves (Laung), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 3 Green Chillies Slit, 2 Onens Thinly Sliced ��2 Inch Ginger Finely Chopped, 6 Cloves Garlic Finelly Finely Chopped Salt To Taste, 1/4 Cup Mint Leaves (Pudina) Loosly Packed & Finly Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the spiced onion pulao recipe, wash the rice thoroughly, and drain off all the excess water using a strainer. Now pat dry the rice on an absorbent paper. Heat A Kadai, on Medium-Low Flame, and dry roast the rice for about 3-4 minters, stirring continously. We are not looking for any color here. Turn off the flame and set aside. Heat a pressure cooker with ghee on medium flame, into the cooker add the whole spices - bay leaves, cloves and cinnamon and allow them to sizzle. Once the spices sizzle, add the onions, ginger and garlic and saute until the onions have softenednow add the slit green chillies, chopped tomatoes and saute until the Tomatos are soft and the tomto the trut, mSHCE . Finally add in the dry roasted rice, 2 cups of water, salt to taste and close the pressure cooker. Pressure Cook for 2-3 Whistles and Turn off the Heat. Wait for the cooker to depressurise and then open the lid. Once done, gently fluff with the Spiced Onion Pulao and transfer to a serving bowl.Serve  Spiced Onion Pulao Recipe along with Smoked Dhaba Dal Recipe or Bhune Pyaz Ka Gosht Recipe, Onion Raita Recipe and finish the meal with a dessert of Oats Apple Phirni Recipe .

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Horse Gram Dal (Kllu/Kulith) Soaked Overnight, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Green Chillies Chopped Finely, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 1 Tonion Chopped Finaly, 1 Tomato Chopped Finaly, 1), 1/2 Teaspoon Ginger Garlic Paste, 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Cinnamon Powder (Dalchini), 1/2 Cup Water Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To prepare gahat rasmi badi recipe (uttaranchal kulith koftas in tomato onion gravy), soak gahat dal in water overnight. Drain the Excess Water and Grind them Into a Coarse Paste With Turmeric, Chopped Green Chilies and A Little Salt.Scoop a Tablespoon of Grounded Paste and Roll Into Small Round Balls. Ensure that the batter is firm to do koftas and is not watery. Remove the koftas on it is cooked and keep it aside.heat a medium sized non-picture pan. Add oil to the pan.once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds and let it splutter.stir Until they become mushy.Add Spice Powders and Combine them Well.Now, Add water to the onion-Tomato mixture and cook until they are complete cakeson it with salt. Now add steamed gahat balls to the curry.cook with the Lid covered over medium flame for About 5 minutes 5 minutes until the balls are blended complete with the curry.serve Kulcha.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Curd (Dahi/Yogurt) Whisked, 4 Amla (Nellikai/Gooseberry) (Alma/Nellikai) Grated, 1/4 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 1 Cucumber Peled And Green Chillies Chills, 2 Green Chillies Chopped Fine (Rai/ Kadugu) Salt To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Oil
recipe_description: To begin making the Nellikai Pachadi Recipe, Into a Blender Grind the Raw Gooseberries, Green Chilli and Salt into a Coarse Mixture. Keep aside.in a bowl whisk the yogurt well until smooth. Stir in the ground gooseberry mixture and grated cucumber. Check the salt and spice levels and adjust to suit your taste.Transfer the Nellikai (Amla) Raita into a serving bowl.The next step is to make the seasoning for the Nellikai Pachadi. Heat oil in a small pan; Add the mustard seeds and curry leaves. Allow them to crackle.pour the seasoning over the Nellikai Pachadi (Amla Raita) and stir it in and serve with your choice of Pulao recipe to a wonderful meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cauliflower (Gobi), 1/2 Cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Grated, 4 Potatoes (Aloo) Cooked And Mashed, 3 Tablespoons Corn Flour, 1 Tablespoon Red Chilli Powlespoon Red Chilli Powler, 1 Tablespoon Powderanea) Roasted, 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Salt To Taste For The Curry, 2 Onens Finly Chopped, 4 Tomatoes Pure, 1 Teaspoon Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 1, 1/2 Tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1 Tablespoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powalere Saledesters, 1 Tablespoon Fresh Cream Reserve, 2 Teaspoons For Garnish Water As Required Coriander (Dhania) Leaves As Required For Garnish
recipe_description: To begin making cauliflower paneer kofta curry recipe place big pot on the heat. Add 1 liter of water into the pot and brings it to a boil. Add 1 teaspoon of salt to the boiling water and turn off the heat.next, to chop up the cauliflower, first remove the stem and leafy parts of the cauliflower and immerase it into into the just-boiled, HOT WATER. Allow the cauliflower sit for about 3 to 4 minutes. Then remove it from hot water, and place it on a kitchen towel. Pat it dry completely.Now grate the cauliflower complete and set it aside for a white. After about 10 minutes, cauliflower would have released a lot of water, squeeze it out and discard the water.next, heat a pan and dry roast the grated Cauliflower UNTILS ComPLEFLELS ComPLELY DRILETY DRIYSTES While roasting it, take care not to cook it too much. It should not change to brown, so it is best to roast it on a low flame. Grated paneer to the cooled cauliflower. Add in the mashed potatoes, and all the spices along with corn flour. Knead well until everything is mixed. Make small lemon sized balls of the mix and arrange them on a plate. Set them aside till further use.next, we need to fry the koftas. Warm some oil in a pan, when oil is hot enough, deep fry the koftas until honly golden brown. For an oil-free version, you could also bake them in a pre-heated oven, or use a kuzhi-paniyaram pan to pan-fry them. Eather way, lay the golden brown koftas onto kitchen paper Add the shahi jera and fry for a minute. Next add the finly chopped onions and fry them till translucent. Add the Tomato Puree and Cook It Togetra Until The Pure Thickens and Begins to Leave the sides of the pan.at this stage, add all the spice powders and mix well. Then add water as required and brings the curry to boil.turn the heat down, add the cream, mix well and finally add the fried kofta balls. Simmer on low heat for about 3 to 4 minutes.Turn off the heat, garnish the curry with the reserved cream and fresh coriander leaves.serv Comforting, Yet Special Homely Meal.

Turn off the flame, and allow the pressure to release naturally. Open the cooker on the pressure has released, fluff the narangi paulao with a fork and mix in the Sugar Whotist Its Hot. Once done, transfer the Narangi Pulao to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Narangi Pulao along with Awadhi Khaas Nihari Recipe or Awadhi Style Dal Masoor Musallam Recipe, Boondi Raita Recipe, Pickled Onions, followed by a dessert of Mango Sago Kheer Recipe - Sabudana Kheer with mangoes

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 3/4 Cup Water, 2 Teaspoon Maharashtrian Goda Masala, 20 Grams Tamarind In, 2 Tablespoon Of Water, 1/2 Taspoon Jaggery Salts Leaves For Garnish For Tempening, 1 Teaspoon Oil, 1 Teaspoon Ghee, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu)
recipe_description: To begin making the maharashtrian amti recipe, first rinse dal in water thoroughly. Add ¾ cup water, turmeric powder and place it in pressure cooker to cook on a high heat. When you hear a whistle, turn the heat down and allow 2 More Whistles to Haappen. Turn the heat off now and allow the pressure from the cooker to release gradually on its own. Mash the Dal with the back of a ladle and mix it well to make a uniform, well cooked texture. Set it aside for use later.place a kadai on the heat, add the mashed cooked Dal to it and allow it to simmer on a low heat. At this stage, if the Dal is too thick, add water to get the desired consistency. Once Dal Starts to Boil, Add Goda Masala, Jaggery and Tamarind Juice. Stir Well and Let it Cook on Low Heat for 10 minutes or until it is uniformly cured.in a small tadka pan heat oil and ghee togetra. Add mustard seeds and allow them to crackle. Add cumin seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, green chilies and sauté for a minute for a minute for the teaming to the dal and switch off the flame. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serving hot with fresh steamed rice and a dollop of ghee.

recipe_ingredients: 8 Pomfret Fish Can Substitute With Any Fish Except Shellfish And Mackerels), 3/4 Cup Fresh Coconut Freshly Grated, 6 Dry Red Chillies Preferably Byadgi, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 3 Whole Black Pepper Tamarind
recipe_description: To begin making the paplet hooman recipe, wash pomfret pieces well with water 3-4 times and add some salt.mix and keep it aside for about 15-20 minutes. Grind together, ingredients mentioned for paste including fresh coconut, dry red chillies, turmeric powder, coriander powder, whole black peppercorns, tamarind using 1 cup water, to fine paste.In a medium saucepan kept on medium high heat, add coconut oil and fry Onions Till they are nicly darkened.you can substitute with olive oil or any oil, but try to use coconut oil for lovely aroma.pour in the prepared coconut paste paste in browned Runny. We Prefer Little Thicker Curry Runny.Let It Come to Complete Boil and Add Pompano Pieces. Mix gently.cover and cook on medium heat for about 6 to 7 minutes. Remove cover and add salt.Gently mix and simmer on low heat for 5 more minutes. Check the fish if cooked properly using toothpick.they should feel soft. Do not overcook, otherwise, fish pieces will break and release bones in the curry. Serve hot.serv

recipe_ingredients: 3/4 Cup Masoor Dal (Whole), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste Ingredients To Be Ground To Paste, 1/3 Fresh Coconut Grated, 20 Grams Tamarind, 3 Dry Red Chillies, 2 Teaspoons Coriander (Dhania) Seeds Ingredients For Seasoning, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 3 Cloves Garlic
recipe_description: To begin making the masoor dal gassi recipe, we will first cook the Dal in the pressure cooker. Place the Dal in the Pressure Cooker, Add Salt, Turmeric Powder and 2-1/2 Cups of Water. Cover the cooker, place the weight on and pressure cook for a couple of whistles.after a couple of whistles, turn the heat to low and simmer for about 4 minutes and turn off the heat. Allow the pressure cooker to rest until the pressure releases naturally. After the Dal is cooked keep it aside.our next step is to make the masala for the gassi. In a small frying pan, dry roast the coriander seeds and red chillies until Fragrant. Once roasted, allows it to cool.Once cooled, place the roasted coriander, red chillies, coconut, tamarind and garlic into a blender. Add a little warm water and grind to make a smooth gassi masala paste.add this masala, to the cooked Dal mixture and brings the Dal to a brishk boil for about 3 to 4 minutes. Adjust the consistency of the moong dal gassi, but adding a little water, as the mixture can thicken when the masala is added. The Gassi is traditional is not in a liquid form but, if of a thick gravy consistency.check the salt and spice levels and adjust to suit your taste. Transfer the masoor dal gassi to a serving bowl.The final step is to prepare the seasoning for the masoor dal gassi. Heat a small tadka pan on medium heat; Add the mustard seeds and allow it to crackle. Add the garlic, red chillies and curry leaves and roast for a couple of minutes.pour the seasoning over the masoor dal gassi and serve.serve the masoor dal gassi along

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Phool Makhana (Lotus Seeds), 1-1/2 Cups Green Peas (Matar) Blanched, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 5 Clooves (Laung) Roughly Pounded, 1 Tomato Finly Chopped, 1 Green Chilli Finly Chopped Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/4 Teaspoon Cumin Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/4 Teaspoon Fenna Fenelieds Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1/4 Teaspoon Kalonji (Onion Nigella Seeds), 2 Teaspoon Corridar (Dhania) Leaves Finaly Chopped For Garnish, 2 Teaspoon Oil Or As Required Salt To Taste To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the bihari makhna aur matar sabzi recipe, blanch the green peas in hot water and keep aside.dry roast the makhana on medium heat for 5 minutes and keeep aside.heat oil in the Same Pan, Add the Pan PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PON PAN , Mustard, Fenugreek, Fennel and Nigella Seeds and Saute Till the Spices within Fragrant.add the Onens and Saute Until Un TurnsLucent.roughly pounded the Garlic Cloves Using A MOTAR and PANTALIC Saute Till the raw smell of the garlic goes away.add the tomatoes, a pinch of salt and saute until the tomatoes turns Turn Mushy. Add the roasted makhana and mix well.cook bihari makhna aur matar sabzi for another 3 minutes, switch off the heat. Remove bihari makhna aur matar sabzi into a serving bowl and garnish with the coriander leaves.serv

recipe_description: To begin making the palak kichdi recipe, in a pressure cooker add rice, dal, broccoli, peas, turmeric, one pinch of hing, litle oil and 2 -1/2 cups of water and pressure cook.in a saucepa A Boil and Add the Cleaned Spinach leaves and let it sit for a minute in the pot whose why the flame is still on. Once it is cool grind it to a paste in a mixer grinder and set aside.in a wide pan, heat some oil and add bay leaf, jera, chopped garlic, pin of hing, GREEN Chillies, Green Chillies, Chillies, Chopped Onenses HINTINS Started to change color to this mixture. Some Water If Necessary and Season with Salt.also Add the Garam Masala If Using At This Stage. recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Basmati Rice, 2 Cup Water, 2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 1 Bay Leaves (Tej Patta), 2 Whole Black Peppercorns, 2 Tablespoon Ghee, 1 Tablespoon Oilspoon Oil, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 1 -Passpoonsospoon Oilspoon Oilspoon Oilspoon Oilspoon Oil, 2 Tablespoon Garlic Paste, 1 Cup Sweet Corn Boiled, 6-7 Baby Corn Sliced, 1/4 Tablespoon Corridar Powder (Dhania), 1/4 Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1/2 Tablespoon Powlespon Powlesoon) Tablespoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/2 Cup Homemade Tomato Puree, 4 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Chopped Salt Taste, 3-4 Tablespoon Fresh Cram
recipe_description: To begin making the corn paulao recipe, take rice cooker or sauce pan, add rice and water.then, add 1 tablespoon ghee, clooves, cynamon, cardamom, bay leaf, pepepercorn, Salt and boil till the rice Drain if there is any excess water remain after boiling. Keep the rice aside.heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add cumin seeds to the oil and when cumin seeds start to splutter, add the onion and saute it till it turns golden brown. Now, add ginger-garlic paste, boiled corn, baby corn and salt. Let it cook for 2 minutes.Now, add the dry spices, start with cumin seeds powder, coriander power, red chilli power, turmeric power and Garam masala power. Let it cook for about 2 minutes. Sprinkle one tablespoon ghee on top and fresh coriander.cover it with aluminum foil and keep it in a preheated oven at 180 degree for about 10 minutes. Once done, take it out and it is ready to be served. It is also a convenient dish to prepare when you have a lot of guests at home.

recipe_ingredients: 8 Rohu Fish Or Katla Or Any Carp, 100 Grams Curd (Dahi/Yogurt), 2 Tablespoons Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Teaspoon Ginger Pastesoon /4 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar, 2 Teaspoons Mustard Oil Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the kasuri methi machine recipe, first wash and marinate the fish pieces with turmeric, lemon juice and salt for 15-20 minutes. This is our first marination.Drain out the excess marination. Beat the yogurt very well adding salt and sugar. Now add garlic paste, ginger paste and fish pieces. Marinete Again for another 15 minutes.heat oil in a kadai or a heavy bottomed pan. Now add half of kasuri methi. Make a Quick Stir.Now Add the Marinated Fish With All Marination. Cover and Cook Till the Fish Pieces are done. It will take Around 8-10 Minutes.Sprinkle Garam Masala Powder. Mix well. Crush the rest of the kasuri methi between your palms and sprinkle on the cooked fish curry. Remove from Fire and Let it rest for another 5 minutes.serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Green Peas (Matar), 3 Onions Ground Into A Paste, 5 Cloves Garlic Finely Chopped, 2 Green Chillies, 1-1/2 Teaspoon Ginger Finely Chopped, 2 Tomatoes Pureed, 1/2 Teaspoooon Red Chailly, 1 Tearandoron ), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Sal To Taste, 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Fineli Chopped, 6 Cashews, 1/2 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 2 Cloves (Laung), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 2 Tablespoons Oil, 1 Tablespoon Butter (Saltad)
recipe_description: To begin making restaurant style green peas masala, we have to first cook the peas. Pressure Cook The Green Peas Along With 2 Tablespoons Water for 1 Whistle. Release the pressure of the cooker immediatily. Keep the cooked peas aside. Meanwhile, soak cashew nuts in warm water for 15 minutes. Once the cashews has softtened, add them to a mixer and grind them along with curd to make a smooth paste.in a heavy bottomed pan, heat oil on medium flame. On the oil is hot, reduce it to a low flame, add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, cardamom and cloves.Once the aroma of the spices start wafing in the air, Add Chopped Garlic & SAUTE UNATIL The RAW SAMELLL Goes. This will take about a minute or two.next, add onion paste, Tomato puree and saute until until the raw smell of onlines goes away. This will take about 5 minutes. Then add all the dry spice powders including Turmeric Powder, Cumin Powder, Coriander Powder, Garam Masala Powder, Red Chilli Powder and Salt to Taste.MIX Them Well and Add 2 TableSpoons of Water to the MIKE Spices are well cooked, add required water to get the required consistency of your choice. Boil for 3 to 4 minutes.next, Add the Cashew-Curd Mixture (Paste) and Stir Continuous for the Proper Mixing of ingredients.Now, Add the Cooked Peas and Let the Green Peas Masala COOOK ON A Low Flame for 5 Minutes.finally , Switch off the gas and add a dollop of butter and chopped coriander leaves on top.Serve restaurant style green peas masala with phulkas or tawa paratha Along with a Cauliflower and Cucumber Raita Forryday.

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Chicken With Bone, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Power, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste Oil For The Masala, 2 Onions Roughly Chopped, 2 Tablespoons Fresh Coconut Grated, 4 Cloves Garlic, 1 Inch Ginger Chopped, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 4 Cloves (Laung), 4 Cloves
recipe_description: To begin making the karnataka style chicken saaru recipe, we will first make the masala for the saaru. Heat a small skillet on Medium-Low Flame, Add the poppy seeds and dry roast them. Once the poppy seeds are roasted, add in the cloves, cinnamon and dry red chilli and roast them well. Turn off the flame and keep it aside.heat another pan on medium flame, Add a tablespoon of oil, add ginger, garlic, onions and saute until the onions Turns Turn TransLuCent.add Grated Coconut and SAUTLLILILILILILILILILILILITION Turn off the flame.in a mixer-jar, combine the dry Roasted Spices along with Ginger-Garlic-Onion Mix, grind to a smooth paste along with Along with some water. Transfer to a bowl and set aside. Heat Oil in the Pressure Cooker Over Medium Heat, Add the Chicken, Turmeric Powder, Salt and Red Chilli Powder. Roast the chicken in the pan for about 5 minutes 5 minutes until the chicken begins to get a roasted texture and is getting partially cooked. After 5 minutes of roasting the chicken, add the ground saaru masala (Onion Coconut & Spices Paste) And Give it a stir. Check for Salt and Add 1 Cup of Water and Pressure Cook for About 3 to 4 Whistles Until The Chicken is Cooked and Turn off the heat.allow the pressure to release. Open the cooker and check for spices and salt and adjust accordingly.serv

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Banana Stem Cut Into Small Diced And Soaked In Butter Milk, 1 Cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali), 1/2 Cup Pearl Onions (Sambar Onions) Finly Chopped, 3 Cloves, 3 Cloves Garlic Cup Tomato Cup Tomato Smashed, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Sambar Powder, 1 Teaspoon Sugar, 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta) Torn, 1 Sprn, 1 Sprn, 1 Sprrn, 1 Torrog Cusponsored) Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Teaspoon Oil Salt To Taste For Temperature, 1 Teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split), 1 Teaspoon Chana Dal
recipe_description: We begin making the Andhra style davva aava koora recipe (Sweet and Spicy Banana stem peanut curry recipe) Smashed garlic, add the small onions and saute till it is translucent.Once by you can add the chopped tomatoes and saute till it is smashed. Add all the spice powders and sugar give it a toss.finally add the banana stem and peanuts inside the pressure cooker with 1 cup water for about 2 whistle. Release the pressure naturally.in order to Temper, heat a small skillet and splutter urad dal, channa dal and dry red chilli and add it to the gravy and give it a stir. Serve the Andhra style davva aava koora recipe (Sweet and Spicy Banana Stem Peanut Curry Recipe) With Steam Rice or with Chapathi to enjoy your lunch meal.

recipe_ingredients: 20 Wonton Wraps (3 � Square) Oil For Deep Frying, 1/4 Cup Crab Meat, 1/4 Teaspoon Ginger Grated, 1 Tablespoon Onion Finly Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Carrot (Gajjar) Gajjar Gajjar Gjjjar Critande  Softened, 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar, 1/4 Teaspoon Soy Sauce
recipe_description: To begin making the crispy crab rangoons, mix all ingredients mentioned under 'for filling' until they are well combined in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate at least for 1 hour, up to overnight.Take one of the wonton wraps and put a teaspoon full of this filing in the center. Using some water, moisten the center of each side.bring toge to the adjacent sides and then all togeether into the middle.heat oil in a pan to boiling temperature. Fry Each of these rangoons bot the sides, till crispy.lay then down on a paper towel to drain excess heat and serve immediatily.Serve crispy crubuns along

recipe_ingredients: 3 Tablespoons Butter (Unsalted), 2 Inch Ginger  Finely Chopped, 8 Cloves Garlic  Finely Chopped, 2 Onions  Thinly Sliced ��3 Brown Cardamom (Badi Elaichi), 1 Cup Homemade Tomato Puree, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Powder Salt To Taste, 1 Teaspoon Jaggery, 1/4 Cup Fresh Cream, 200 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Cut Into Cubes, 1/4 Cubles, 1/4, 1/4 Cups Water
recipe_description: To begin making the paneer butter masala biryani recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep ready.in a large biryani pot or a cooking pan, heat butter medium heat, add the ginger, garlic and forests. Allow the onions Turn Soft and Golden Brown.stir in the Badi Elaichi, Tomato Puree, Turmic Powder, Red Chilli Powder, Coriander Powder, Garam Masala Powder, Jaggery and Salt. Mix well and brings the tomato gravy to a brisk boil. When the gravy begins to boil, add in the paneer pieces and fresh cream. Mix Well, and ENSURE That You are left with a thick semi coating masala on the paneer.ster. Cups of water and allows the paneer butter masala biryani to come to a boil. Once it comes to a boil, turn the heat to low, cover the pan cook until all the water is absorbed.Onece all the water is absorbed, turn off the heat and allow the biryani to rest for 10 minutes Gently Stir more Pudina Leaves and Serve the Paneer butter Masala Biryani Warm Along with Raita.Serv Finish this Simple Yet Delicious Meal With Kesar Shahi Tukda with Rabri Recipe (Saffron Bread Pudding with Spiced Milk Custard)

recipe_ingredients: 300 Gram Fish, 3 Teaspoon Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Onion Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Ginger Paste, 2 Tomatoes Chopped, 3 Cloves Garlic Finely Chopped, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmon Tureric Powderic Powderc), 1/4 Teaspoon Vinegar, 1/4 Lemon Juice, 50 Ml Coconut Milk Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the fish molee recipe, first wash the fish cutlet pieces and season with salt. In a heavy bottomed pan, heat some oil on medium flame. Add cumin seeds and let it crackle. Add chopped onion and saute until the onions are translucent. Add garlic, ginger paste and saute until the raw smell of garlic and ginger goes away.add in the chopped tomatoes and saute until until the tomatoes are Mushy and Oil Starts to Leave from the sides. This will take 3 to 4 minutes.after 3 to4 minutes in turmeric power and red chili power, mix well and pour a cup of water into the pan. Once the water reactions the boiling point, lower the flame, add coconut milk and the fish pieces.when the fish is full cooked, add limb juice and Vinegar. Add appropriate amount of salt and guive the curry a good stir. Turn off the flame.serv You can also make it for your weekend lunch with other kerala recipes.

recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Liter Milk Full Fat, 70 Grams Thin Semiya (Seviyan), 1/2 Teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi), 1/3 Cup Gulkand Safron Strands A Less, 6 Pistachios Chopped Rose Water Chopped Rose Water For Garnish
recipe_description: To begin making the gulkand seviyan kheer recipe in a saucepan brings the milk to a rolling boil. Once the milk has boiled, Reduce the flame, and allow the milk to simmer on low flame, until it reduces to half its quantity. This process will take a good 20 minutes. ENSURE The Milk is stirred obcastionally to prevent the bottom of the pan burning the milk. It is also always a good idea to keep a ladle inside the milk container. Once the milk is reduced, add the cardamom powder, and a less strands of saffron. Next, to this add the gulkand and mix vigorously until it melts and comes togeether along with the milk. Adjust the Sweetness of the Gulkand Kheer, Based on the Sweetness of the Gulkand, if required Add Sugar to Suit Your Taste. Since the gulkand is alredy packed with the rose flavor, depending on how strong or mild you prefer, add the rose water, according to how Aromatic you like the gulkand kheer to be. Finally, add in the thin seviyan and wait until the seviyan wilts down and become soft. Turn off the heat, transfer the gulkand kheer to a serving bowl and garnish with some crushed pistachios and dry rose petals. Serve Gulkand Seviyan Kheer as Dessert or Part of A Meal That Consists of Ajwain Puri Recipe, and Aloo Tamatari Recipe for a lovely comforting Sunday Brunch.

recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Cup Kaddu (Parangikai/Pumpkin) Cubed, 1/2 Cup Green Peas (Matar) Cooked, 2 Tablespoon Fresh Coconut Grated, 2 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Power Power) Tablespoon Coconut Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Teaspoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 5 Curry Leaves, 2 Cups Water Salt As Per Taste As Per Taste
recipe_description: To begin making pumpkin peas palya recipe, take a vessel and boil water. Now add pumpkin cubes and cook for 5 to 10 minutes or until they are half cooked. Strain water and keep this aside.heat a heavy walk, add oil. Now add mustard seeds and saute well until it splutters.Now Add Chana Dal, Fry Until they Turn Golden. Add curry leaves, fry for less seconds.Now add the cooked pumpkin cubes and cooked peas. Add amchur powder, garam masala and red chili powder, mix well. Let it cook for about a minute. Close and Cook for 3-5 minutes. Serve Pumpkin Peas Palya Along With Tomato Onion Sambar and Steamed Rice for a Weekday Meal. You can also serve curry leaves buttermilk with this meal.

recipe_ingredients: 5 Small Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Teaspoons Garam Masala Powder, 2 Teaspoons Red Chilli Powder, 2 Teaspoons Jaggery, 1 Tablespoon Tamarind Paste, 2 Tomatoes  Finely Chopped Salt  To Taste To Be Ground Into A Paste, 2 Tablespoons Black Sesame Seeds, 2 Tablespoons Raw Peanuts (Moongphali), 2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Inch Ginger, 6 Cloves Garlic, 2 Onions Sliced ��2 Green Chillies To Temper To Temper, 1 Tablespoon Gingelly Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Pinch Asafoetida (Hing), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves
recipe_description: To begin making the chettinad kathirikai chops, wash and cut the brinjals into wedges and soak it a bowl of salted water. In a small pan on medium flame, dry roast the cumin seeds, black sesame seeds and peanuts until fragrant. Turn off the flame and allow it to cool.in a mixer jar, grind the roasted cumin seeds, sesame seeds, peanuts, ginger, garlic, green chilies and onions into a fine paste using litle wait. Set aside.moving ahead, in a heavy bottomed pan, heat oil on medium flame, add the Mustard seeds and let them crackleadd asafoetida, and curry leaves. Saute for a more minutes until the curry leaves stop crackling. Add the Ground Masala, Turmeric Powder, Garam Masala Powder, Red Chilli Powder and Salt. Mix well. Add the drained brinjal wedges, tomatoes, covers and cook on low flame for 2 minutes.Open the Lid, Add the Jaggery, Tamarind Pulp, Half a Cup of Water and Bring to a BOIL. Adjust water to the required consistency. Cover and Cook on low flame for 12-15 minutes, until the brinjals are complete cured and begin to become mushy. Turn off the flame. Serve Chettinad kathirikai chops along with hyderabadi biryani with basmati rice (mutton & chicken) or Ambur Star Chicken Biryani Recipe and Sweet & Spicy Coriander Tadka Raita for a Complete Biryania meal.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Tomatoes, 1 Tablespoon Kabuli Chana (White Chickpeas) Boiled, 1 Cup Chana Stock (Water In Which Channa Was Boiled), 1-1/2 Teaspoons Lemon Juice, 1/4 Teaspoon Juice Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 2 Teaspoons Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finely Chopped For Tempening, 1 Teaspoon Ghee, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 1 Clove Garlic Finely Chopped, 5-6 Curry Leaves For Fresh Rasam Masala, 5-6 Curry Leaves, 1/2 Teaspoon Whole Black Pepper Corns, 1-1/2 Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Dry Red Chillies
recipe_description: To begin with kabuli chana rasam, grind tomatoes to a paste and dilute it with 2 cups of water.add turmeric power, hing and salt to this, transfer to a saucepan and brings to a Rolling BOILING TILLING TILLG Fresh Rasam Masala, in a mixer-jar combine black peppercorns, cumin seeds, curry leaves and Dared Red Chillies. Grind to a Coarse Masala. Mash coarsely the boiled channel and add the freshly ground rasam masala, boiled channel and the 1 cup of Chana stock and boil until the rasam froths up. Heat One Teaspoon of Ghee in a Tadka Pan, Add Mustard Seeds and Allow it to Splutter. , Once it splutters, add finly chopped garlic pieces and curry leaves. Pour this temperature over the boiling rasam. Turn off the flame. ServE Channa Rasam with Steamed Rice With a Dollop of Ghee and Elai Vadam, Chow Chow Pasiparuppu Poriyal Recipe (Chayote Squash and Mohog Stir Fry) and Pudina buttermilk recipe.

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Green Chawli Beans (Yard Long Beans/Karamani/Barbati) Finely Chopped, 1 Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 4 Cloves Garlic Finally Chopped, 2 Tomatoes Finely Chopped, 1 Tespoon Redlindes, 4 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 2 Teaspoons Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder)
recipe_description: To begin making the north Indian chawli ki sabzi recipe, chop the beans and pressure cook along with a little salt and 2 tablespoons waters and 2 tablespoons waters and turn of the heat. Release the pressure immediatily to maintain the fresh green colors of the beans.heat oil in a kadai/ wok; Add the ajwain and allow it to crackle. Add the garlic and saute for a more seconds.add the tomatoes, turmeric, red chilli, coriander, amchur powders and saute until the tomatoes build mushy. Finally add the cooked green chawli, jaggery add more salt required and stir well to comb Ki sabzi recipe along with phulka, dhaba style dal fry and yogurt to make a healthy lunch or dinner

recipe_ingredients: 1 Yellow Bell Pepper (Capsicum)  Diced, 1 Red Bell Pepper (Capsicum)  Diced, 1 Tablespoon Groundnut Powder, 2 Teaspoons Sesame Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Maharashtrian Goda Masala  Optional, 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/4 Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1 Teaspoon Tamarind Paste, 1-1/2 Teaspoon Jaggery Grated, 2 Teaspoons Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Coreeds As Required For Garnishing
recipe_description: To begin making the bell pepper sabji recipe, keep all the ingredients prepaped and ready. First heat oil in a kadhai/wok.add the Mustard seeds and on they splutter add the cumin seeds.add in the colored bell peps and saute till it softns. Add hing, turmeric power, red chilli power and Goda masala (if using) and mix well.add salt and cook covered for 1 to 2 minutes. recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Which Wheat Flour, 1 Teaspoon Salt Ghee To Layer And Cooking Parathas
recipe_description: To begin making the whole wheat lachha paratha recipe we will first knead to make the dough.in a large mixing bowl, add the whole wheat flour and salt. Mix well. Add a tablespoon of oil and rub it into the flour until you see coarse crumbs. Add a little water at a time and knead well to make a smooth and soft paratha dough. Cover the dough and allows it to rest for 10 minutes.after 10 minutes, knead for a minute for again until the paratha dough is soft and pliable. Divide the dough into 10 equal portions. Preheat a Skillet on Medium Heat. If you are using a cast iron skillet, then green the skillet well and allow it to preheat.keep some flour aside for dusting while rolling the dough. Shape Each Dough Porting into a round ball, press it between the palm of your hands.dust the dough in flour and roll it into a 6 inch diameter circle. Smear some ghee or oil on this rolled out dough. Dust some flour over the ghee. This flour and ghee will help the lachha paratha split its layers.fold the lachha paratha dogh like an accordion, simlar to a saree pleat. Start from one end of the dough and record the end. The dough is essentially made into a pleat. Once you have stacked up all the pleats one over the other, roll the dough into a swiss roll. Flatten the pleated swiss roll between the palm of your hands, dust in flour and roll into a 4 to 5 inch diameter circle. You will be able to see the layers very cleverly.place the rolled latha lachha paratha on the preheated skilelet. Cook on medium heat on both sides for a few seconds, then smear ghee and cook until golden brown and cooked through completely.Proceed the same way with the remaining portions of the paratha dough.Serve the Whole Wheat Lachha Paratha along with Matar Paneer  or Smoked Dal Makhani Dhaba Style and Carrot Cucumber Tomato Salad with Lemon and Corrider for a Weeknight Dinner or even for breakfast along with Kesar elaichi aam chunda pickle and HMMEMADE YOGURT.

recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Cup All Purpose Flour (Maida), 2 Tablespoons Ghee Salt Pinch Water As Required For Stuffing, 1 Cup Desssicated Coconut, 1/3 Cup Sugar Powdered, 1/4 Cup Sooji (Semolina/Rava)
recipe_description: To begin making the baked modak and karanji recipe, first make the dough by mixing togeether maida, ghee and pinch of salt. Knead it Well, Cover it with Wet Cloth and Keep it covered for 10 minutes. The next step is to make the stuffing. On a skillet, melt ghee and fry sooji until fragrant. Add coconut and fry for Around a minute a minute or until it slightly changes its color.add SUGAR, Mix and Cook on Low Flame for Around a minute. Add dry fruits and cardamom powder. Mix Everything Well and Cook for Another Minute and Switch off the Stove. Keep the mixture for cooling.Now let's make karanji and modak. First Make Small Balls out of Dough. Size of a golf ball.roll it by one by one with rolling pin to make thin discs.keep these discs covered with moist cloth or moist kitchen paper towel to keep them moist karanji, Place a SPOON FULLLING ATILING Each disc and fold the circle in a half-moon shape and with the help of forms, press the folded side to make nice design.for making modak, the same process but Hold the DISC on your left Palm in the shape of a bowl and Just place the filling in the center of the disc and merge all the sides of the discre in the shape of a fresh fig.after you make all the karanjis and modaks ready, let's bake them.place them.place them. Greased aluminum foil (green with little ghee). Brush Each Karanji and Modak with Ghee to Give them a Nice Glaze after they are baked. After 20 minutes, remove and keep on the cooling Rack.Once cooled down complete, store in the air tight control. Serve baked modak and karanji along with some snacks such as moong dal mini samosas and chana chaat to your guests or family members.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Fresh Coconut, 1 Teaspoon White Dal (Split), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Corrider (Dhania) Seeds, 6 Dry Red Chillies, 15 Grams Tamarind, 1/2 Teaspoondorindederind, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 2 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu), 2 Dry Red Chillies Broken For The Vegetables, 1 Cup Green Peas (Matar), 2 Potatoes (Aloo) Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the alugadde batani gasi recipe - potatoes and peas gassi, prep all the ingredients and keep aside.into a pressure cooker, add the green peas along with a little salt and shop of win. Pressure Cook for 2 Whistles and Turn off the Heat. Release the pressure immediatily to retain the fresh green colors of the peas. Keep aside.to cook the potatoes, cut the potatoes into half and add it into the pressure cooker. Add half cup of water and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles and turn off the heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Once pressure has released and the potatoes have cooled peel the potatoes and cut into cubes. Keep aside.to make the freshly ground coconut masala, heat a pan over medium heat. Add the urad dal and dry roast until it become golden brown. Add the cumin seeds, coriander seeds and dry red chillies and roast them for a more seconds until the seeds start cracked.turn off the heat and allow the Roasted Ingredients to Cool.add the ROASTEDDD DAL, COMINE SEEDES, COMINS, COMINS Along with the coconut, tamarind, turmeric power, jaggery and half cup of warm water. Blend to make a smooth paste. Keep aside.heat oil in a pan; Add the mustard seeds, dry red chillies and allow the seeds to crackle. Stir in the curry leaves and give it a stir.add the ground coconut masala, the cooked peas and potatoes along with 1/2 cup of water and stirll to combine. The gasi is usually thick, so you could adjust the consistency by adding more water if you like. Give the gasi a brisk boil for 3 to 4 minutes and turn off the heat. Check salt and adjust according to taste and serve hot. Serve Batani Gassi Recipe (Uudupi Style Potatoes and Peas Curry) With Steamed Rice, Uudupi Style Southekayi Palya Recipe for a Complete Meal.

recipe_description: To begin making the lauki chana dal sabzi recipe, soak the chana dal in 2-1/2 cups of water for 3 to 4 hours.fired cook the channa in the pressure cooker. Place the Soaked Chana Dal in the Pressure Cooker Aug Water Such that the water is at least 2 inches Above the Dal.Sprinkle some salt and a pinch of Turmeric power. Pressure Cook for 3 to 4 Whistles About 15 Minutes and Turn off the Heat and Allow The Pressure to Release Naturally.Next step is to cook the lauki in the pressi in the press. Heat A Little Ghee Over Medium Heat, Add the Bay Leaf, Ginger, Green Chilli, Tomato, Turmic Powder, Red Chilli Powder, Garam Masala Powder, Salt, Jaggery and Give it A Stir.Sauts SOFTEOS Add the peled and chopped lauki/ bottle gourd along with 1/4 cup of water and Give it a stir. recipe_description: To begin making the Thakkali vengaya sambar recipe, wash toor dal and soak in water for 10 minutes. Transfer Soaked Toor Dal to a Pressure Cooker, Add 2 Cups of Water.Pressure Cook The Dal For 3-4 Whistles and Let the Pressure Release Naturally.Open the Lid, Scoop Out the COOKED DALED DAL ANTOE With the help of a potato masher mash the dal and keep aSide. In a Same Pressure Cooker, Add the Onions and Tomatoes Along with Tamarind Extract, Sambar Powder, Salt and 1/4 Cup of Water. Close the Lid and Pressure for two whistles over the medium heat. Open the Lid Once the Steam is released.If you are not using the pressure cooker method - heat oil in a saucepan, sauute the smalls onions onens onens until tinder and the snake aroma is released.Add.dddi and 1 /4 cup of water and simmer until the tomatoes are tender for 10 minutes. Now mix the cooked to the onion tamarind sambar curry. Stir to Combine and Transfer to a Large Sahucepan.Meanwhile, to Temper The Tomato Onion Sambar, Heat Oil in A Small Pan, Add Mustard Seeds.ONCE MUSTEDS SEEDS Start Crackling ASAFOETIDA POWDER, CRICKLIG POWDERE Saute Till the Curry Leaves Turn Crips. Turn off the heat and pour the seasoning into the sambar.place the tomato onion sambar over medium-haigh heat and brings the sambar to a boil. Turn the heat to low and allow the sambar to simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring in between. If the tomato onion sambar is too thick, add a little water to get the right consistency and simmer. Taste the seasoning and adjust to suit your taste.garnish the Tomato onion sambar with chopped corridar leaves and serve hot. recipe_description: To make the bhaat na muthiya dhokla recipe, we will first prepare the steamer by filling it with water and green the steamers. Powder, Coriander Leaves, Bottle Gourd and Oil for the Muthias and Mash Well Using a Masher or with Your Hands. recipe_description: To begin maharashtrian kairyichi amti recipe, we will first prep all the ingredients and keep ready.to grind the fresh coconut masala; In a mixer jar, combine the grated coconut along with the dry red chillies, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, garlic, ginger, and methi seeds. Grind this mixture into a fin paste adding 1/2 cup of warm water. Set Aside. To make the maharashtrian kairyichi amti recipe, heat a pan with oil on medium heat. Add the Mustard seeds and allow it to crackle.sty in the curry leaves, the raw mango and a pinch of salt. Add @ cup of water and cover the pan. Cook the mangoes in the water until soft. This will take about 4 to 5 minutes the mangoes are cooked, stir in the coconut masala and simmer for another 5 minutes until the amti has come togethr and is well cooked. Check the salt and adjust to taste accordingly. Turn off the heat and serve hot.serv
