recipe_description: To begin making the beetroot, Aloo & Amaranth Leaves Sabzi Recipe, Pressure Cook The Baby Potatoes in Water for 2 Whistle. Let the pressure release naturally and then open and peel the potatoes. Keep aside.heat a kadai and add some oil. On the oil is hot add musteds, hing, cumin seeds and allow it to splutter for lesser for seconds. After the seeds have splutted add the garlic, green chillies and onions and saute till onions are soft.Once the onions have softly softened add the baby potatoes and roast for two two Minutes.Now Adds, Hilader, COLDERE PILDER to combine. Once the masalas are well combined into the potatoes add the grated beetroot, salt to taste and stir. Add in the amaranth leaves and combine everything well. Close the Pan with a Lid and Cook Till the Leaves and Beetroot Are Nearly Done. Open the Lid and Stir in the Garam Masala and Cook Till the Beetroot and Amaranth Leaves are cooked.Turn off the heat and transfer the beetroot, Aloo & Amaranth Leaves Sabzi Into A Serving Owl.serve the beetroot, aloo and amranth leves sabzi recipe With Phulka, Green Moong Dal Makhani and Sweet Potato & Green Bean Salad by the side to make it a rich and delicious meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Yellow Moong Dal (Split), 1/2 Cup Masoor Dal (Whole), 250 Grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) Chopped And Steamed, 1 Tomato Finly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Grated, 1 Teaspoon Turmon Turderic Powderic Powder Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Salt To Taste Ingredients For Seasoning, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Dry Red Chillies
recipe_description: To begin making the Dal Palak Recipe we will first cook the lentils (dal) using a pressure cooker using 3 cups of water. See video recipe of how to cook lentils using pressure cooker.next we will cook the spinach with the spices. There are two ways you can do it - in a sauce pan by sauteing it or in a pressure cooker.below you will find the recipe of how you cook the spinach in a pressure cooks, as the method is really fast. If you are using a saucepan method, then follow the same procedure, but just covers the pan and cook until the spinach is soft.into a pressure cooker, add a teaspoon of oil; Add the ginger, green chillies, tomatoes, turmeric power, cumin power, garam masala power, salt and spinach. Stir all the ingredients togeether just to combine them. Once Combined, Cover the Pressure Cooker, Place the Weight on. Pressure Cook Until You Hear One Whistle. After the first whistle, turn off the heat. We will now place the pressure cooker under cold running water to release the pressure immediatily. This way, we will release the pressure qiqly and the spinach does not get over-cured and remain fresh and green.Now that you have the spinach and lented and lentils ready, if your cookier is concerned, added dal inch and give in It a stir. Bring the Dal Palak to a Quick Boil. Our next step is to proceed to make the seasoning. Into a small saucepan, heat a tablespoon of ghee. Add in the cumin seeds and dry red chillies. Roast them until they crackle and sizzle.add this seasoning to the Dal Palak and Give it a stir. Serve the Dal Palak for a simple and healthy weeknight dinner along with steamed rice or paneer pulav or traditional tawa parathas. Also, do make sure to slice up some onions, squeeze in some lemon and toss it along with salt and serve it with the Dal Palak. The combination is delicious.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Green Chillies Finely Chopped Sal To Taste For The Dough:, 2 Cups Whole Wheat Flour, 1 Teaspoon Salt Extra Virgin Olive Oil Or Ghee To Smear While Cooking
recipe_description: To begin making the stuffed mooli paratha heat olive oil in a wide pan.add the radish, salt and saute on high heat till all the moisture from radish evaporates.turn of the heat and add the stuffing and add the stuffing for the stuffing and add the stuffing .Allow the stuffing mixture to cool down.mix all the ingredients for the dout in a mixing bowl, add Little water at a time and knead to make a smooth dough. Divide the dough into 8 portions.dust a flat working surface with some flour. Roll a portion into a ball and flatn it using your fingers and roll it out into a 3 inch diameter circle.place about 2 tablespoons of the stuffing in the center, gather the edge and seal it. Carefully roll it out into a circle about 6-7 inches in diameter.heat a skillet over medium heat and cook the mooli paratha in olive oil until cook caked through, and has golden spots on the aids.repe And the mooli paratha filling.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Cups Basmati Rice, 1 Onion, 1 Green Chilli, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera), 1 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Cloves (Dalchini), 2 Cloves (Laung), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/4 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 2 Teaspoon Oil Salt As Required For The Tikka, 200 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Cubed Into, 1 Inch Thick Pieces, 2 Tablespoons Curd (Dahi/Yogurt), 3 Teaspoons Gram Flour (Besan), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powdermalidermalidermaharmahasposaharmahaspon Pone Powdersospon Powderson Fenugreek Leaves), 1 Tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste Coconut Oil As Required
recipe_description: To begin making paneer tikka pulao, we will wash and soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. The next step is to make the marinade for the paneer. In a large bowl, Combine Curd, Gram Flour, Ginger Garlic Paste, Red Chilli Powder, Garam Masala Powder and Little Salt.MIX EVERYTHIN SOGETHER SOGETHER INTO THICK PASTE. Add paneer cubes to the paste and mix well so that the paste is well coated on each paneer cube. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.heat a griddle and add two teaspoons cooking oil, place the paneer cubes and fry until slightly golden on all sides. Remove from Tawa and Keep Aside.The Next Step is to make the Pulao. Heat a Teaspoon of Oil in a Pressure Cooker and Add Cumin Seeds, Cinnamon, Cloves, Cardamom and Bay Leaf. Saute for a minute.add chopped onion and slitted green chilli. Saute until the onions Turn Soft and Translucent.Once the onions Turn Soft, Add the Paneer Tikkas, Turmeriic Powder, Garam Masala Powder and Mix Lightly Whitahouts The Paneere Cubes. Drain the soaked basmati rice from water and add to the pressure cooker. Saute for a minute until the masalas coat on to the rice.add 3 cups of water along with required Amount of Salt. Mix well and brings the water to a boil.close the cooker and pressure cook for a whistle. Once the pressure releases naturally, gently fluff the rice with a form. Garnish with coriander leaves.serv

recipe_ingredients: 3 Karela (Bitter Gourd/ Pavakkai) For The Bharwa Karela Stuffing, 1/2 Cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Grated, 2 Potatoes (Aloo) Boiled And Mashed Sal To Taste, 1/2 Teaspoon Black Pepper Powder, 1 Spriger (Dhania) Leaves Chopped, 1 Spriger, 1 Teaspoon Oil For The Bharwa Karela Masala Gravy, 1 Teaspoon Oil, 2 Onions Finly Chopped, 1 Cup Homemade Tomato Puree, 1 Inch Ginger Chopped, 4 Clives Garlic Chopped, 1 Green Chilli Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Poopon Poseronia) Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Pinch Sugar, 2 Tablespoons Fresh Cream Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the bharwa karela makhani recipe, wash the karela well, and very lightly peel of the skin, allow the green of the karela to be there, as it is important for health.Cuts. Gently scoop out the flesh and seeds from inside the karela, in a way that you should be left with hollow cylinders of the karela pieces. In a pressure cooker, add the karela pieces and two tablespoons of water and pressure cook for one whistle. Turn off the heat and allows the pressure to release naturally. Drain the water if any and transfer the karela into a bowl and set aside. *For the Bharwa Karela Stuffingin a Mixing Bowl, Combine the grated Paneer, Mashed Potatoes, Salt and Pepper, Chopped Coriander and Mint Leaves. Mix well. Stuff Karela Stuffing Mixture Genearously into the Karela Cylinders. Heat a pan with oil on medium flame, place the bharwa karela in it and cook until slightly Roasted Turning Turning them Around from time to time to time. This will take about 5-7 minutes. Once the bharwa karela have got a nice brown color, turn off thee flame and set aside. *For the bharwa karela makhani gravyin a saucepan heat oil on medium flame, add the chopped ginger garlic and saute for a couple of minutes. Next add in the onions and saute till they turn into a light brown color. This will take about 6-8 minutes. Once the Onion-Ginger-Garlic is Well Cooked, Add in the Green Chilies and Tomato Puree, Cover and Cook for 5 Minutes. After 5 minutes, open and add in all the dry masalas- Red Chilli Powder, Coriander Powder, Jeera Powder, Garam Masala Powder, Sugar and Salt. Bring the bharwa karela makhani gravy to a single boil. Turn down the flame, mix in the cream. Once the cream is well combined, turn off the flame. In a serving plate arrange the bharwa karela and then pour the makhani gravy over it. recipe_description: To begin with goan mushroom xacuti, prep all the ingredients and keep ready.into a pan; Add red chilies, Poppy seeds, clooves, cinnamon sticks, peppercorns, cumin seeds, corridar seeds, fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds and roast them for about 3- 5 minutes on the low flame and arsh. Remove the mixture from the heat and set aside to cool.add the roasted spices, tamarind and onion-colleconut mixture into the small jar of the mixer grinder and blend to make a smoth passing little water. The next step is to make the xacuti masala. Heat oil in a flat bottomed pan. Add the ground masala paste and saute for a few minutes. Add mushroom pieces, turmeric power, salt to taste and give it a stir for a more seconds. Add just a little water to give the goan mushroom xacuti a gravy like Consistency and cover the pan.simmer the goan mushroom xacuti masala for 3 to 4 minutes and turns off the heat. Mushrooms Get Coory Quickly and Also Release a Lot of Water.ONCE DONE, TURN OFF The HEAT and Stir in the Coriander Leaves - It brings in a freshness to the goan mushroom xacuti. Serve the goan mushroom xacuti along with butted PUV puns or even a matar paulao for lunch or dinner.

recipe_description: To begin the preparation of Parsi Salli MURGH, WASH and Clean Chicken nicely under running water and keep aside.heat oil in a pressure cooker on Medium heat, Add the bay leaf, clooves, cardamom, Cardamom, Carinnamon, Cinnamon, Cinnamon Chopped onions and saute till they attain a deep brown color. Now add the dry spice powders- chicken, turmeric powder, garam masala powder, red chilli powder, salt and mix well. At this stage, add the nutmeg and cardamom power, add 1 cup of water and close the pressure cooker. Pressure Cook for 2 Whistles and Turn off the Flame. Allow the pressure to release naturally. recipe_description: To begin making the chunni ki puri/ dal & besan puri recipe, wash and soak the moong dal for 20 minutes. Heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally.Onece the pressure has released, mash dal with a whisk or churner, and allow it to cool. There will be very light water remains which you can use to make the poori dout. Add a little water if required and knead into a firm dough. Add a teaspoon of oil on the top and knead for a lesser more minutes until smooth. Cover with a muslin cloth and set aside.in a heavy bottomed pan, add required Amount of oil for fry for frying the dal & besan puri.dvide the dal & besan puri dout Equal portions, and dust the surface white white Dough balls with the rolling pin. Roll them out into a 2-1/2 to 3 inch diameter circle.Ol is heated, gently SLICE In the Dal & Besan Puri.using a slotted spoon, keep pouring oil over the puri and gently press the puri, so that The chunni ki puri puffs up. At this point, turns the puri to the other side and fry till it turns golden brown.drain the dal & besan puri on a kitchen towel to absorb the extra oil and serve hot. Serve Chunni Ki Puri/Dal & Besan Puri Along with Jodhpuri Aloo Recipe or Rawal Pindi Style Chole Recipe and Boondi Raita for a Weekday Meal.

recipe_description: To begin making Seppakizhangu Roast (South Indian Style Arbi Fry) Recipe, take the Arbi and Pressure Cook Them With Enough Water for 1 Whistle. Switch off the heat and release the pressure, this will avoid the overcooking of arbi. Now peel the skin of the arbi, cut each of them into 2-3 pieces and keep it aside.Take a big bowl, add all the dry ingredients except oil and mix well.Now add the arbi mix Vegetable with Spices. Marinete the Arbi/Colocasia in this mix for 20 minutes for 20 minutes for a pan on medium heat, add the marinated arbi and give it a gentle stir.let the arbi cook and get roasted in medium Heat, Occasionally Givetle Stir. Serve the Seppakizhangu Roast (South Indian Style Arbi Fry) Recipe with Steamed Rice and Rasam. This also makes a great combination with hot phulkas or chapatis.

recipe_description: To prepare the methi masoor dal recipe, first wash and soak the masoor dal in 3 cups of water for at least 30 minutes. While the Dal is soaked clean, wash and pat dry the fenugreek leaves.next, finly chop the methi leaves. Heat a teaspoon of oil in a wok or a kadai and add the chopped methi leaves into the wok.saute the methi leaves, until it gets softened. Methi Leaves Cooks very quickly and this will take less than a minute to cook. Once the Leaves has Softened, Turn off the Heat. Keep it aside.in the next step, we will cook the masoor dal with the spices. Heat Ghee in a Pressure Cooker Over Medium Heat. Add asafoetida and cumin seeds and allow it to crackle.next add onion and saute till they turn soft. Once the onions have softened, add the chopped tomato, green chilli along with coriander power, turmeric power, red chilli power and saute for a minute or so So Over Meedium Heat. Close the Lid of the pressure cooker and put the weight on.cook masoor dal for 3 whistles. After a couple of whistles, turns the heat to low and simmer the Dal for about 5 minutes. After 5 minutes, turn off the heat. Allow the pressure to releases naturally from the pressure cooker.Once the pressure has released, open the cooker and lightly mash the masoor dal with the back the back of a spoon. Stir in the Sautéed Methi Leaves and Serve.serv

recipe_ingredients: 250 Grams Jackfruit Raw (Kathal) Mustard Oil For Frying For Gravy, 3 Onions, 2 Tomatoes  Boiled And Pureed, 5 Cloves Garlic, 1/2 Inch Ginger, 1 Tablespoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 4 Cloves (Laung), 1 Tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Tablespoon Curd ( Dahi / Yogurt) Salt To Taste For Stuffing, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 1 Teaspoon Mustard Oil, 1 Teaspoon Garlic Butter, 50 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Finly Chopped Black Pepper Powder To Taste Bak, 1/2 Cup Mozzarella Cheese Grated
recipe_description: To begin making the stuffed kathal, let us first cut the raw jackfruit. In order to do that we have to first, green our hands and the knife that we are going to use, with some oil.this important tip enSURERS That The Hands Do's Stick Sticky from The Natural Fruit Latex/Sap Preuse Does not get onto your hands and makes the cutting process hassle-free. Keep a bowl of water mixed with some salt, ready to put the cut raw jackfruit pieces to avoid discoloration of the fruit.now that your hands and knife are well green, cut the jackfruit in half, funds Pieces and soak them in the salted water.place the jackfruit in a pressure cooker with 1/4 cup of water and pressure cook for 1 whistle and turns of the heat.allow the pressure to release. Drain any water from the jackfruit and set aside.heat mustard oil in a pan on medium heat, shallow fry until crisp. This will take about 5-7 minutes. Drain on an absorbent paper and set aside. In a pan, heat garlic butter on low heat, add fennel seeds and allow it to sizzle, next add the onions and fry till soft, for about 3 minutes. Next add paneer and fry well. Turn off the flame. Stuffing for the stuffed kathal is ready.Take kathal pies and stuff the jackfruits with the onion-paneer stuffing. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the stuffed kathal, give it a quick mix and let it rest for an hour while the flavors infuse. Meanwhile, to prepare the gravy.heat oil in a pan, on medium heat, add onions and saute for a couple of minutes. Once thee onions are transparent, add tomatoes and give it a good mix, cover and cook until mushy, this will take about 3-4 minutes. Turn off the flame. Once the onion-Tomato cools down, grind it in a mixer to a coarse paste. Heat A Kadai on Medium Flame, Transfer The CoarSe Tomato-Onion Mixture and Add Chilli Powder, Garam Masala and Turmeric Powder, Curd and Saute Till The Masala Is Cook is COLL and COMESED Well and Comes to the COCORDITHES to Done Pour this thick masala in an out safe bowl. Put in the stuffed Kathal/jackfruit.Sprinkle grated mozzarella and bake in the preheated oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 10-15 minutes.Garnish with spring onions and serve Stuffed Kathal  with hot Laccha paratha/tawa paratha recipe for a weeknight dinner.

recipe_description: To begin making the pumpkin tawa paratha recipe, we will first press the pumpkin till it is complete cooked, this will take about 4 to 5 to 5 whistle and once Down allow the presence The flour, salt, mashed pumpkin, a tablespoon of oil and knead adding little water at a time to make firm and smooth dough. Add one tablespoon of oil to coat the dough and knead again. Cover and set aside for 15 minutes.knead once and divide the dough into 8 portions. Preheat the Iron Skillet on Medium Heat.roll the paratha dough portions into balls and flatn them with the palm of your hand. Toss is on flour and roll them out into circles of approximately 3 inches in diameter. Put 1/4 Teaspoon Oil and Spread it on the rolled dough.we need to get a triangle shape, to do so do so, first fold into a semi-circle, this fold the semi-circle into half lead. You get a mini triangle shape. Tossing the Triangle on a Little Flour, Roll it out Gently Into A Larger triangle. Continue the same process of rolling with the remains. After a more seconds you will not notice an air pockets popping out. At this point flip the plain paratha and smear about 1/2 a teaspoon of ghee. Using a flat spatula do a light pressing and turning motion to the pumpkin paratha.flip to the other side and press and turn in a similar way. You will notes brown spots Around the pumpkin parathas and it will be Slightly Crisp. Remove from heat and place on a platter or serve hot.Continue the similar process with the remaining rolled portions and stack the cooked pumpkin paratha one above the other and serve hot.Serve the Pumpkin Chapathi Recipe along with Dahi Bhindi Recipe or Carrot and Beans Thoran (Subzi recipe) for your everyday lunch ideas.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Rice Salt If Required, 2 Cups Water
recipe_description: *Method to cook rice in pressure cooker method the rice well under running water. Place one cup of washed rice along with two cups of water in the pan of the pressure cooker.keep the weight on, cover the pressure cooker. Cook the rice in the pressure cooker on medium high heat and after the first whistle turn the heat to low. Once you turns the heat to low, Simmer the rice for 3 minutes. After 3 minutes, turn off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally. Rice takes a longer time to cook, hence wait for the pressure to release complete and then open the cooker and use as desired. In a heavy-bottomed saucepan with a light-fitting Lid, Combine 2 cups water, washed rice and salt (optional) .bring the mixture to a boil over high heat. As water begins to boil turns the heat to low, cover the pan with a Lid and simmer the rice mixture for 12 to 15 minutes or until it's caked and all the water is absorbed.Tued.TURN off the heat to In the pan for at least another 10 minutes. Before serving, fluff the rice with a form and serving.

recipe_ingredients: 5 Karela (Bitter Gourd/Pavakkai), 2 Sweet Potatoes Boiled, 2 Tomatoes Finely Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Finely Chopped, 3 Green Chillies Finally Chopped, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haspoon Turmerc Powder) Powder, 1 Teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder Salt As Needed, 2 Tablespoons Mustard Oil Or More To Cook
recipe_description: To begin making the bharwa karela with sweet potato filling recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep ready.lightly peel the skin karelas/bitter gourd, cut into 1 inch cylders and skop outs Sprinkle salt and turmeric to remove the bitterness. Let it rest for 20-25 minutes.to prepare the sweet potato masala. Pressure Cook the Peled Sweet Potato in a Pressure Cooker With 1 Cup of Water and Cook for 3 Whistles. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Once cooled, peel the skin and eater grate or mash the potato.heat a skillet with oil over medium heat; Add ginger and allow it to soften for more than. Add chopped tomatoes and green chilies and sprinkle with salt. Allow the Tomatoes Turn Mushy.Add Turmeric Powder, Red Chili Powder, Chat Masala, Garam Masala and Salt. MIX Everything to be Incorprated Well.cook for at Least 2 Minutes Until the Flavors Come Through. Check the salt and spices and adjust to suit your taste. Now fill the karelas with potato masala filling tightly.heat a wide pan with the oil; Place the bharwa karela with sweet potato filling and pan fry the karela until it is cooked and has brown spots.cover the pan and cook, so the steam helps it cook faster. Once done, you can sprinkle a little more chaat masala and serve hot.serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cauliflower (Gobi) Cut Into Small Florets And Blanched, 1/2 Cup Green Peas (Matar), 1 Carrot (Gajjar) Cubed And Blanched, 1 Bay Leaves (Tej Patta), 3 Cloves (Laung), 3 Clung, 1/2 Tespoon Coming Seeds (Jeera), 3 Cloves Garlic Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Onions Chopped, 2 Tomatoes Chopped, 2 Tablespoons Cashew Nuts, 2 Tablespoons Whole Almonds (Badam) Blanched, 1/2 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yoghi / Yoghhi / Yogurt) ) Leaves For Garnish
recipe_description: We begin making the subz noor e chashm recipe (Nawabi curry recipe) by making the paste for the curry, heat a non stick pan and cracked Add onions and saute till it is translucent and golden brown.later on Add the Chopped Tomatoes, Cook Till It Becomes Mushy and Soft. Add in the nuts and saute for 2 more minutes. Remove it from the flame and let it cool so you can blend it into a smooth paste using a blender.ONCE it is done mix it with curd and combine it honestly.next is to make the curry, etc. Oil, add in the cumin seeds, ginger and garlic. Saute Till it is brown. Add all the vegetables and saute for about 10 minutes. Add the spice powders that are listed and cook against for 4 more minutes for 4 more minutes. Check for Seasoning, According to Your Taste.SERVE The Subz Noor E Chashm Recipe (Nawabi Curry Recipe) Along with whole wheat lachha paratha to enjoy the awadhi meal.

recipe_ingredients: 6 Soya Chaap, 1 Tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste, 1/2 Cup Curd (Dahi/Yogurt), 1 Red Bell Pepper (Capsicum), 1/4 Cup Cheese Greated, 4 Green Chillies, 1 Teaspoon Powon, 1 Teaspoon Powder, 1 Tandoi Masala Masala Masala, 1 Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Fresh (Chopped) Salt To Taste Oil As Required
recipe_description: To begin making the tandoori style soya chaap curry recipe, first remove the sticks from soya chaap and dice them small circular pieces.next, take a bowl and add diced soya chaap, ginger chaap, ginger chap, and salut toge. Use your hands to rub the mixture on soya chaap. Let it marinate for 15 minutes.meanwhile, take the red pepper and keep it directly on gas (fire) for roasting. Roast Till It Gets charred from all the sides. Let it cool down for lesses. Use your hands to remove the burnt skin from the pepper. Grind the Roasted Pepper & Green Chillies together.Now take the marinated soya chaaps and add red pepper paste, whisked yogurt, grated cheese and all the spices into it. Give it a good stir so that soya chunks are properly coated. Cover the bowl with a cling film & leave it for another 15 minutes of marination. Once marinated, then heat up oil in pan and first add the soya chunks in order to fry them. Cook them for 7-8 minutes and then add the left over marinade into the pan. Cook on low heat till the gravy starts thickening.adjust salt as per your taste and turn off the heat. Garnish it with some fresh coriander leaves and servor hot.serv

recipe_ingredients: 10 Badi Hari Mirch (Large Green Chilli) Also Known As Bajji Chilli, 1 Tablespoon Mustard Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 2 Tablespoons Gram Flour (Besan), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli, 1 ), 1 Teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder), 2 Teaspoons Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Teaspoon Lemon Juice, 1/4 Cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali)  Crushed, 1 Tablespoon Jaggery Salt  As Required, 6 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves  Finely Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the mirch ki sabzi recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.split the chillies in half and remove the seeds and cut into 1-1/2 inch piecesheat oil in a wok/kadai and on Ajwain and allows it to crackle for a less seconds. Stir in the Green Chillies and Sprinkle a Little salt and give it a stir.turn the heat to low, cover the pan and simmer for a couple of minutes until the chilli begins to sweeat a little and starts relationship. Once the chillies is releasing moisture, add the masalas -Red Chilli Powder, Turmic Powder, Amchoor, Garam Masala, Coriander, Besan and Salt. Give it a stir.cover the pan again and eccap stirring the mirch ki sabzi until it is cooked. Once the green chillies are cooked, add pound peanuts, a little jaggery and optionally lemon juice to give the mirch ki sabzi that extra tanginess and mix well.Oll.Oll.once the mirch ki sabzi well tossed in the masala. Ki sabzi into a serving bowl. Stir in the Chopped Corrider Leaves and Serve Hot.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Jackfruit Raw (Kathal), 2 Black Cardamom (Badi Elaichi), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini) ), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Salt To Taste Sugar To Taste, 2 Sprig Bay Leaves (Tej Patta), 1 Tablespoon Gheee Ghee Ghee Ghee Ghee Ghee Ghee, 2Bles Mus Took
recipe_description: We begin making the Bengali style raw jackfruit curry recipe, let us first cut the raw jackfruit. In order to do that we have to first, green our hands and the knife that we are going to use, with some oil.this important tip enSURERS That The Hands Do's Stick Sticky from The Natural Fruit Latex/Sap Preuse Does not get onto your hands and makes the cutting process hassle-free. In a bowl, whisk togetra curd along with water to make the buttermilk. Keep a bowl of buttermilk aside to drop in the chopped jackfruit now that your hands and knife are well green, cut the jackfruit in half, discard the skin and start making 1 Inch Pieces and Soak Them in the buttermik. Drain and transfer the raw jackfruit into a bowl. To marinate the jackfruit, to the jackfruit bowl, add turmeric power and mustard oil and give it a good mix. Marinate it for about 20 minutes 20 minutes.heat oil in a pan on medium heat, add the cinnamon sticks, bay leaf and cardamom.Once it begins to sizzle, add half of the Sliced ​​on the Ginger and Garlic, FRIY TILILIC, FRIY TILILIUD Once done cool it down and grind it into a smooth paste and keep it aside.heat a pressure cooker on medium heat, add oil and fry the rest of the onions Till it is Golden Brown. Then add the marinated jackfruit and fry it for 5 minutes. Keep cooking the jackfruit in the masala till the oil comes to the surface.add about 1/2 cup of water and mix well. Close the pressure cooker and pressure cook the raw jackfruit curry for 2 whistle till it becomes tender.allow the pressure to release naturally and check for seasoning. Serve the Bengali style raw jack fruit curry recipe along with ragi tawa paratha and kala chana salad recipe with carrots, cucumber & tomatoes and tomatoes and beetroot carot raita recipe for a Complety This meal is also diabetic friendly.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 1 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi) For Sambar, 100 Grams Red Amaranth Leaves  Finely Chopped, 100 Grams Spinach Leaves (Palak)  Finely Chopped, 100 Grams Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves)  Finely Chopped, 30 Grams Tamarind  Soaked In Warm Water, 1 Cup Pearl Onions (Sambar Onions)  Halved, 1 Teaspoon Jaggery  Grated/Powder, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Salt To Taste For Roasted Masala, 1 Tablespoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 1 Tablespoon White Urad Dal (Split), 2 Tablespoons Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 1 Tablespoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns, 1/2 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 4 Dry Red Chillies, 1/2 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated For Seasoning/ Tadka, 1 Teaspoon Gingelly Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Dry Red Chilli, 1 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1/4 Teaspoon Assafoetida (Hing)
recipe_description: To begin making huli soppu saaru recipe first prep all the ingredients and keep them ready. First step, wash the toor dal and add it into the pressure cooker along with 2 -½ cups of water and turmeric powder. Cover the cooker and pressure cook for 2 whistles.after 2 Whistles Turn the Heat to Low and Simmer For 3 to 4 Minutes and Turn of the Heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure releases, open the cooker and mash the dal lightly until smooth.next, soak the tamarind in hot water for 15 minutes. After 15 minutes, Using your fingers, mash the tamarind well into the water until it feels complete.this process help to extract the juice and sourness from the tamarind. Do this process another Couple of Times by Adding 1/4 cup of water at a time. Combine all the tamarind water into a single bowl and set aside.for the masala, preheat a pan over medium heat; Add the Chana Dal and Urad Dal and Roast Until the Dals Turn Golden and Lightly Crisp. At this stage, add the coriander seeds, cumin seeds, methi seeds and dry red chillies.stir for another minute or two until you notice the seeds the seeds start crackling and you can smell the aromas comeing third. -40 seconds and turn off the heat. Allow the mixture to cool. Once cooled, blend to make a fin powder. Keep aside.to cook the sambar add the tamarind water, chopped greens, onions, ground sambar powder, turmeric power and salt. Add 1/4 cup of water and pressure cook for 2 whistles turns off the heat and release the pressure immediatily. Release the pressure immediatily by lifting the weight off with a fork. Releasing the pressure immediatively ensures the greens retain its bright colors. Keep aside.finally to put the saaru togeether, heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat; Add the mustard seeds, cumin and red chillies and allow the seeds to crackle. Add the curry leaves and asafoetida. nature in the cooked dal and the cooked soppu saaru into the seasoning. Give the huli soppu saaru a stir and brings the huli soppu saaru to a brisk boil for 2 to 3 minutes and turn off the heat. Check the salt and adjust according to your taste. Transfer the Huli Soppu Saaru to a Serving Bowl. ServE Huli Soppu Saaru Recipe Along with Steamed Rice, Uudupi Tomato Rasam Recipe, Aralu Sandigige Recipe (Puffed Paddy Fryums) and Karnataka style beetroot palya recipe for a delythin mealy.

recipe_ingredients: 250 Grams Spinach Leaves (Palak), 1/2 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 4 Garlic Finly Chopped, 2 Fresh Red Chillies Chopped Sal Chopped Sal As Required, 1/2 Teasporered Chilliod /4 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera)
recipe_description: To begin making the Dry Palak Dal Sabzi, wash and finely chop the spinach and keep it aside.Cook toor dal with half cup of water and make sure water should be in 1:1 ratio and make sure that it is half cooked and not mushy .Heat a wok/kadhai and add oil, cumin seeds, red chillies and garlic cloves.let the cumin seeds splutter and then add chopped onion and stir for a with.add spinach, Turmeric power and Stir Well. Add salt and stir against 5 to 6 minutes.when spinach gets cooked, Add toor dal and stir well for 5 minutes.after 5 minutes, Add Red Chilli Powder and Stir Well. When Dal Gets Cooked, Add Garam Masala and Stir for Few Minutes and Switch off the Flame.Serve Dry Dry Palak Dal Sabzi with Steamed Rice, Phulka or Whole What Lachha Paratha.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Govind Bhog Rice Cleane Washed, 20 Cashew Nuts, 20 Sultana Raisins, 2 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 2 Cloves (Laung), 2 Bay Leaves (Tej Patang), 2 Bay Leaves (Tej Patta), 2 Thalspons Teaspoons Ginger Grated, 1 Tablespoon Sugar, 2 Tablespoons Ghee, 1 Tablespoon Oil
recipe_description: To begin making bengali style basanti pulao recipe, smear the gobindobhog rice with turmeric and keep aside for 20 minutes for the rice to absorb the gorgeous yellow.heat a Tablespoon Each of Gheepe On low heat, fry the cashewnuts and raisins till they turn golden brown, keep aside.in the same oil, on low medium heat, add the bay leaves, crushed cinnamon, crushed cinnamon, green cardamom, clooves, sauts till the toes .Add the ginger paste and saute for a minute or two; Just about till till the raw smell of ginger vanishes.Now add the rice and give a gentle stir, Taking care not to break the grains. Saute Gently for 3-5 Minutes Over a Medium Flame.Add 4 cups of warm water (Golden rule - rice and water should always be in a 1: 2 ratio), add sugar and salt to taste.throd Raisins. Cover and Cook Till almost all the water has been absorbed, by time the rice should be almost don.switch off the flame, add the remaining ghee, cover and allow to rest for a further 10-12 minutes. The basanti pulao is ready.serv To end the meal on a sweet note serve it with rasgulla recipe.

recipe_ingredients: 100 Grams Horse Gram Dal (Kllu, 5 For The Kuzhambu, 5 Cloves Garlic, 2 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns Salt  According To Your Taste, 1/2 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Cups Tamarind Water, 3 Teaspoons Gingelly Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu)
recipe_description: To begin making the kollu puli kuzhambu, soak humse gram dal in a sufficient Amount of water for a couple of hours. After 2 hours, pressure cook horse gram along with 1 chopped tomato, salt and the required water.Pressure Cook for 4 Whistles, Turn the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes. Turn off the heat, allow the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure has released naturally, open the pressure cooker and drain the house gram and set aSide. Reserve the water for the gravy. Place a heavy bottomed pan on low heat and add 1 teaspoon oil. Once the oil is hot, add peppercorns, cumin seeds, coriander seeds and 5 dry red chillies. Roast all the ingredients for 4 to 6 minutes, until you can smell the aroma of the spices. Once they are roasted, add few curry leaves, peled shallots, grated coconut, garlic and continue to saute till the onions become soft. Add in the cooked Horse Gram with Tomato. Stir all the ingredients well and turn off the flame. Allow the ingredients to cool and grind to chutney consistency with The process of making this to a puli kuzhambu starts from here. This chutney is the base for the pli kuzhambu. To make the pli kuzhambu, in a bowl, add the prepared tamarind water, the ground kollu tomato chutney along with the water it was cooked in. The kuzhambu should be thin.slice the remaiing shallots and break the remaining chillies into pieces. In an earthenware pot, add 2 teaspoon of oil and add the shallots, broken red chillies, turmeric powder and asafoetida.alternatively if you do not have an earthenware pot, use can make it in a saucepan. Once the onion turns pink, add the tamarind Horse gram kuzhambu mixture and simmer for 15 minutes till the gravy thickens and raw smell of tamarind goes. Meanwhile in a tadka or temperature pan, add a teaspoon of oil. Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds. Add 2 finly chopped shallots and fry till they are golden brown. Now add the curry leaves and turn off the tadka pan.pour the temperature over the kollu puli kuzhambu and turn off the heat. Your Tangy Kollu Puli Kuzhambu is ready to be served. Serve Kollu Puli Kuzhambu along with hot steamed rice, carrot poriyal and elai vadam for a comforting week

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Boneless Chicken, 3 Tablespoon Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal), 1 Tablespoon Rice, 3 Tablespoon Broken Wheat (Dalia/ Dalia/ Godumai Rava), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 4 Onets Sliced ��1 Teasponspones Sliced, 2 Grilceder Grilced Tablespoons Red Chilli Powder Salt To Taste, 2 Tablespoon Mint Leaves (Pudina) Chopped, 2 Tablespoon Corrider (Dhania) Leaves Chopped, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 3 Tablespoon Ghee For The Masala, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 1 Tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns, 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 3 Cardamom (Dalchini), 3 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds, 2 Badamos, 2 Badamos
recipe_description: To prepare MUGHLAI Style Chicken Haleem Recipe, Dry Roast All The Spices Mentioned Under Masala List. Grind it into fin powder. Keep aside.soak chana dal, broken wheat, rice separely.in a saucepot add oil. Add Sliced ​​Onions, Ginger Garlic Paste, Green Chilies Sauté Until Onens Turn Brown. Remove from Stove and Keep Aside.Add 3 cups water into the same pan. Add chicken pieces, salt and red chili powder. Cover and brings it to rolling boil.Once chicken changes color, add the soaked dal, broken wheat and rice. Cover and Cook. Once the chicken is cooked, remove the bay leaf.start mashing the chicken and dal once chicken gets cooked. Continue mashing and cooking until all the chicken is full mashed along with dal. Keep adding a little water as and when required.Now add the sauated onion-ginger garlic-green chilies mix mixed with roasted spices. Add salt to taste and continue to cook and mash.Once the haleem turns thick and all ingredients are well mashed, add ghee and mix against the MUGHLAI Style Chicken Haleem Recipe to serving to serving ghee on top. Serve MUGHLAI Style Chicken Haleem Recipe Topped With Caramelized Onens and Serve as a Main Course Meal With Mutton Kema Samosa Recipe.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Raw Banana, 2 Potatoes (Aloo) Boiled, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Teaspoon Panch Phoran Masala, 2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder For The Gravy, 1 Tomato, 1 Inch Ginger Grated, 2 Green Chillies Finely Chopped, 2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Sugar, 1 Teaspoon Sugar, 1 Teaspoon Sugar Powder, 2 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 2 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Chopped
recipe_description: We begin making the Bengali style kanch kolar kofta curry recipe (Green plantain kofta curry recipe) The Tomatoes Till it is soft and mushy. Add salt and sprinkle little sugar to reduce the tanginess. Then let it cool down and grind the Tomato into a Pure Consigncy.Once the potatoes and raw bananas are cooked, smash it along with the masala powders and make ito a ball. PAN Fry them on the Kuzi Paniyaram Pan Till it is brown honestly. Keep it aside.heat a kadai with oil, add bay leaf and crackle cumin seeds, then add the tomato pure, add in all the spice powders mentioned in the gravy list. Add 1 cup of water and let it boil for about 15 minutes. Serve the bengali style kanch kolar kofta curry recipe (Green Plantain Kofta Curry Recipe) with Hot Phulkas and Steam Rice.

recipe_ingredients: 250 Grams Green Beans (French Beans) Cut Into, 1/2 Inch Pieces Or As Desired, 1 Onion Sliced, 2 Green Chillies Slit, 3 Tablespoon Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teason Seeds Jera), 8 Curry Leaves, 2 Kashmiri Dry Red Chillies Broken, 3 Tablespoon Fresh Coconut Grated, 1 Teaspoon Salt Sugar A Pinch
recipe_description: To begin making the goan style french beans foogath recipe, wash the freench beans, remove the string and chop them into 1/2 inch piece.heat oil in a heavy bottomed paan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves, dry red chillies and green chillies. Saute for About 1 to 2 Minutes.After 1 to 2 Minutes, Add Onions. Saute for 3 to 4 Minutes Till onion Turns Translucent. Then add beans, salt and a pin of sugar. Saute.add Around 1/4 cup of water, cover and cook for 8 to 10 minutes. Don't over cook to maintain the crunching.when beans is cooked, finally add grated coconut, toss and switch off. Goan Style French Beans Foogath is Ready to RILISH.

recipe_ingredients: 250 Grams Colocasia Root (Arbi), 2 Tablespoon Gram Flour (Besan), 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Halmeric), 1/2 Teasponmagponmagponm Power, 1 Teaspoon Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 1/4 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jera), 1 Green Chilli Finaly Chopped, 2 Teaspoons Lemon Juice, 1 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Choppeder
recipe_description: To begin making the besan arbi roast recipe, scrub the arbi to get rid of mud and dirt. Pressure cook the arbi with little water and a pinch of salt for 1 whistle (do not over cook). After the first whistle, turn off the heat. Once the pressure releases, open the cooker. Peel the skin off and cut them into half lengthwise and place it in a large mixing bowl.in a wide plate mixan, red chili power, coriander power, coriander power, salt, turmers power, amchur power, Amchur POWDER POWDER The Turmeric Powder, Coriander Powder, Red Chilli Powder, Amchur Powder and Cumin Powder to the Cut Arbi and Toss to Coat Well. Heat 2 Tablespoons of Oil in a Heavy Bottomed Pan, Add the Besan Arbi in Small Batches and Roast Until Until Golden Brown in Color on All Sides. Do this in 2 to 3 batches, add oil as the pan gets dry. Drain the roasted arbi on kitchen towel and place it in a serving platter.in another pan add 1 teaspoon of oil. Add cumin seeds, carom seeds and finly chopped green chilies and saute for 10 seconds. Add this to the besan arbi and toss gently. Sprinkle some corridor leaves and lemon juice on top of the besan arbi and serve.serv

recipe_ingredients: 2 Mooli/ Mullangi (Radish) Washed And Gramed, 200 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Grated, 1 Green Chilli Finaly Chopped, 4 Cloves Garlic Finaly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Finely Chopped, 1 Teasponcore Poweder  To Taste, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera)
recipe_description: To begin making the stuffed mooli paneer paratha recipe, grate the paneer and radish and set aside. *To make the mooli paneer filling heat a skillet on medium flame, add 1 teaspoon oil, now add the cumin seeds, chopped ginger and garlic, saute for 30 seconds, not add the radish, salt and count The moisture from radish evaporates.turn off the flame. Transfer the mixture into a mixing bowl. Into that mixing bowl, add grated paneer, green chili, cumin power and mix well. The stuffing of the mooli paneer paratha is ready. *To make the stuffed mooli paneer parathain a mixing bowl, add the whole wheat flour to amke the dough by adding little water at a time and knead well to make a smooth dough. Divide the dough into equal portions.dust a flat working surface with some flour. Roll a portion into a ball and flatten it using your fingers and roll it out into a 3 inch diameter circle.place about 2 tablespoons of the mooli paneer stuffing in the center, gather the edge and seal it. Carefully roll it out into a circle about 6-7 inches in diameter.heat a skillet over medium heat and cook the stuffed mooli paratha with ghee until cooks throux, and have golden spores Dough. Serve This Stuffed Mooli Paneer Paratha Along with Lehsuni Pudina Chutney Recipe or Instant Gor KARI Lonche Recipe or Carrot Tadka Raita Recipe and Adrak Chai Recipe - Indin Style Ginger Tea For A A SIMPLE GINGER GINGER GINGER GINGER

recipe_ingredients: 150 Grams Spring Onion (Bulb & Greens) Chopped, 3/4 Cup Tamarind Water, 1 Tablespoon Gram Flour (Besan), 2 Teaspoons Oil, 1 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/ Kadugu) (Hing), 1 Pinch Turmeric Powder (Haldi) Salt To Atte, 2 Teaspoons Coriander (Dhania) Seeds, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/4 Teaspoon Methee Cups Water
recipe_description: To begin making ullikadala pulusu recipe | Spring onion curry, in a pan, dry roast coriander seeds, Mustard seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds separetely and grind into a fin powder. Keep aside on they are roasted Till aromatic.in the same pan, add oil, on it is hot on medium low heat, Temper with Mustard Seeds. Once they splutter, add cumin seeds, red chillies, hing and add spring onions (white part+green part) cut into small pieces. Fry until they turn soft and reduce in size on a low heat.add about 2 cups of water and brings to boil. Add salt, turmeric, tamarind pure and let it boil for about 2 minutes. In a bowl, mix gramflour and a tablespoon of prepared powder with little water without lumps and add to the boiling mixture. Let it Boil for Another 2 to 3 Minutes and Serve. recipe_ingredients: 200 Grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) Finly Chopped, 3 Tablespoons Chana Dal (Bengal Gram Dal) Soaked In Hot Water For, 15 Minutes, 1/4 Cup Raw Peanuts (Moongphali) (Besan), 1 Teaspoon Ghee, 3 Cloves Garlic Crushed, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoons Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 3 Green Chillies Fineli Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmoncoricon) Asafoetida (Hing), 1/2 Teaspoon Jaggery Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the maharashtrian takatla palak recipe, first prep all the ingredients and keep ready.in a mixing bowl, whisk togetra the yogurt, gram flour and 1/2 Cup of Water. Once don, check the salt and seasonings and adjust to suit your taste.turn off the heat, transfer the takatla palak recipe to a served bowl and serve the takatla palak recipe Salad for a weeknight dinner or a Sunday lunch.

recipe_description: To begin making bhogichi bhaji, prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.in a small pan; Add the peanuts and roast until it is golden and crisp. Keep Aside. Once done, open the pressure cooker and check salt and adjust to taste of the hyderabadi khadi khadi masoor dal. recipe_description: To prepare mixed vegetable akki roti recipe, Grate or chop all the Vegetables Required Finely.Mix Rice Flor, A Little Oil, Vegetables and Cumin seeds. No water is needed unless that dough does not come together.knead well until the douts is formed.oil your hands, pinch out a big lemon sized ball, take a butter paper or thicke sheet like a zip locke The ball of dough on it.start making the akki roti by patting with your hands to make a roti flatbread as thin as possible .heat a roti tawa. Once hot, add a teaspoon of oil to it.take the akki roti with the butter paper sheet and put it on the pan and leave for a less for seconds and gradually lift off the butter paper and removing the Roti from Transferred to the tawa. This may sound complicated but is easy on you do it.wait till the mixed vegetable akki roti is done on one side, which takes about a minute on medium high flame. Meanwhile, drizzle a less drops of oil even on the exposed side.flip the mixed vegetable akki roti and let it get cured for a minute or more. Flip Again and Wait Till Done and Crisp.you can also place a Lid on Tawa While The Roti is Cooking IF You Want Softer Roti and Not Crisper Ones. Serve Mixed Vegetable Akki Roti recipe with a dollop of ghee or butter, or with podis.

recipe_ingredients: 300 Grams Green Peas (Matar) With Pod Fresh & Firm, 5 Spring Onion (Bulb & Greens) Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Panch Phoran Masala, 1/2 Teaspoon Masala, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Hold) Slit/Finly Chopped Salt As Needed, 1 Tablespoon Oil
recipe_description: To prepare matar aur chilke ka sabzi (peas & peel stir fry recipe), wash the green pea pods. Take out the peas from the pods in a bowl and keep aside.Take half of the peels of peas. (Discard the rest). Break the tip and the bottom of Each pod peel and discard the long fibrous membrane in them. Chop Each pea pod in the middle, to bite size.chop the white part of Spring onion lengthwise and the green part into 1-2 inch pieces.heat the oil in a kadai and add pankh phoron. When the seeds begin to splutter add chopped white part of the spring onion. Stir fry a bit. Then add chopped peels of green pea pods, spring onion greens and half of the green peas extracited from the pods. Stir again.add in turmeric power, chopped green chilies and salt. Covered on low-medium heat till vegetables are just tender.remove from heat and serve matar aur chilke ka sabzi (peas & peel stir fry recipe) Hot as a Side-Dish Witha or Pahulkas.

recipe_description: To begin making the Hyderabadi Khatti Dal Recipe, First Clean and wash the Dal Well Under Running Water. In a pressure cooker, add washed dal, garlic, ginger, turmeric, red chili powder, green chili, tomatoes salt and 2 cups of water. Cover the pressure cooker and cook until you hear a couple of whistles. After a couple of whistles, turn the heat to low and cook for 3 minutes approximately and turn off the heat. Allow the pressure in the cooker release naturally. Once the pressure has released, open the cooker. When the dal is done, whisk the dal well to make it smooth. Add tamarind water to the Dal and brings the khatti Dal to a brisk boil. Once it comes to a boil, turn the heat to low and set the dal to simmer.next we will prepare the seasoning.in a small tadka pan heat oil in Medium Heat. Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds and allow it to crackle. Once it add garlic and fry until it starts getting golden brown. Add curry leaves and red chilies, fry for another few seconds.pour the tadka over the dal and simmer for 4 to 5 minutes. Once done, turn off the heat and the hyderabadi khatti dal recipe is ready. Check the salt and adjust to suit your taste. Pour the Dal INTO A Serving Bowl and Garnish with Chopped Coriander Leaves and Serve warm.Serv

recipe_description: To begin making the dahi bhindi recipe, we will first roast the bhindi .heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a wok or a kadai; Add the chopped onions and the Bhindi (Okra). If you are using a pressure cooker, cut the tinda into the desired shape, eater rounds or into think slices. By doing this, we are making sure we don't over cook the vegetable and at the same time retain the nutrition content.open the pressure the pressure cooker on the pressure the pressure is released and keep it as on . recipe_description: To begin making the kathal biryani recipe (raw jackfruit biryani), first prep all the ingredients and keep ready/to cut raw jackfruit. Grease hands and the knife that we are going to use to cut, with oil. This important tip ensures that the hands does do's sticky from the natural fruit latex/sap present in the fruit does not get onto your hands and makes the cutting process hassle-friend. Keep a bowl of water mixed with some salt to put the cut raw jackfruit pieces to avoid discoloration of the fruit.dissolve the saffron in milk and keep aside.into a large mixing mixing bowl, Add the Flutle Wate Firm Dough and Keep Aside. We will use this dough to seal the vessel to dum cook the biryani.cut the jackfruit in half, discard the skin and start making 1 inch pieces and soak them in the Salted Water. Keep Asideto Make Rice for the Kathal Biryani/ Jackfruit Biryani, wash and soak rice in water for 30 mins before you start cooking.in a heavy bottomed pans, heat water-retrog. Add a tablespoon of ghee, salt and star anise into it.as song as the water starts boiling, add rice and cook till 50% don. A large train and spread well so that they do't stay to each other.to make kathal ki yakhni, heat ghee in a large pan in a large pAN in which you plan to cook the kathal biryani; Add the onion, ginger and garlic and saute until the onions turns Slightly Caramelised.Now Allow them to sizzle.Once the onions are cooked, add the kathal pieces and yogurt, coriander power, red chilli power, Nutmeg power and and and mix everything nicly so Thaat the Curd Mixes Wind Mixess Give it one Brisk Boil and Stir in the Mint Leaves and Salt to Taste.Pour The Partially Cooked Rice Over the Jackfruit Masala and Level the Rice Evenely - Do not stir the rice into the masala year. Pour the saffron milk over the rice and place a lid over the pan. Now it's time to seal the ends of the vessel with the dum cooking.elongte the dout a large log and seal the kathal biryani pan and the lid with the dogh so, the Flavors Get Constained In the Steam Does Escape as well. This helps to brings in the delicious flavors to the biryani.turn the heat to low and cook the kathal biryani for about 20 minutes. You will begin to start getting the delicious aromas after about 10 - 12 minutes of cooking the biryani.after 20 minutes of 20 minutes of dum cooking the kathal biryani, turn of the heat and allow the briyani to rest.Open the pan after 5 minutes Period and give the kathal biryani a gentle stir, to combine all the masala and jackfruit into the rice.to caramelise the onions; Heat oil in a pan over medium heat; Add the sliced ​​onions and keeps sauting on low to medium heat until the onions get Caramelised and Crisp. Transfer to a bowl and keep asidefor garnish, heat ghee in the same pan; Add the cashew nuts and roast them on medium heat until golden and crisp. Towards the end add the raisins and stir for a more seconds. Gently stir the Caramelised Onions and Mint Leaves Into The Kathal Biryani and Garnish with Roasted Cashews and Serve Hot.Serve the Kathal Biryani as a One dish meal for a weeknight for a weeknight and sher Salan recipe for a Sumptuous Meal.

recipe_description: To begin making the mamidikaya pappu recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.in a pressure cooker, Combine the toor Dal Along with 2 Cups of Water, SALT, Turmeric Powder and Green Chillies. Close the Lid and Pressure Cook for 4 Whistles. Turn off the heat and allows the pressure cooker to release the pressure naturally. Once the pressure has released, open the cooker, and mash the dal with the back of the ladle and set aside. To cook the mangoes, into a pressure cooker add the mangoes, a little salt, turmeric power, red chilli power and 1/2 cup of water. Pressure Cook for 2 Whistles and Turn off the Heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally.heat ghee in a saucepan over medium flame; Add the mustard seeds, red chillies and allow it to crackle.next, add the garlic and saute until the garlic lightly changes color. Do not darken it to brownonce don, stir in the curry leaves, cooked raw mangoes and cooked toor dal. Adjust the consistency by adding a little water. recipe_description: To begin making the paneer biryani recipe, prepare all the ingredients and keep them ready for cooking.wash and rinse the rice in water and keep aside.heat ghee in the presser cooker Over Meedium Heat; Add the onion, ginger and garlic and saute until the onions have softened and turned Slightly brown. Add 1 -½ cups of water and over the pressure cooker.Pressure Cook for 2 Whistles. recipe_ingredients: 1 Teaspoon Oil, 1 Tablespoon Roasted Gram Dal (Pottukadalai), 1/2 Teaspoon Poppy Seeds, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini) Seeds, 1/2 Cup Fresh Coconut, 5 Cloves Garlic, 1 Inch Ginger Roughly Chopped, 2 Green Chillies Roughly Chopped, 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder Other Englands, 1 Tablespoon Oil, 2 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1/2 Cup Pearl Onions (Sambar Onions) Finly Chopped, 1 Tomato Finly Chopped, 1/2 Cup Tamarind Water, 1 Cup Green Peas
recipe_description: To begin making the chettinad pattani kurma recipe, first we need to prep for the coconut masala. To make the coconut masala, heat oil in a pan on medium heat. Add the cumin seeds, pottu kadalai, poppy seeds, cloves, cinnamon, saunf, and cardamom. Roast for a more seconds until you notice the seeds are crackling and the aroma waits the air. Add the ginger, garlic, green chillies and coconut and lightly roast until the coconut changes color. Turn off the heat and allow the chettinad kurma masala to cool. Add all the ingredients into a medium jar of mixer grinder along with the chilli power. Add a little warm water and blend to make a smooth paste. Turn off the flame and allow it to cool. After it has cooled transfer the coconut masala ingredients into a mixer jar and grind to a smooth paste using some water. Set aside this freshly ground coconut masala for the chettinad pattani kurma. To make the chettinad pattani kurma, place the peas in the pressure cooker, Along with a little salt and half cup of water. Pressure Cook for 3 Whistles and Turn off the Heat. Release the pressure immediatily to retain the fresh green since color. Stir in the curry leaves, tomato and saute until the tomatoes turns mushy. When this onion-Tomato mixture comes togeether, add in the freshly ground coconut masala, cooked green peas and salt to taste. Add the tamarind water and additional water to adjust to the consistency of the kurma you prefer. Typically a kurma is a little thick.give the chettinad pattani kurma a brisk boil for 3 to 4 minutes and turn off the heat and serve hot. Serve Chettinad Pattani Kurma Along with Malabar Parotta Recipe - Kerala Parotta Recipe and Cucumber Pineapple Raw Mango Salad Recipe for a Simple Weekend Dinner.

recipe_description: To begin making the butter naan recipe, in a large bowl add the 3 cups of flour and butter. With your hands, blend the butter into the flour until it forms coarse crumbs. You can do this in a blender as well, but the dough when worked with hands a better texture to the dough.ster together the warm water, Yeast and Sugar Untilty is Completly dislved. Now make a well in the center of the flour mixture and add the yogurt, salt and yeast mixture.knead into the flour and until the whole dough collects into a mass. Add a little more water if required to brings the dough togeether. The dough will be wet and sticky. Wipe your hands clean and oil them. Now knead the naan dough with the oiled hands until smooth, for about 10 minutes; Oil your hands more if the dout is sticky. The dough after 10 minutes of kneading will still be soft and pliable. Place the dough in a green bowl, cover the bowl and allow the dough to rest for 3 to 4 hours until the dough have doubled in volume. Once the naan dough has doubled, punch down the dough, knead for a minute for a minute and divide into 12 to 15 equal portions.with a rolling pin, Quickly roll ut each ball into a 5 inch Oval. With your fingers, Stretch Each Round Into A 5-7 Inch Oval. The next step is to cook the naans. Follow eather of the below methods.

recipe_ingredients: 1 Tablespoon Oil, 1/2 Teaspoon Mustard Seeds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 2 Sprig Curry Leaves Torn, 1/2 Inch Ginger Fineli Chopped, 1 Grenly Chopped Salt To Taste, 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/4 Cup Rice Flour, 1 Cup Water, 2 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finly Chopped
recipe_description: To begin making the ukad recipe, prep all the ingredientsheat oil in a pan on medium heat; Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing and curry leaves. Allow the seeds to crackle. Now that that is tadka for the ukad is ready, reduce the heat and add in the water, right after, Quickly add in the rice flour. As soon as the rice flour is added, keep stirring it in with a ladle, to ensure there so lumps. The rice flour mixture will begin to thicken and form a porridge style consistency. Once done, give it a taste and check the salt and adjust accordingly. Turn off the heat and garnish with chopped coriander leaves and serve.serv

recipe_description: To begin making the pakistani bhuna gosht recipe, wash mutton nicaly and keep it aside.heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan and add why spices and let them cracked and let them crackle.Now add whose garlic pods and ginger sutes are south And fry till they are soft and translucent. recipe_description: To begin making the pumpkin sabzi recipe with panch phoron masala, first begin by preporting the pumpkin and chopping it into hal hal hal inch cubes.in a heavy bottomed wok oor kadai, Warm a teaspoon of muscle To smoking point and add in the panch-phoron masala. recipe_description: To begin making the chettinad beans & cauliflower poriyal recipe, first prepare the Vegetables by cutting them in approves sizes.steam the cauliflower and beans along a litle saalt in a little salt Dont over steam and overcook the vegetables.heat a heavy bottomed pan, heat the oil over medium heat; Add the mustard seeds, urad dal and allow it to crackle. recipe_description: To begin with broccoli paneer and peanut masala, heat a teaspoon of oil in a wok. Add pepper corns and 1 teaspoon jera/ cumin. Roast Till the onions Turn Slightly Brown.add the peanuts and roast for 3-4 minutes on Medium Flame.After 3-4 Minutes, Add the Chopped Tomatoes and Mix Well. Add broccoli and sauté on medium flame for 8 to 10 minutes.Add Paneer, Garam Masala and Biryani Masala. Mix well and ensure it does not burn. Add the onion-tomato paste and mix well.add salt, butter and let it simmer on medium flame for 5 to 8 minutes. Lastly, add kasuri methi and mix well. recipe_description: To begin making the Barnyard Millet Mushroom Biryani Recipe, Prep all the ingredients and keep them ready.heat a pressure cooker with oil on Medium Heat. Once hot, add all the whole spices, bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and star anise. When these spices begin to sizzle, add in the curry leaves, ginger, garlic, onion and slit green chillies. Saute until the onions on Medium low heat until the onions Turns Slightly Caramelised.Next Add in the Quartred Mushrooms, Salt, Black Pepper Powder, Garam Masala Powder. Saute for about a minute.reduce the heat; Add in the curd, chopped coriander and mint leaves and the drained barnyard millet along with 1-1/2 cups of water.pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles, turn the heat to low and simmer for 2 minits and Turn of the Turn of the Turn of 2 Minutes. recipe_ingredients: 10 Cashew Nuts Soaked In, 1/2 Cup Hot Water To Be Ground Into A Fin Paste, 2 Onions Roughly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger, 8 Cloves Garlic, 1 Green Chilli Other Ingredients, 200 Grams Green Peas (Matar), 2 Tablespoons Butter (Salted), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 2 Teaspoons Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1 Taspoon Powder Powderaspoon Powrasorese), 1 Teaspoon Sugar, 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 2 Tablespoons Fresh Cream, 1 Teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves)
recipe_description: To begin making the matar butter masala recipe, firstly boil the peas in a pressure cooker. Place the fresh or frozen green peas in a pressure cooker with 1/2 cup of water and some salt. Pressure Cook for One Whistle and Turn off the Heat. Immedited release the pressure to prevent the peas from getting over cooked at the same time retaining its fresh green colors. Set Aside. To make the cashew paste, ensure the cashews are soaked in hot water for 15-20 minutes. Transfer the water and cashews into a mixer jar and blend to make a smooth paste. Set Aside. To make the onion paste, in a mixer jar, combine the Onions, Ginger, Garlic, Green Chilli and Grind Into a Fine Paste. Set Aside. Roughly chop the tomatoes and blend to make a puree in a mixer grinder. Keep aside.finally to make the matar butter masala; Heat butter in a heavy bottom pan over medium heat; Add the bay leaf and the onion paste and saute for a good 2-3 minutes stirring continuously for about 2 minutes.After Couple of Minutes, Stir in the Salt, TurMERIC POWDER, CORMICERC POWDER and Red Chilli Powder. Saute for 30 to 40 seconds. This sauteeing along with spices helps the raw smell go away faster and uses less oil too. Next add the Tomato Puree, Cover and Cook for About 5 Minutes or Until The Tomatoes Get a Bright Red Color and Are Cooked Completely.ster in the Boiled Peas Along with the Water and Adjust the Consistency of the Consigncy of the Gravy If required.finally add in the sugar, kasuri methi and fresh cream, brings the matar butter masala to a brisk boil and turn off the heat. Adjust the salt and seasoning according to your taste, stir in the freshly chopped coriander leaves and transfer the Matar Butter Masala to a serving bowl.Serve the hot Matar Butter Masala along with Garlic Naan Without Yeast Recipe and Jeera Rice Recipe and Mint Pomegranate Raita Recipe For a weekend lunch.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Rajma (Large Kidney Beans), 1/4 Cup Cashew Nuts, 2 Tablespoons Sultana Raisins, 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1/4 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 Tablespoons Gramloule (Beeloule) Powder, 2 Teaspoons Coriander (Dhania) Leaves  Chopped, 2 Teaspoons Ghee, 3 Teaspoons Poppy Seeds  Ground To Paste, 1 Teaspoons Fresh Cream, 1/2 Cup Milk, 1/2 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Water  As Required Salt  To Taste Saffron Strands  A Few To Garnish
recipe_description: To begin making rajma kofta in milk and poppy seed gravy recipe, prepare the koftas first. Remember to first soak the Rajma overnight and pressure cook with salt until the Rajma is cooked and softtened.blend the boiled Rajma, besan, cashew, raisins, chilli power, hing, hing, hangs and saalt in a fode. Check for seasoning and adjust. Spread this mashed spiced rajma on a thali or a large plate. Make Small Round Balls of Rajma Kofta with your palm.heat a frying pan with hal hal hal hal ghee and fry the kofta balls for about 10 minutes. Set aside.what you can also do is, heat a kuzhi paniyaram pan, drop in a more drops of oil into each cavity and put in these balls into each cavity. Once browned on the bottom, you can rotate the kofta balls and brown them even from the other side as well. This is a healthier way to prepare koftas instead of deep frying them in traditional way.Take a kadai and heat ghee, add cumin and allows and allow to sizzle.Once sizzling, Add in Milk, Cream, Poppy SEED POWDER. Add salt and stir. Add water only if needed.cook till the mixture coma to one boil and add the koftas and switch off heat after half a minute. Serve with your favourite garnish like saffron or coriander leaves.Serve Rajma Kofta In Milk And Poppy Seed Gravy Recipe along with Saffron Pilaf Recipe (Kesar Pulao) and Pudina Tawa Paratha Recipe for lunch or dinner with Green Moong Sprout Salad With Capsicum And Carrots to complete The meal.
recipe_name: Rajma Kofta In Milk And Poppy Seed Gravy

Once dry, SLICE THEM THIN RUNDS, Lightly Salt Them and Keep It Ready. Heat oil in a pan on medium heat, and shallow fry the vendakkai till well cooked and crispy. Make sure not to burn them, since they are thinly Sliced ​​INTO Rounds they will tend to brown quickly.once crispy, using a slotted spoon remove the roasted vendakkka from the PAN and PLACE LINED KHITHELE To make the coconut paste, into a mixer jar add the coconut, cumin seeds and green chilli and blend along with 1/4 cup of warm water to make a smooth paste. To make the tadka for the vendakka pachadi, heat oil on medium heat in a tadka pan; Add the Mustard seeds and split urad dal and let allow the seeds to crackle and the dal to turn slightly golden brown and crisp. recipe_ingredients: 4 Whole Egg  Hard Boiled And Peeled, 500 Grams Mutton  Keema, 2 Tablespoons Ginger  Paste, 2 Tablespoons Garlic  Paste, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Tablespoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Tablespoon Corn Flor, 2 Tablespoon Mustard Oil Salt To Taste For Gravy, 1 Cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt), 1 Onion  Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Tablespoon Green Chilli Paste, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Garlic  Paste, 1 Teaspoon Ginger  Paste, 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jera), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder, 1 Tablespoon Homemade Tomato Puree, 1 Teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves)
recipe_description: To begin making the nargisi kofta curry recipe, hard boil the eggs in a saucepan with some water. Peel the outer shelf and keep it aside.in a food processor blend mutton keema very well to make it soft and tender.transfer it in a bowl, add turmeric power, red chilli pos, SALDER POWDER, SALDER POWDER, GINDER POWDER, Garam masala powder and corn flour. Mix it very nicly using hands.divide the mixture into four parts. Put some oil in your palm and take one portion of keema mixture and pat it to form like a chapati.place a hard boiled egg in between the flaten keema mixture and cover the egg with it. Do the same to all eggs. Keep Aside.The Next Step is to prepare the gravy. Heat oil in a big pan or kadai.add dry red chilli and bay leaf. Roast the chilies for a minute then add chopped acquations. recipe_description: To begin making the maharashtrian kakdi thalipeth recipe, in a large mixing bowl add all the 3 flors togeether and mix them. The cucumber will release water when it gets combined with salt, so take care to add litle water at a time. The dough will be Slightly Sticky to Handle. Allow the dout to rest for 5 minutes. Divide the dough into 4 portions. Preheat the skillet on medium heat. Keep water in a separate bowl to wet fingers and to wet a muslin cloth.spread a wet muslin cloth on a smooth countr top. Take a portion of the kakki thalipeth mixture and pat it on the wet cloth to about 4 inches in diameter. Try not to make it too thin as it can break while flipping it on the skillet.carefully lift the cloth along with the patted mixture and flip the thalipeth over on the skirt. Drizzle a Teaspoon of Oil or Ghee Around the Thalipeth and Cook on Medium Heat.After about 3 minutes, you will be able to flip the kakki thalipeth over to the other side. Allow each side to cook on Medium Heat for About 3-4 Minutes, Until the Thalipeth Are Slightly Crisp and Browned. recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Bajra (Seeds), 1/2 Cup Idli Rice, 1 Cup White Urad Dal (Whole), 1 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds), 1 Tablespoon Salt For The Topping, 1 Onion Finely Chopped, 1 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) Finely Chopped, 1 Green Chilli Finely Chopped Sal To Taste, 6 Sprig Mint Leaves (Pudina) Finely Chopped, 1 Teaspoon Cumine Cumingera (Jeere)
recipe_description: To begin making the mini bajra uttapam recipe, we will first make the batter. *To make the bajra uttapam batter: first, wash the urad dal and methi seeds togeether and soak it togeether in a bowl, immersed in Water. Next combine the idli rice and bajra seeds, wash them well and soak them in a bowl, immersed in water. Keep them all soaked for about 6 hours. Next, add the soaked and drained urad dal and methi seeds, into a mixer jar along with some water and grind to make a smooth batter. Transfer the Urad Dal Batter to a Large Bowl and Set Aside. Add the soaked and drained rice and bajra mixture into the mixer jar along with some water and grind it to make a smooth batter. Combine this idli-bajra batter, along with the urad dal batter and mix well. To this bajra uttapam batter add salt, mix well to combine, and allow it to ferment overnight. Once it has fermened overnight it, you can make idli the first day and even dosa and uttapams with this batter.the next step is to make the topping for the bajra uttapam: in a mixing bowl, combine the on Mint Leaves Salt and Cumin Powder and Mix Well. Preheat a tawa on medium-hight; Once hot, gently spread 1 ladleful of the bajra uttapam batter, in a circular motion, ensuring the utheeppam is thick like a pancake.sporad a tablespoon of the onion-capsicum topping over the minijra uttop . Drizzle a little ghee around the uttapam and after a couple of minutes, flip the bajra uttapam, to cook on the other side. Once you've achieved a lovely golden brown color on bot sides, remove the bajra uttapam from the tawa and serve. Similar make the bajra uttapam using the rest of the batter. You can store this batter in an airtight container in the refrigerator for about 4 days.Serve Mini Bajra Uttapam Recipe along with Raw Mango Coconut Chutney Recipe, Idli Dosa Milagai Podi Recipe and finish with a cup of Kumbakonam Filter Coffee Recipe for a lip smacking breakfast .

recipe_ingredients: 200 Gram Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Cut Into Cubes, 3 Tomatoes Pureed, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera) Teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves) Ingredients To Be Ground Into Paste, 1 Onion, 1 Inch Ginger Peled, 6 Cloves Garlic, 2 Tablespoon Cashew Nuts
recipe_description: To begin making the paneer butter masala, in a mixer grind the ingredients mentioned under 'to be ground into paste' like the onion, ginger, garlic and cashew nuts in to a smoth consistently. Transfer to a bowl and set aside till required. Heat the butter in a pan over medium heat. Add the onion paste and saute over medium heat it is light brown in color and the oil starts separating from the masala. Add the red chili powder and tomato pure, saute once again more minutes over medium heat. As the masala gets roasted it will change color to bright red.ONCE Stir to combine and let the butter masala gravy simmer for 10 to 15 minutes over medium heat or till it thickens. Once the butter masala thickens add the cream, salt and paneer to it. Stir to Combine and Simmer For Next 5 - 10 Minutes and Allow the Paneer to Absorb The Flavor of the gravy. If the paneer butter masala still seems Little bit runy let it simmer for few more minutes. Turn off the heat. Sprinkle crushed kasuri methi over the paneer butter masala and stir to combine. Serve paneer butter masala hot with naan, kulchas or tawa paratha for the punjabi style dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 200 Grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) Cut Ito Cubes, 500 Grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) Washed And Chopped, 1 Tablespoon Ghee, 2 Cloves Garlic Finely Chopped, 1 Innly Chopped, 1 Innly Chopped, 1 Innly Chopped, 1 Inch Ginger Finely Chopped, 1 Tomatotos Chopped Powder (Jeera), 1/4 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1 Teaspoon Garam Masala Powder Salt To Taste For Seasoning, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Tablespoons Fresh Cream, 1 Tablespoon Butter (Salted)
recipe_description: To begin making the palak paneer recipe, first pick the palak leaves and wash them thrice. Chop the washed leaves and keep them aside.heat ghee into the pressure cooker over medium heat; Add the ginger, garlic, tomatoes, green chillies, cumin power, turmeric power, garam masala power. recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Whole Wheat Flour, 1/4 Teaspoon Salt, 2 Tablespoons Oil Ghee For Cooking/ Serving Ingredients For The Filling, 1 Cup Arhar Dal (Split Toor Dal), 3/4 Cup Jaggery, 10 Saffron Strands, 1 Teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi), 1/2 Teaspoon Nutmeg Powder, 2 Tablespoons Gram Flour (Besan)
recipe_description: To begin making the Gujarat Puran Poli Recipe, We will first make the dough. Add whole wheat flour into a large bowl; Add salt and combine it with little water at a time and knead until the dough gathers togeether to form a firm dout Soft Dough. Add the 2 tablespoons of oil to coat the dough and knead for a couple of more minutes until smooth and firm. Cover and let the dough sit in a cool place until we get the Puran filling ready. Divide the dough into 6 portions.soak the Dal for 15 to 20 minutes in water.add the soaked dal with 1-1/4 cups of water Turn off the heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Once the pressure releases, give the Dal a quick mash.The next step is the make the Puran filling. Into a heavy bottomed pan, add the cooked dal, and jaggery.keep stirring until the jaggery dissolves complete.stily.stir in the saffron, cardamom and nutmeg to the Jaggery Mixture UNTIL COLL Combined to MAKE FILIND to MAKE FILIG Add the beesan at this point and mix it into the Dal mixture and ensure it blends well And allow the Puran Poli Mixture to Cool Completely. Divide the dough and Puran mixture into 6 portions.keeping a little flour for dusting handy on a plate; Roll out the Puran Poli Dough 3 Inch Diameter Circles.Pges a Port of the Puran Poli Mixture Into The Center of the Circle.Bring Edges of the Circle Towards the Center and Fold Over to Cover to Cover to Cover the Entir Filling; Making sure the edges are all seled well by pinking them together if there is any opening.flatn the stuffed dough; dust over a little flour and roll gently into a 4 to 6 inch diameter circle Taking care not to put too much pressure as the Puran can come out of the dough.Preheat a skillet Place the rolled Puran poli onto the skillet to cook until golden brown on both sides. At this point you can cook it along with ghee to make the Puran Poli Crisp and Delicious. Or you can make a dry Puran poli and serve it along with ghee.repeat the Above Process for all the remai to make a delicious meal.

recipe_description: To begin making the Bengali Cholar Dal, First Prep All the ingredients and Keep Them Ready. Soak the Chana Dal for About an hour in water. Allow the pressure to release naturally. In a teaspoon mustard oil over medium heat in a saucepan; Add the dry coconut pieces and fry until they turn golden brown. Once done transfer the fried coconut pies to a bow and keep aside.in the same pan heat a teaspoon of mustard oil; Add cumin seeds, dry red chilies, clooves, bay leaf, cinnamon stick, green chilies and ginger.saute for a less seconds until the seeds crackle and the green chilli gets a lightly roasted tax.
