recipe_ingredients: 500 Grams Sardine Fish, 1 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 10 Shallots, 3 Green Chillies, 1/4 Tablespoon Dry Red Chilli Crushed, 2 Inch Ginger, 3 Clove Garlic, 1/4 Teaspoon Methi Powder Teaspoon Coconut Oil Adjustable, 1 Sprig Curry Leaves, 1 Kokum (Malabar Tamarind) Soaked For Cleaning Fish, 2 Kokum (Malabar Tamarind) For The Curry Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To prepare meen peera (Kerala style fish recipe with shreded coconut), wash and clean the sardines and marinete it with 1 small piece of kudampuli and salt. Keep aside for 10 minutes for 10 minutes. Drain all the water. Cut Each Fish Into 3 Pies for Better Incorporation of Flavor.Add the Cleaned Sardines Into a Meen Chatti (Earthenware) or a PAN. Keep Aside.crush or Coarsely Grind (If you are using a mixer grinder for this process, just pulse it 1 or 2 times till you get a coarsely crushed mixture) Togera coconut, gerlic, green chillies, 2 celsi Leaves and crushed dry red chili.add the coconut mix and 2 big pieces of kudampuli into the sardines and mix well. (I used my hands, but you could always use a wooden spoon to do this) Add Salt, Coconut Oil, Fenugreek Powder, Turmic Powder and Turmic Powder and the rest of the curry leaves and the curry leaves and MIX Well.Now Add a Little Water, Just The fish.bring the mixture togeether with a wooden spoon and press down firmly.cover with a lid and cook on a high flame. When the mixture is boiling, lower the flame and cook for about 5-6 minutes.Now Continue to Cook the Fish with the Lid Slightly Open. Sauté occasionally and cook till water has reduced.open the Lid Completely and Continue to Cook the Fish a Medium High Flame Until Most of the Liquid has dried up. (Take care not to burn the dish) .Serve meen peera (kerala style fish recipe with shreded coconut)

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Green Moong Sprouts, 1 Onion Finly Chopped, 3 Cloves Garlic Crushed, 1 Tablespoon Ghee Or Oil, 1 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu) Asafoetida (Hing), 1/4 Teaspoon Maharashtrian Goda Masala, 1/4 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 2 Kokum (Malabar Tamarind), 1/2 Teaspoon Jaggery Grated, 2 Teaspoon Powon Grated, 2 Teaspoon Powon Power, 2 Town Leaves Chopped For Garnishing
recipe_description: To begin making the sprouted moong dal recipe, heat ghee/oil in a pressure cooker.add the Mustard seeds and the cumin seeds and on they crackle add the onion, garlic, asafoetida and sautes it for 1-2 minutes. .AFTER 1-2 minutes, add the turmeric power, moong sprouts and sauté it for about a minute.next add the masala, chilli power and mix cell. Add water and then add the kokum, jaggery, salt, peanut powder and stir well to combine.cover it with the lid and cook it till the cooker relations 1 Whistle or Till the Sprouts Are Well Cooks. Adjust the consistency of water if required.Onece the sprouted moong dal is done, garnish with fresh coriander leaves and service hot.serv

recipe_ingredients: 6 Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra), 2 Tomato Roughly Chopped, 6 Pearl Onions (Sambar Onions) Quartred, 4 Garlic, 1 Cup Tamarind Water, 1 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/2 Teaspoon Teaspoon Teaspoon Teaspoon Teaspon Powder), 1 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1 Teaspoon Sugar Salt To Taste Seasoning, 1 Teaspoon Ghee, 1/2 Teaspoon Musteds (Rai/Kadugu), 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/4 Teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds)
recipe_description: We begin making the Andhra style vendakaya charu recipe (ladies finger rasam recipe) Allow them to crackle. Next Add in the Chopped Onions, and Garlic Pods. Saute Till it is translucent. Then add in the tamarind water, sugar, salt, red chilli power, turmeric powder, asafoetida, coriander powderand chopped tomatoes and okra. Stir Well.Pressure Cook The Rasam for About 3 Whistles, Turn off the Flame and Allow The Pressure to Release Naturally. Serve the Andhra style vendakaya charu recipe (ladies finger rasam recipe)

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Bajra Flour (Pearl Millet), 1/2 Cup Jowar Flour (Sorghum), 1/2 Cup Whole Wheat Flour More For Dusting, 2 Cups Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) Fenugreek Leaves Finely Chopped, 2 Inch Ginger Droup Chillies Finely Chopped, 1 Tablespoon Seeds (Til Seeds), 2 Teaspoons Red Chilli Powder, 2 Teaspoons Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Haldi) (Hanspoon) (Hang), 5 Tablesponsponsponsponsponsponsponponponpone) Taste, 1 Tablespoon Oil
recipe_description: To prepare gujarati methi na dhebra recipe, get all the ingredients ready.in a large bowl, mix fenugreek leaves, ginger paste, green chilies, sesame seeds, chilli power, tari power, tari power, Turmeric Powertida. Mix it well with a teaspoon of oil. Keep Aside for 5 Minutes to Help Fenugreek Leaves Soften A Bit.Add Millet Flour, Whole Wheat Flour, Sugar and Salt.mix well using your finger so finger so finger so finger It will look crumbly texture.add tablespoon yogurt at a time and knead to form soft dough. You will need 5 or 6 tablespoon of yogurt depends on your flour or depends on the consistent of your yogurt. So add Little yogurt at a time so will come to know how much you need. Cover it and let it stand for 10-15 minutes.After 15 minutes, knead it again to make it smooth.Divide ito 10 equal portions. Roll Each Porte Between Your Palm and Press into a circle.heat the tawa on medium heat.dip the flattened ball into dry chapati flour and coat wall. Roll it gently in to circle about 6 inch in diameter.Once hot placed rolled dhebra on the skillet. When you see bubbles on it flip it. After few seconds apply some oil on it and flip it again and press gently using spatula and cook until you see some brown spot on bottom side.apply some again (optional) on online side and Flip and Flip on Take it out from Skillet/Tawa Proceed Similarly Till All The Dough is used up.serv

recipe_ingredients: 2 Apples Peled And Cubed, 2 Tomatoes Pureed Or Grated, 1/2 Teaspoon Turmic Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 1/4 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder Powder, 1 Cinnamon Stick (Dalchi)  Torn Salt Or Black Salt To Taste, 2 Sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Finly Chopped Oil For Cooking
recipe_description: We begin making the apple tamatar ki sabzi heating a kadai with a little bit of oil and add bay leaf and cinnamon. Saute Till the Aroma is released.next, Add the Tomato Pure and the other Spice Powders. Saute for About 10 Minutes Till the Raw Smell of the Tomato Goes Away.Add the cubed apple and give it a stir. Cook for 10 More Minutes Till the Apple Backets Mush and Imbibibes a Little bit of the gravy.Serve your sweet and tangy apple tamatar ki sabzi with singhare ka cheela or ajwain puri to make it a Complete Meal.

recipe_ingredients: 1/2 Cup Barnyard Millet (Bhagar Flour), 1/2 Cup Rajgira Flour (Amaranth Flour), 1/2 Cup Tapioca Flour (Sago), 1/2 Cup Singoda Flour (Water Chestnut) For The Thestnuthath:, 2 Potatoes (Aloo) Peled And Grated, 1 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 2 To, 3 Green Chillies Crushed Or Finely Chopped, 2 To, 3 Teaspoons Coriander (Dhania) Leaves, 2 Tablespoons Groderes Ghhhees Ghhhee Green
recipe_description: To begin making the upvas thalipeth, first mix all the flourrs in a mixing bowl.in a heavy bottom pan and on low heat, dry roast the Flour mixture for 2 to 3 minutes Till you get the typical aroma. Stir Continiously.cool and use as required. For the thalipeth, in a mixing bowl add all the ingredients except ghee.mix and add water to make a soft dough. Divide the dough into 4 to 5 portions.grease a non-screen griddle (pan) with some ghee. Place one portion of the dough in the pan and pat with your fingers to make a circle (4-5 inch) .make a hole at the center and 4 more around it using the tip of your finger.drizzle some ghee in these holes. Cook covered on low heat till the underside cooks and turns brown.flip and cook on the other side for a minute. REPEAT for the rest of the dough. You can use a plastic sheet/banana leaf to pat the dough.Serv

recipe_ingredients: 2 Tablespoons Ghee, 1 Tablespoon Ajwain (Carom Seeds), 1/4 Teaspoon Asafoetida (Hing), 1/2 Cup Flat Green Beans Cut Into, 1/2 Inch Size Pieces (Suans Or Snow Peas_, 1/4 Cupate, 1/4 Cup Elephant Yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu) Peled And Cubed, 1/4 Cup Purple Yam Peeled And Cubed, 1/4 Cup Raw Banana Peled And Cubed Salt To Taste Vegetables To Be Stupedables To Be Stuffed, 5 Small Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant) With, 4 Slits On Top, 5 Baby Potatoes Peled With, 4 Slits On Top For Undhiyu Masala, 1 Cup Fresh Coconut Grated, 1/2 Cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves Chopped, 4 Cloves Garlic, 1 Inch Ginger, 1 Inch Ginger, 1 Green Chillies, 3 Teaspoons Seeds (Til Seeds), 3 Teaspoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds Rospoon Corians Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1 Tablespoon Lemon Juice, 2 Teaspoons Sugar, 1 Teaspoon Sal To Taste Ingredients For Methia Muthia, 1/4 Cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) Finely Chopped, 1 Cup Gram Flour (Besan), 1/2 Teaspoon Turmeric Powder (Haldi), 1/2 Teaspoon Red Chilli Powder, 1/4 Teaspoon Asafotida (Hinspootida) Salt, 2 Teaspoons Sugar, 1 Lemon Juice Extracted, 1 Teaspoon Oil Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin making the gujarati undhiyu recipe - Mixed Vegetable With Fenugreek Dumplings, We will finally make the Muthiyas. Divide the dough into 15 small portions. You can alternatively steam the Muthia's - Prepare a Steamer and Place the Muthia in the Steamer's Colander and Allow it to Steam for 10 Minutes on Medium -High Heat. After 10 minutes you will notes the color have changed to golden yellow.t urn off heat; Take the Congress filled with Muthia's out of the steamer and keep Aside. *Method to make Undhiyu: Add all the ingredients for the masala into a mixer grinder, add 1/4 to 1/2 cup of water and grind to make a smooth paste. Fill the slit brinjals and baby potatoes with Above Filling Mixture and Keep Aside. Keep the Remining filling Aside. Add in two tablespoons of ghee, add the ajwain seeds and allow it to crackle. Add the stuffed brinjal and baby potatoes.add the yam, sweet potatoes, raw bananas and beans and layer it with the coconut mixture. Add 1/4 to 1/2 cup of water and pressure cook for 4 to 5 whistles and turn off the heat. Allow the pressure to release naturally Cover the pan and simmer for another 10 minutes and add more water if required. Cook until you notice all the vegetables and the muthias are well coated with the masala.heavy bottomed pan methodin a heavy bottomed paan; Add in two tablespoons of ghee, add the ajwain seeds and allow it to crackle. Add the stuffed brinjal and baby potatoes and shall fry until partally cooked. This process is done on low heat, so it consums less oil and also cooked even from the inseide. Add 1/4 to 1/2 cup of water and cover the pan and cook until the vegetables are cooked and coated well with the masala. This takes about half an hour.Once its three-front don, add the pan fried muthai's. Cover the pan and simmer for another 10 minutes and more water if required. Cook until you notice all the vegetables and the muthias are well coated with the masala.adjust the taste by adding more or less of salt. Check the consistency of the dish to see if you need to add more or less water to suit your taste. Turn off the heat and transfer to a serving dish.Serv Undhiyu on a Festive Occination Can Be Served Along With Methi Thepla, Khandvi, Puran Poli, Kadhi, Masala Chora Pulav, Paka Kela Raita and Mohanthal.

recipe_description: To make the saragva nu shaak recipe, we will first cook the drumsticks in a pressure cooker or a steamer.heat the oil in a pressure cooker over Medium heat; Add the kalonji seeds, ginger curry leaves, bay leaves and cinnamon stick.sauté for a less seconds and add the chopped tomatoes, turmeric powder, coriander power, cumin power, jag chilli power, SALTETH SHILTHE SHIGERTH SHIGERTES A bit.add the drumstick and 1/4 cup of water and cover the pressure cooker.Pressure cook the saragva nu shaak for about 3 to 4 whistles and turns of the heat. For this heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a frying pan, add the boiled potatoes, a pin of turmeric power, and salt.saute the potatoes till they are light golden brown in color. This process of roasting the potatoes gives a added flavor to the dish making it healthier and skipped the traditional process of deep frying them in oil.once golden brown and lightly crisp trainfs the potatos to For the Bengali Aloo Dum: in the same frying pan, add 2 tablespoons of oil. recipe_ingredients: 1-1/2 Cups Rice, 1/4 Teaspoon Saffron Strands, 2 Tablespoons Milk, 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin Seeds (Jeera), 1/2 Teaspoon Fennel Seeds (Saunf), 1 Inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini), 2 Cloves, 2 Tablespoons Ghee

recipe_ingredients: 4 Whole Eggs, 3 Tomatoes Finely Chopped, 2 Onions Finaly Chopped, 6 Cloves Garlic, 1/4 Inch Ginger, 6 Tablespoon Butter (Salted), 1 Bay Leaf (Tej Patta), 1 Tej Patta, 1 Taspoon, 1 Taspoon, 1 Taspoon, 1 Tablisoon Girmon Girmon Girmon Girmonge Fenugreek Leaves), 20 Cashew Nuts, 1 Tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste Salt To Taste
recipe_description: To begin with egg butter masala, soak cashew nuts in hot water for 30 minutes. After 30 minutes draws the water and retain the cashew nuts.boil the eggs and after cooling, crack and discard the shell. Keep them aside.in a kadhai, add 2 tablespoon butter. After the butter mults, add onion and fry till onion turns pinkish. Add Tomato and Saute Till Tomato Turns Mushy.Add Garlic, Ginger and Saute Till Raw Smell of Garlic Goes Away. Add the boiled cashew nuts and mix.switch off the gas. After cooling, grind the saten onion tomato mixture by adding 1 cup water.strain this gravy by passing through a strainer. recipe_description: To begin making the broccoli & vegetable coconut curry recipe, we will first make the broccoli curry masala. In a small pan; Roast the dry red chillies, fenugreek, coriander, cumin and black pepper until you can smell the roasted aromas. Add these to a small blender jar, along with the coconut, tamarind and turmeric powder. Grind the spices adding very little water to make a smooth curry paste. The curry paste is now ready. Keep aside.in the next step; Heat a teaspoon of oil in a pan; Add the chopped onions, broccoli and red bell peps and saute for a couple of minutes until the onions are soft and tender. Add the curry paste and saute for a couple of more minutes. Stir in salt and add about 1-1/2 to 2 cups of water. You essentially want a thick gravy for the broccoli curry; hence add water accordingly. Give all the ingredients a good stir. Check the salt levels and adjust to suit your taste. Give the mixture a quick boil only when you are ready to serve. Serve the broccoli & vegetable coconut curry, along with neer dosa or kerala (lachha) paratha or even plain steamed rice.

recipe_description: To begin making makki and methi roti recipe, heat some water in a pan till lukewarm.in a steel mixing bowl, Add mize flour, chopped fenugreek leaves, Green Chillies, Carom SEED and SALT. Place roti on the tawa and cook it on low flame from both sides until brown spots appear. Prepare the rest of the rotis in a similar way and serve hot. Serve Makki and Methi Roti with Sarson Ka Saag and Burani Raita for a delicious weekday meal.

Once cooled, add the roasted chole masala spices into a small jar of the mixer grinder and blend to make smooth powder.keep the amritsari chole masala powder aside.final power aside.final step is to brings to bested 2 tabether Pan over medium heat; Add the onion, ginger and saute until the onion softens.once the onion softns, add the dry amritsari chole masala, chopped tomatoes, Turmeric power and kala namak and saute the tomats until weecoms mushy and saft. recipe_description: To begin making moong dal sookhi sabzi, first wash dal and set it aside.heat oil in a deep pan and add red chillly power, turmeric powder and salt. Stir it for 10-12 seconds. Do not let the spice powders burn.after 10-12 seconds, add the washed lentil to this and give it a good stir.add enough water to cover the dal, reduce the heat and cover the pAN with.Cook the Dal Till The water gets evaporated. Check if the Dal is cooked, if not add a little more water and cook till the dal is cooked properly.make sure that you do not over cook the dal. Once done, open the lid, turn the heat to high, add garam masala power and give it a good stir.saute the moong dal sookhi sabzi sabzi till it is complete. After the dal has drilled completely, turn off the heat and transfer it to the serving bowl, add chopped green chillies, coriander leaves and lemon juice on the top.serve moong, moong, dal saokhi sakhai sakhel saukshi sakesi sack Dinner.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Cups Whole Wheat Flour, 3 Mooli/ Mullangi (Radish) Grown (Approximately, 2 Cups), 2 Green Chillies Finly Chopped, 2 Teaspoons Cumin Power (Jera) Taste Oil Oil For Cooking
recipe_description: To begin making the Mooli Paratha (Thepla) recipe, we will first cook the mooli. Heat a teaspoon of oil in a stir fry pan; Add in the grated mooli, sprinkle some salt and saute on medium heat until the mooli is soft. Turn off the heat and allow the mooli to cool completesly.Onece the mooli is coolede, in a large bowl combine, the mooli, whoat will flour, green chillies, cumin power, comin power, corridor leaves and salte to taste. The mooli will alredy has a lot of moisture in it, so when you knead the ingredients into a mooli paratha dout, add the water a little at a time.knead to make a smooth and soft mooli paratha dough. Add a teaspoon of oil on you have kneaded the mooli paratha dough, so that the dough feels non sticky to touch. If you kneaded the paratha dough well for at least 3 to 4 minutes, then start making the mooli paratha right away.preheat the iron skillet on medium heat. Divide the mooli paratha dough into 15 portions.roll the portions into balls; Flatten them with the palm of your hand. Toss the Mooli Paratha Dough on Flour and Roll Them Out Into Thin Circles to Approximately 6 inches in diameter.as you roll them out, you can keep tossing the mooli paratha in dry dry to prepare flour to prevnight of rolling the mooli paratha dout with the remaining balls. It is ideal when you can roll out the all portions of Mooli Paratha Dough before you can start cooking them.

recipe_ingredients: 2 Tablespoon Curd (Dahi/Yogurt), 1 Teaspoon Ginger Garlic Paste, 1 Tablespoon Coriander Powder (Dhania), 3/4 Teaspoon Cumin Powder (Jeera) Salt To Taste, 1/2 Taste, 1/2 Turmoon Turmoon Taspoon Taspoon Taspoon Powder) Fenugreek Leaves), 1-1/2 Teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder, 2 Tablespoons Oil, 750 Grams Chicken Cleaned For The Masala Gravy, 4 Tablespoons Oil, 1 Tablespoon Ginger  Finely Chopped, 1 Tablespoon Garlic  Finely Chopped, 1 Cup Homemade Tomato Puree, 2 Onions  Chopped Roasted Till Soft And Ground To Paste Salt  To Taste Red Chilli Powder  Red Chili Powder To Taste, 2 Tablespoon Fresh Cream
recipe_description: To begin making the chicken changezi recipe, pat dry the chicken well after cleaning and prick it with a fork. This helps in getting the marinade deep inside chicken.to marinete the chicken, in a mixing bowl, combine the chicken along with dahi, ginger garland paste, corridar power, corridar power, cumin power, saalt, saalt, plan And marinade for 3-4 hours.Now bake chicken at 180 ° c for 30-40 minutes according to the size of the chicken pieces. Burn a small Piece of Charcoal on the Direct Flame.place your chicken in a dish, Once it is cooked. In the middle place a kateri. Put the blazing charcoal in that katori and pour ghee over it. Cover and let it rest till all the smoke is infused in the chicken. This will give you a smoky flavor to chicken. Now, for the gravy, heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add the ginger garlic and cook for about 30 seconds or till the raw smell disappears.add Tomato pure and masala gravy of onions. Cook until all comes togeether (About 2-3 minutes). Add salt, red chilli powder and baked chicken.Now cook for 5-7 minutes so that gravy is well absorbed by chicken. In the end simply add the cream, mix and serving. Garnish with coriander leaves.serv

recipe_ingredients: 1 Cup Gram Flor (Besan), 1-1/4 Cups Ghee, 1-1/4 Cups Sugar, 1 Cup Water
recipe_description: To begin making homemade mysore pak recipe, prep all the ingredients and keep them handy. Every step comes one after another and hence you need to keep them all ready and handy.also green a small rectangular or a square pan which can give you a decent height to be shaped into a mysore pak. Approximately 10 x 6 inches (OUTER edge) is the size of the pan (inner edge is 9 x 4 inches) in a heavy bottomed preheated over medium heat, Roast the besan/ gram flour for 2 to 3 minuts 2 to 3 minuts Aroma from the gram flour. This will help the raw smell go away from the besan. Ensure you take care not to allow it to brown or burn. Once roasted remove it into a bowl and keep aside.in a heavy bottomed non stick pan; Add the sugar and water and brings the water to a boil ensuring the sugar dissolves complete in the water. Keep stirring so that the sugar disksolves well. Continue to Allow the Sugar Water to Boil - after about 4 minutes you will notes that the sugar water is getting bubbly and sticky as well. It will form a foamy texture. It combines well without any lumps and gets a smooth consistency. The heat needs to be on low from now on till the end of the making of mysore pak.once the gram flour is combined well, add ghee light at a time and reepeat the addition of ghee after 3-4 Minutes of Stirring. The 1-1/2 cups of ghee needs to be added in small batches with a space of 3 minutes of 3 minutes Pak.Once the entrere mass comes together and looks smooth, add the last portion of ghee and continue to stir till the whole masses rises up like froth and you will notic that that mysore pak gets a verie purthers stirring.Once it comes to this consistency, remove from the heat and pour the mysore pak mixture into the green. Bang the trey lightly to allow the mass to spread all over and looks Evenyly Spread.Dip a Flat Spatula with Ghee and Spread over the MySore pak to Level it down and make it smooth from the top. Continue this light pressure and spread until the top of the mysore pak looks smooth.at. Mysore pak pies out of the pan and serve.store the mysore pak in airtight container for 1 week or in the refrigerator for 2 weeks.serv .
